+ MOFO ADVISES AD HOC GROUP OF SENIOR SECURED NOTEHOLDERS AND DIP LENDERS IN VILLAGE ROADSHOW ENTERTAINMENT GROUPS SUCCESSFUL CHAPTER 11 EMERGENCE

May 4, 2026 - MoFo guided an ad hoc group of senior secured noteholders and DIP lenders (the "Ad Hoc Group") through the successful chapter 11 cases of Village Roadshow Entertainment Group USA Inc. and its affiliated debtors ("Village Roadshow" or the "Debtors") before the Honorable Thomas M. Horan in the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware. Village Roadshow emerged from bankruptcy on May 1, 2026.

+ MOFO ADVISES MODIV INDUSTRIAL ON ITS $535 MILLION ALL-STOCK SALE TO GLOBAL NET LEASE

MoFo advised Modiv Industrial (NYSE: MDV) on its $535 Million all-stock sale to Global Net Lease (NYSE: GNL).

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ATTORNEYS RECOGNIZED ON 2026 VIRGINIA SUPER LAWYERS 'RISING STARS LIST

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. The "Rising Stars" designation is awarded to no more than 2.5% of lawyers practicing in each state who demonstrate outstanding professional achievement and peer recognition early in their careers. Candidates are identified through a rigorous selection process that combines peer nominations with independent research and evaluation.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP WHO REPRESENTS LATIN AMERICA'S BIGGEST BANKS 2025: THE FULL LIST

Greenberg Traurig is featured in a Latin Lawyer's LACCA article titled "Who Represents Latin America's Biggest Banks 2025: The Full List."

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

Source: Company Website

Greenberg Traurig is featured in a Latin Lawyer's LACCA article titled "Which Law Firms are Leading in the Financial Sector?"

+ GOODWIN PLC [4 Press Releases]

+ GOODWIN GUIDES PHARMAKON ADVISORS ON $250 MILLION DEBT FACILITY WITH ZENAS BIOPHARMA

The Intellectual Property team advised Pharmakon Advisors on entering into a five-year, up to $250 million senior secured debt facility with Zenas BioPharma, who are developing a bifunctional monoclonal antibody drug product for the treatment of IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD).

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES HUMANS& IN RAISING OVER $500 MILLION AT $4.5 BILLION VALUATION IN SEED ROUND

The Technology team guided humans& in raising over $500 million in a seed financing round, which values the company at approximately $4.5 billion. The round was led by SV Angel and co-founder Georges Harik, while Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, and Alphabet's VC arm, GV, among other venture capital investors, also participated.

Source : Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES EVANTIC CAPITAL IN PARTICIPATION IN SYNTHESIAS $200 MILLION SERIES E

The Technology team advised Evantic Capital in its participation in the $200 million Series E funding round for Synthesia, the AI video platform transforming how organizations create and share knowledge.

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-MNG3-RTGT-G02S-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH $180 MILLION INVESTMENT

Pure Salmon Japan secures a significant preferred note investment of over USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. This investment marks a pivotal milestone for Pure Salmon Japan, as it continues to develop its state-of-the-art land-based salmon farming facility in Japan. The transaction was completed on 1st April 2026 and is expected to support the construction of the facility, which is designed to meet the growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The partnership with Fortress Investment Group highlights the institutional backing that Pure Salmon Japan has garnered for its ambitious project. With a target production of 10,000 tonnes, this facility aims to become the largest land-based salmon farm in Japan. By utilizing advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology, the facility is set to contribute to Japan's food security by reducing reliance on imports and ensuring a stable supply of locally produced salmon.Erol Emed, CEO of Pure Salmon Japan, expressed gratitude for the investment, emphasizing the strategic importance of the project amidst Japan's focus on enhancing domestic seafood production. He stated, 'We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan.'Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding history in Japan, is not only bringing capital but also valuable expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, noted the importance of the facility in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure. This investment aligns with Fortress’s broader strategy to support sustainable initiatives that address pressing global challenges such as food security and environmental sustainability.As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan aims to engage with additional long-term equity partners to further bolster its operational capabilities. The successful closing of this financing not only showcases the increasing institutional investor interest in sustainable aquaculture but also positions Pure Salmon Japan as a key player in the evolving seafood supply chain in Japan.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. This acquisition, made on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by affiliates of Fortress, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.The Tuscan Gardens community features 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus approximately one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, catering to an affluent and growing senior population along Florida’s northeast coast. Under AgeWell’s operational leadership, occupancy and performance metrics have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, expressed enthusiasm about the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast features thoughtfully designed residences, diverse dining options, wellness and fitness amenities, landscaped courtyards, and personalized care programs tailored to individual resident needs. The community benefits from strong demographic trends and limited new senior housing supply locally, as well as proximity to healthcare, retail, and coastal amenities.This acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP FACILITATES $96 MILLION LOAN FOR JASPER MULTIFAMILY COMMUNITY

NEW YORK – Fortress Investment Group today announced that funds managed by its affiliates have closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a newly developed 296-unit Class A multifamily community located in Downtown Los Angeles, California. This financing transaction was completed on the 9th of February, 2026, highlighting Fortress's commitment to supporting high-quality real estate developments.Jasper, owned and developed by Cityview, is strategically positioned near the University of Southern California campus. The property boasts a mix of studio, one-, and two-bedroom residences, featuring high-end finishes and a comprehensive amenity offering, which includes affordable housing units. The proceeds from the loan will be used to refinance existing construction debt and to support the ongoing leasing efforts of this new community.Damian Gancman, Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer at Cityview, remarked on the efficiency and solution-oriented approach of Fortress Investment Group, stating, 'It was a pleasure working with Fortress Investment Group on this transaction. The team delivered a seamless execution from start to finish.' This partnership exemplifies Fortress's ability to facilitate significant transactions in the multifamily housing sector.Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt at Fortress Investment Group, emphasized the importance of the Jasper project, noting, 'Jasper represents the type of asset we are focused on supporting: high-quality, newly built multifamily housing backed by an experienced sponsor with significant equity invested in the project.' This financing reflects a compelling investment opportunity for Fortress's investors.Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group LLC is a leading, highly diversified global investment manager, managing approximately $54 billion in assets as of September 30, 2025. The firm specializes in various investment strategies, including credit and real estate, private equity, and permanent capital investment. This latest transaction underscores Fortress's ongoing commitment to investing in multifamily housing projects across the United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR'S VERTIS INFRA TRUST PLANS IPO FILING FOR UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE

KKR's infrastructure investment trust, Vertis Infrastructure Trust, is poised to file draft papers for an initial public offering (IPO) by the end of May 2026. As per sources, the filing is expected to take place on or before the 31st of May, 2026-05-04 20:29:41.558299, and could enable the trust to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales.The IPO plans come as Vertis operates a portfolio of toll and annuity-generating road projects across India. While the potential deal size is estimated at ₹3,000 crore, insiders note that this figure may be adjusted downward since the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The final amount remains under discussion, reflecting a cautious approach to fundraising amid a competitive InvIT landscape.Investor interest in infrastructure investment trusts (InvITs) has surged, particularly among institutional players and high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) seeking refuge from stock market volatility. The current geopolitical climate, notably influenced by the Iran war, has prompted a shift towards safer investment vehicles that offer stable, long-term yields. Reports suggest that InvIT fundraising could exceed ₹25,000 crore this calendar year, driven by a series of upcoming IPOs.Notably, recent months have seen significant activity in the InvIT sector, with the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) successfully raising over ₹9,000 crore through two separate InvIT offerings in March. This momentum continued into April, when EAAA India Alternatives-sponsored Citius Transnet Investment Trust raised ₹1,105 crore, showcasing strong demand despite challenging market conditions.Vertis Infrastructure Trust has established a robust presence in India's infrastructure landscape, having been primarily developed through strategic acquisitions. KKR's Asian infrastructure fund initiated this platform in 2021 with the acquisition of operational toll and annuity road assets, and further expanded its portfolio in 2024-25 with a significant acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. Qatar Airways Cargo has officially resumed its freighter and belly operations to and from Doha, following a temporary halt in services due to safety concerns. As of 28th April 2026, the airline has published its new schedule, which includes the resumption of freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly, starting from 26th April.In addition to the Dubai route, Qatar Airways Cargo is set to launch freighter flights to Sharjah (SHJ) three times a week, commencing on 1st May. These include QRLIV codes for commodities like fish, mammals, and diplomatic cargo, as well as LHO codes for the transportation of live human organs and blood.Qatar Airways Cargo has ensured that the remaining products remain accessible for booking through standard sales channels. Customers can make reservations via the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, reflecting the airline's commitment to maintaining robust service levels despite previous operational challenges.The resumption of operations by Qatar Airways Cargo is a significant development for logistics and transportation in the region, signaling a recovery in freight services to the UAE. urn:contentItem:6JHD-SVN3-S56J-J28G-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR NEWS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR NEWS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION COMMITS TO WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI DISRUPTION

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a grant-based national initiative to address disruptions from automation, aiming to prevent job displacement and help workers transition into AI-complementary roles. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development, stressing program design, metrics and evaluation. It will measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to understand how automation affects employment. Funding will come through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The choices made during implementation will be critical in determining whether the foundation's funding yields scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects. This initiative is a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate move, intended to catalyze innovative retraining models and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It is also expected to boost demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES $100 MILLION JOB INITIATIVE IN BALTIMORE AT BIG BETS FOR AMERICA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, bringing together more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies for revitalizing the economy and expanding opportunity across communities.Key announcements included a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, aimed at creating about 1.6 million additional jobs across sectors with strong growth potential, including healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, stressed restoring the promise of a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also unveiled a $12 million boost for Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and spur growth in underserved communities. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to improve education and childcare in areas with high childhood poverty. The event also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and highlighted cross-sector partnerships with organizations such as the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is set for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION CALLS FOR UNIFIED DATA PLATFORM AT ONE HEALTH SUMMIT

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for unified data at the One Health Summit. He warned that data remains siloed across humanitarian, nutritional and climate sectors, hindering timely decisions and resource allocation. He said, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." To illustrate potential benefits, Winter highlighted the drought crisis in Somalia, where the foundation is funding a local tech incubator to create a food and nutrition systems dashboard that integrates multiple datasets. The tool is designed to provide timely information to local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes about $2 billion annually to support farmers and pastoralists. He concluded by stressing that empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, combined with local information, could lead to more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC RECOGNIZES FORD FOUNDATION FOR SUSTAINED HUMAN RIGHTS SUPPORT IN NIGERIA

On Apr. 28, 2026, the National Human Rights Commission in Abuja awarded the Ford Foundation an Award of Appreciation for its sustained contributions to human rights in Nigeria. The honor was collected by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa, during a gathering with NHRC senior staff and officials.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu said the Foundation has consistently supported efforts to widen access to justice and to boost the Commission's capacity. He noted that Nigeria faces complex rights challenges and that ongoing collaboration with development partners is essential to turning policy into practical improvements for citizens. The reception underscored the importance of continuing joint work, with gratitude also extended by Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, who highlighted milestones such as the MOVE project and the dashboard system that benefited from the Ford Foundation's early backing.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO TARGET GBV THROUGH SOFT POWER

The Ford Foundation has urged ECOWAS First Ladies to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, describing the First Ladies’ soft power as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. The appeal was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, during a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose Hands Off Our Girls campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how soft power, when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.

+ FORD FOUNDATION LAUNCHES $60 MILLION DRIVE TO BOLSTER DEMOCRATIC VALUES

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to strengthen democratic processes across the United States. The announcement, dated April 14, 2026, commits grants to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting democracy and the rule of law. The funding will support a diverse array of groups across the ideological spectrum, working to boost civic participation and safeguard voting rights. Recipients include Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The foundation notes that this investment builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has provided more than $1 billion in grants to nonpartisan initiatives in the United States. President Heather Gerken framed the grants as strengthening democratic institutions, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHD-CMN3-S7TP-92JW-00000-00Primoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisCLASS ACTIONLAW & LEGAL ISSUESPrimoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses

SAN DIEGO, May 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE: PRIM). The investigation focuses on Primoris’s executive officers and whether investor losses may be recovered under federal securities laws.

What if I purchased Primoris securities?

If you purchased Primoris securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click here to join the investigation.

Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471.

There is no cost or obligation to you.

Background of the investigation

On May 5, 2026, Primoris reported its first quarter 2026 financial results and updated its full-year outlook. Among other things, the Company disclosed revenue of $1.6 billion, down 5.4% compared to the prior-year period, and net income of $17.4 million, compared to $44.2 million in the prior-year period.

Primoris also disclosed adjusted EBITDA of $60.5 million, down 39.1% compared to the first quarter of 2025, and adjusted EPS of $0.59, compared to $0.98 in the prior-year period.

In addition, Primoris disclosed that revenue in its Energy segment decreased by $152.9 million, or 13.8%, compared to the prior-year period. The Company attributed the decrease primarily to lower renewable energy activity due to slower-than-anticipated starts of new projects, release of new work, and financial close associated with certain projects.

Primoris further disclosed that Energy segment operating income decreased by $49.1 million, or 62.2%, compared to the prior-year period, due to decreased revenue and increased costs on certain renewable energy projects. The Company stated that these higher costs were driven in part by project redesign efforts, changes in project sequencing, labor productivity challenges, and unfavorable weather conditions. Energy gross profit as a percentage of revenue declined to 7.6%, compared to 10.7% in the prior-year period.

Primoris also reduced its full-year 2026 outlook, including adjusted EPS guidance of $4.80 to $5.00, compared to prior guidance of $5.80 to $6.00, and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $480 million to $500 million, compared to prior guidance of $560 million to $580 million.

Following this disclosure, Primoris’s stock price declined sharply, damaging investors.

In light of this disclosure, Johnson Fistel is investigating whether Primoris complied with the federal securities laws. If you suffered losses from your investment in Primoris stock, contact Johnson Fistel.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Securities Fraud & Investor Rights

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder-rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits and also assists foreign investors who purchased shares on U.S. exchanges. To learn more, visit www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements

In 2024, Johnson Fistel was ranked among the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by ISS Securities Class Action Services, reflecting the firm’s effectiveness in advocating for investors and recovering approximately $90,725,000 for clients in cases where it served as lead or co-lead counsel.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-TKT3-S1N0-G0C0-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION COMMITS TO WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI DISRUPTION

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a grant-based national initiative to address disruptions from automation, aiming to prevent job displacement and help workers transition into AI-complementary roles. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development, stressing program design, metrics and evaluation. It will measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to understand how automation affects employment. Funding will come through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The choices made during implementation will be critical in determining whether the foundation's funding yields scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects. This initiative is a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate move, intended to catalyze innovative retraining models and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It is also expected to boost demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES $100 MILLION JOB INITIATIVE IN BALTIMORE AT BIG BETS FOR AMERICA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, bringing together more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies for revitalizing the economy and expanding opportunity across communities.Key announcements included a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, aimed at creating about 1.6 million additional jobs across sectors with strong growth potential, including healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, stressed restoring the promise of a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also unveiled a $12 million boost for Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and spur growth in underserved communities. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to improve education and childcare in areas with high childhood poverty. The event also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and highlighted cross-sector partnerships with organizations such as the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is set for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION CALLS FOR UNIFIED DATA PLATFORM AT ONE HEALTH SUMMIT

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for unified data at the One Health Summit. He warned that data remains siloed across humanitarian, nutritional and climate sectors, hindering timely decisions and resource allocation. He said, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." To illustrate potential benefits, Winter highlighted the drought crisis in Somalia, where the foundation is funding a local tech incubator to create a food and nutrition systems dashboard that integrates multiple datasets. The tool is designed to provide timely information to local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes about $2 billion annually to support farmers and pastoralists. He concluded by stressing that empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, combined with local information, could lead to more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC RECOGNIZES FORD FOUNDATION FOR SUSTAINED HUMAN RIGHTS SUPPORT IN NIGERIA

On Apr. 28, 2026, the National Human Rights Commission in Abuja awarded the Ford Foundation an Award of Appreciation for its sustained contributions to human rights in Nigeria. The honor was collected by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa, during a gathering with NHRC senior staff and officials.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu said the Foundation has consistently supported efforts to widen access to justice and to boost the Commission's capacity. He noted that Nigeria faces complex rights challenges and that ongoing collaboration with development partners is essential to turning policy into practical improvements for citizens. The reception underscored the importance of continuing joint work, with gratitude also extended by Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, who highlighted milestones such as the MOVE project and the dashboard system that benefited from the Ford Foundation's early backing.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO TARGET GBV THROUGH SOFT POWER

The Ford Foundation has urged ECOWAS First Ladies to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, describing the First Ladies’ soft power as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. The appeal was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, during a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose Hands Off Our Girls campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how soft power, when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.

+ FORD FOUNDATION LAUNCHES $60 MILLION DRIVE TO BOLSTER DEMOCRATIC VALUES

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to strengthen democratic processes across the United States. The announcement, dated April 14, 2026, commits grants to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting democracy and the rule of law. The funding will support a diverse array of groups across the ideological spectrum, working to boost civic participation and safeguard voting rights. Recipients include Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The foundation notes that this investment builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has provided more than $1 billion in grants to nonpartisan initiatives in the United States. President Heather Gerken framed the grants as strengthening democratic institutions, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-02F3-S18X-D01Y-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) PURSUES DRAFT PAPERS FOR ₹3,000 CRORE INVIT IPO BY END MAY

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file draft papers for an initial public offering by end May 2026, targeting a raise of up to ₹3,000 crore through a mix of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT operates a portfolio of toll and annuity road projects across India, largely assembled by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund since 2021, with growth in 2024-25 through the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The portfolio comprises 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.The final size of the offer remains under discussion, as management weighs limited requirements for fresh primary capital against a competitive fundraising environment for InvITs. Industry observers note that the market for infrastructure InvITs continues to attract institutional investors seeking stable, long-term yields, even as macro headwinds persist.Momentum in the sector is evident, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April. Vertis Infrastructure Trust seeks to participate in this ongoing cycle with a disciplined approach to capital formation

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) COMMITS $10 BILLION TO AI POWER AND DATA CENTER EXPANSION

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) allocates $10 billion to AI power plants and data centers, expanding the firm’s footprint in AI infrastructure.On 3rd May 2026, this move aligns with a broader trend where Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta are investing over $100 billion worldwide in AI infrastructure.The investment seeks long-term power agreements to secure capacity as demand from AI data centers grows.Geopolitical dynamics are noted, with Canada and Germany coordinating to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence, and Google's prospects are cited by market analysts as potentially supporting a stock price near $310.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) TO SHARE STRATEGIC OUTLOOK AT BANK OF AMERICA SECURITIES CONFERENCE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) will have its Chief Financial Officer Robert H. Lewin speak at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on Feb. 10, 2026 at 2:40 PM ET. The presentation will be available via a live webcast in the Investor Center on KKR's site, with a replay obtainable shortly after the event. KKR operates as a global investment firm focused on alternative asset management, capital markets, and insurance solutions, with a portfolio across private equity, credit, and real assets and partnerships that manage hedge funds. Its insurance activities are conducted through Global Atlantic Financial Group, providing retirement, life, and reinsurance products. For more information on KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and its strategies, visit the company’s website. Investor Relations contact details are available there.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS INKS $300 MILLION TERM LOAN WITH OAKTREE TO BACK MOTIVA PLATFORM EXPANSION

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility from Oaktree Capital Management, completed on 3rd May 2026, with refinancing through 2031 and US$35 million reserved for growth.The loan offers fixed-rate, no-amortization terms and a first-priority lien on assets, designed to boost funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues expansion.Analysts view Motiva as a driver of global adoption for its implants and minimally invasive procedures. Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, up about 25-26% year over year. The facility provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without forcing an immediate equity raise, though ongoing cash burn remains a risk.Some watchers project roughly 30% revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could underpin optimism about growth while highlighting the path to profitability.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND WITH BROAD INVESTMENT MOMENTUM

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, signaling continued investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The first close is expected in spring, as noted during an earnings call by chief executive Michael AroughetiThe fund follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalThe firm’s broader capital activity includes Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets including a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, Ares' co-president, said the fundraising aims to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares also raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, supported by commitments to SSG Fund VIINotable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account with a mandate aligned to the SSG series, targeting non performing loans and single-asset distress across Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) FACILITATES GROWTH THROUGH DIRECT LENDING WITH ENCORE FIRE PROTECTION LOENBRO AND ARCHIMED

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors. The firm also highlights notable Q1 activity.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, Ares Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring the breadth of its direct lending platform. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals over $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESTARTS FREIGHTER AND PASSENGER LINKS TO UAE ON EXPANDED NETWORK

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed both freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after safety concerns. The new schedule confirms freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly from 26th April and to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly from 1st May, while daily passenger services to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with freighter reach planned to exceed 50 destinations by 1st May and the overall passenger network expected to top 150 by 16th June.Urgent freight restrictions have been lifted on routes such as Victoria, Australia, with the carrier introducing special handling codes QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, and LHO for live human organs and blood, effective 1st May.Reservations remain accessible via the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, alongside standard sales channels. The resumption is described as a key milestone for regional logistics and supports ongoing expansion.Industry stakeholders are watching the pace of growth, as the UAE freight market benefits from improved connectivity and service levels.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INTRODUCES NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE, AIMING TO SUSTAIN PREMIUM EDGE

Qatar Airways announced the Qsuite Next Generation, with a launch planned later this year on the Airbus A350-1000 after delays with the 777-9, as of 23rd April 2026. The Next Generation builds on the iconic Qsuite introduced in 2017, maintaining sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating while adding a movable 4K OLED screen and stronger privacy options. The initiative reinforces Qatar Airways' premium strategy and its Doha hub, where exclusive check-in, fast-track security, and premium lounges contribute to a seamless travel experience. By investing in product development, the airline seeks to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure customers as competition in luxury cabins intensifies.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS SETS OUT PHASED EMBARGO LIFTS DURING RECOVERY

Qatar Airways is steadily expanding its operations as it rebuilds its network, with passenger routes restored to more than 150 destinations and freighter coverage over 50 locations as of April 20, 2026.The restoration is part of a wider plan to increase capacity amid rising air freight demand. Some shipments remain embargoed, with AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes restricted, and live organs and blood not accepted.QRLIV shipments can be booked under different codes, as long as Doha transit time stays within 24 hours. Other products remain bookable via standard sales channels, and the carrier emphasizes adaptability and a commitment to service as it expands.

value.Title: US set to drop fraud cases against India 's Billionaire Gautam Adani: Bloomberg

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-2MG3-RWWN-G4V7-00000-00US set to drop fraud cases against India's Billionaire Gautam Adani: Bloomberg2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisUS set to drop fraud cases against India's Billionaire Gautam Adani: Bloombergarabtimes

NEW DELHI, May 14: US authorities are reportedly preparing to drop fraud-related cases against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani. This move could provide significant legal relief for the Adani Group after months of regulatory scrutiny in the United States.

According to a Bloomberg report citing sources familiar with the matter, the US Department of Justice may announce as early as this week that it is ending its criminal investigation involving Adani. The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is also reportedly moving toward resolving a related civil fraud case, potentially bringing the long-running legal dispute to a close.

The development comes despite ongoing efforts by Gautam Adani and his nephew Sagar Adani to dismiss the SEC lawsuit filed against them in federal court in New York. In court filings submitted in April, the Adanis denied any wrongdoing and argued that investors did not suffer financial losses linked to the 2021 bond offering at the center of the SEC’s allegations.

The SEC case centers on a $750 million bond issuance by Adani Green Energy in 2021. Lawyers representing the Adanis argued that the SEC lacks jurisdiction because both men are based in India, the alleged conduct took place entirely within India, and the securities involved were not listed on any US stock exchange.

According to the defense, the bonds were issued outside the United States under Rule 144A and Regulation S exemptions. They were initially sold to non-US underwriters before being partially resold to qualified institutional buyers, leading the defense to characterize the SEC’s claims as an improper attempt to apply US securities law beyond its borders.

The Adanis’ legal team also argued that the SEC failed to demonstrate investor harm, noting that the bonds matured and were fully repaid with interest in 2024. The filings further stated that regulators had not shown direct involvement by Gautam Adani in approving the bond issuance, participating in key meetings, or making statements specifically targeting US investors.

Statements referenced by the SEC concerning environmental, social and governance (ESG) commitments, anti-corruption measures and corporate governance practices were described by the defense as general corporate messaging rather than evidence of fraud.

If the Justice Department formally withdraws the criminal case and the SEC settles, it would remove a major legal uncertainty hanging over the Adani Group. The conglomerate has continued to expand across sectors, including ports, airports, renewable energy, and infrastructure, despite increased scrutiny from US regulators. Analysts say such a move could also strengthen the group’s ability to re-enter international capital markets.

value.Title: The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it? The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?
value.Overview: The Community News (Aledo, Texas), WEB EXTRAS, 1073words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-19T3-RVDS-P4F5-00000-00The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:47ZLexisNexisWEB EXTRASThe Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?Juanpablo RamirezFranco for Grist

The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?

There are no wind turbines on the Great Lakes and it isn't for lack of wind.

This titanic network of interconnected freshwater lakes with a surface area larger than New England, New York, and New Jersey combined is theoretically ideal for wind farms. Winds sweeping across the lakes are stronger, more consistent, and less turbulent than those over land. The National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimated that the Great Lakes states have enough offshore wind potential to generate more than three times their combined annual electricity consumption.

Plus, unlike proposed wind farms along the East Coast, where the federal government controls the seabed, Great Lakes states have jurisdiction over their lakebeds, Grist reports. While projects would still face federal oversight, some proponents see wind development in the Great Lakes as a potential workaround to President Trump's attacks on the industry.

"If it's done correctly and we're able to harness even a fraction of that, we could offset a lot of electricity demand," said Melissa Scanlan, director of the Center for Water Policy at the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee.

Still, the vast inland lakes remain untapped largely due to a lack of streamlined permitting processes at the state level and economic hurdles. Offshore wind is expensive, and unlike the East Coast, where a mature wind industry has built specialized ships and ports to build turbines in the sea, the Great Lakes states lack the infrastructure. Ultimately, renewable energy experts say the region doesn't have the legal framework or technical capacity to support such massive projects.

So far, offshore wind power which involves installing turbines in large bodies of water, typically the ocean in the United States, has been concentrated along the Eastern seaboard. Projects are still very rare: Just three are currently operating, with several more under construction, all along the East Coast.

More than a decade ago, President Barack Obama's push for renewable energy buoyed a short-lived boom in offshore wind projects particularly in the Great Lakes. The New York Power Authority and cities such as Toronto explored boosting their energy supply with wind power from Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. However, the initial boost only propelled one project forward: Ohio's Icebreaker Wind in Lake Erie. After clashing with Ohio regulators over whether turbines could run through the night and battling a court case filed by local residents over impacts to bats and migratory birds, the developers eventually ran out of money and called the project off in 2023.

Building renewable energy offshore in federal waters has now become harder than ever. On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed a memorandum shutting down all offshore wind permitting, effectively suspending five large-scale East Coast projects. Although courts later overturned those cancellations, the uncertainty around offshore wind development under the Trump administration has persisted. And since any project on the Great Lakes would still need federal permits and reviews under the Clean Air Act and the Endangered Species Act, among others, wind developers have largely backed off their plans.

"That told us that this was not the time to reintroduce floating offshore wind in Illinois," said Jim Lanard, co-founder of Magellan Wind, an offshore wind developer focused on the Great Lakes. "Rather, we needed to face the existential threat to the industry."

Lanard said the regulatory uncertainty means it's a bad time for business. His company, which in the past has submitted comments to the Illinois power regulators related to the deployment of offshore wind on Lake Michigan, has temporarily paused pursuing work on the Great Lakes.

"Governors and legislatures are so focused on trying to figure out how to run their governments with the reduced support coming from WashingtonD.C.," Lanard said. "It's not time for them to add additional burdens onto their staff, who don't know how they're going to balance their budgets."

Though Illinois' jurisdiction over part of Lake Michigan presents some opportunities to work around Trump, Lanard said developers are not going to come to Illinois until there is a federal policy to protect the industry from whiplash elections. Moreover, Illinois and the other Great Lakes states have yet to establish a substantial legal framework to make local offshore wind farms a reality. Given these challenges, he estimated it will be some time before there are any wind farms on the Great Lakes.

"I'm looking at five to seven years from 2029," Lanard said.

For years, state Representative Marcus Evans Jr. has championed a plan to make Illinois' shores home to the first offshore wind farm in the Great Lakes. The legislation, known as the Illinois Rust Belt to Green Belt Pilot Program Act, was designed to direct state planners to solicit proposals for a new utility-scale offshore wind farm and begin purchasing offshore wind power. First introduced by Evans in 2022, the measure has long failed to pick up enough momentum to break out of the statehouse. In 2025, Illinois Senator Robert Peters refiled the bill, but it didn't receive a committee assignment. Evans said he decided against refiling the bill this year.

"I didn't have the support," said Evans, a South Side Chicago Democrat. "I used my energy this year to focus on some other items, but it's still something that I think is important," he added.

Since the bill would require a localized industry to build, transport, and upkeep the turbines, Evans saw the push as a huge boon for the region's clean energy workforce one that the state of Illinois has long been trying to prop up.

Illinois passed the Climate and Equitable Jobs Act in 2021, which committed the state to investing $80 million annually to build out workforce training hubs focused on clean energy-related jobs. The same bill also put the state on track to transition its energy sector to 100 percent clean energy by 2050. Evans had hoped his initiative could help achieve both goals at once.

He is still optimistic that Lake Michigan will be next.

"I will file [a bill] in the future," he said.

value.Title: The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?
