urn:contentItem:6JHB-19T3-RVM1-00G7-00000-00The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:47ZLexisNexisWEB EXTRASThe Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?Juanpablo RamirezFranco for Grist

The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?

There are no wind turbines on the Great Lakes and it isn't for lack of wind.

This titanic network of interconnected freshwater lakes with a surface area larger than New England, New York, and New Jersey combined is theoretically ideal for wind farms. Winds sweeping across the lakes are stronger, more consistent, and less turbulent than those over land. The National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimated that the Great Lakes states have enough offshore wind potential to generate more than three times their combined annual electricity consumption.

Plus, unlike proposed wind farms along the East Coast, where the federal government controls the seabed, Great Lakes states have jurisdiction over their lakebeds, Grist reports. While projects would still face federal oversight, some proponents see wind development in the Great Lakes as a potential workaround to President Trump's attacks on the industry.

"If it's done correctly and we're able to harness even a fraction of that, we could offset a lot of electricity demand," said Melissa Scanlan, director of the Center for Water Policy at the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee.

Still, the vast inland lakes remain untapped largely due to a lack of streamlined permitting processes at the state level and economic hurdles. Offshore wind is expensive, and unlike the East Coast, where a mature wind industry has built specialized ships and ports to build turbines in the sea, the Great Lakes states lack the infrastructure. Ultimately, renewable energy experts say the region doesn't have the legal framework or technical capacity to support such massive projects.

So far, offshore wind power which involves installing turbines in large bodies of water, typically the ocean in the United States, has been concentrated along the Eastern seaboard. Projects are still very rare: Just three are currently operating, with several more under construction, all along the East Coast.

More than a decade ago, President Barack Obama's push for renewable energy buoyed a short-lived boom in offshore wind projects particularly in the Great Lakes. The New York Power Authority and cities such as Toronto explored boosting their energy supply with wind power from Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. However, the initial boost only propelled one project forward: Ohio's Icebreaker Wind in Lake Erie. After clashing with Ohio regulators over whether turbines could run through the night and battling a court case filed by local residents over impacts to bats and migratory birds, the developers eventually ran out of money and called the project off in 2023.

Building renewable energy offshore in federal waters has now become harder than ever. On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed a memorandum shutting down all offshore wind permitting, effectively suspending five large-scale East Coast projects. Although courts later overturned those cancellations, the uncertainty around offshore wind development under the Trump administration has persisted. And since any project on the Great Lakes would still need federal permits and reviews under the Clean Air Act and the Endangered Species Act, among others, wind developers have largely backed off their plans.

"That told us that this was not the time to reintroduce floating offshore wind in Illinois," said Jim Lanard, co-founder of Magellan Wind, an offshore wind developer focused on the Great Lakes. "Rather, we needed to face the existential threat to the industry."

Lanard said the regulatory uncertainty means it's a bad time for business. His company, which in the past has submitted comments to the Illinois power regulators related to the deployment of offshore wind on Lake Michigan, has temporarily paused pursuing work on the Great Lakes.

"Governors and legislatures are so focused on trying to figure out how to run their governments with the reduced support coming from WashingtonD.C.," Lanard said. "It's not time for them to add additional burdens onto their staff, who don't know how they're going to balance their budgets."

Though Illinois' jurisdiction over part of Lake Michigan presents some opportunities to work around Trump, Lanard said developers are not going to come to Illinois until there is a federal policy to protect the industry from whiplash elections. Moreover, Illinois and the other Great Lakes states have yet to establish a substantial legal framework to make local offshore wind farms a reality. Given these challenges, he estimated it will be some time before there are any wind farms on the Great Lakes.

"I'm looking at five to seven years from 2029," Lanard said.

For years, state Representative Marcus Evans Jr. has championed a plan to make Illinois' shores home to the first offshore wind farm in the Great Lakes. The legislation, known as the Illinois Rust Belt to Green Belt Pilot Program Act, was designed to direct state planners to solicit proposals for a new utility-scale offshore wind farm and begin purchasing offshore wind power. First introduced by Evans in 2022, the measure has long failed to pick up enough momentum to break out of the statehouse. In 2025, Illinois Senator Robert Peters refiled the bill, but it didn't receive a committee assignment. Evans said he decided against refiling the bill this year.

"I didn't have the support," said Evans, a South Side Chicago Democrat. "I used my energy this year to focus on some other items, but it's still something that I think is important," he added.

Since the bill would require a localized industry to build, transport, and upkeep the turbines, Evans saw the push as a huge boon for the region's clean energy workforce one that the state of Illinois has long been trying to prop up.

Illinois passed the Climate and Equitable Jobs Act in 2021, which committed the state to investing $80 million annually to build out workforce training hubs focused on clean energy-related jobs. The same bill also put the state on track to transition its energy sector to 100 percent clean energy by 2050. Evans had hoped his initiative could help achieve both goals at once.

He is still optimistic that Lake Michigan will be next.

"I will file [a bill] in the future," he said.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-59C3-S8C5-135N-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

+ COOLEY LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

+ COOLEY LLP FACES A LEADERSHIP CHALLENGE AS LITIGATION PRACTICE EXPANDS

Cooley LLP's litigation practice is advancing into the spotlight as a finalist for The American Lawyer's General Litigation Department of the Year. The firm is reputed for excellence and is now negotiating what its partners describe as a 'test of leadership.' Led by Ian Shapiro, the team is addressing pivotal challenges in a rapidly evolving legal environment.A recent interview with Shapiro, joined by Michael Attanasio and Kathleen Hartnett, shed light on the firm’s strategic decision-making. They highlighted Cooley’s work representing Jenner & Block in its high-profile bid against the administration, underscoring the commitment to meaningful advocacy. The interview was published roughly 10 hours ago, offering a timely snapshot of the firm’s operations.In parallel with Jenner & Block matters, Cooley has taken on a range of pro bono matters that emphasize public service and social justice. This dedication has fueled growth for the firm over the last year. As the litigation group expands, Cooley intends to deploy its expertise to address complex legal challenges while upholding its core values.The expansion of Cooley’s litigation practice reflects a larger industry shift toward specialized services. This strategic move enhances Cooley's competitive standing and positions it for future opportunities. With the fiscal year ending soon, observers will monitor Cooley's next moves.During this unfolding 'test of leadership,' the legal community and investors will look to see how Cooley navigates these challenges. The firm’s proactive approach and commitment to excellence suggest it could emerge stronger in a crowded market.

+ REED SMITH ESTABLISHES FIRST BOSTON MANAGING PARTNER POSITION TO DEEPEN MARKET PRESENCE

ReedSmith LLP announced the appointment of its first Managing Partner in Boston, a move designed to intensify the firm's regional focus. The appointment is effective from 23rd April 2026. ReedSmith LLP has added a fund finance veteran from McDermott Will & Emery to its Miami office, cementing its footing in the fund finance sector. + RAY THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES $125 MILLION SERIES B TO ACCELERATE RTX-015 AND RTX-021

Ray Therapeutics announced it closed a $125 million Series B that was upsized and oversubscribed, with Janus Henderson Investors leading and a group of new investors including Adage Capital Management, Franklin Templeton, Invus, and Marshall Wace.Existing backers such as 4BIO Capital, Deerfield Management, MRL Ventures Fund, Novo Holdings A/S, Norwest, and Platanus participated, with a leading law firm providing advisory support.The funds are earmarked to support late-stage development for RTx-015 targeting Retinitis Pigmentosa and RTx-021 for Stargardt Disease and Geographic Atrophy, as RayTx moves toward commercial readiness.The advisory team members named were Dan Koeppen, Kassandra Castillo, and Nimit Dhir, signaling the value placed on strategic partnerships.

+ PRO BONO LEGAL TEAM BACKS P-REC AGGREGATION FACILITY ADVANCING RURAL ELECTRIFICATION

On March 25, 2026, the African Development Bank Group and Nordic Development Fund unveiled an $11.3 million plan to pilot the P-REC Aggregation Facility, the first use of RECs as direct funding for a mini-grid portfolio in 14 African countries.Co-managed by Camco Clean Energy and Energy Peace Partners (EPP), the facility aims to sign long-term purchase agreements with mini-grid developers in Burundi, Ethiopia, and Nigeria, to deliver electricity access to about 856,000 people.A pro bono legal team, including Scott Zimmermann, Nadia Senter, and Ethan Miller, supported EPP through the transaction, illustrating the firm's commitment to innovative climate finance.By aggregating RECs, the initiative seeks a sustainable financial model supporting local developers and contributing to climate resilience and energy equity; the AFDB announcement is available.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

+ REGULATOR ALIGNS WITH TOP FIRMS AS CFTC CREATES INNOVATION TASK FORCE

The CFTC has formed an Innovation Task Force with Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to strengthen the regulatory framework for derivatives markets as technology reshapes the sector.The 11th April 2026 announcement notes the collaboration aims to incorporate insights from both firms to reflect evolving market realities, including digital assets and new trading technologies.Sidley Austin will provide regulatory guidance while Latham & Watkins contributes practical policy experience, helping the commission evaluate how tools like blockchain and AI intersect with oversight objectives.The task force is part of a wider effort to balance prudent supervision with fostering innovation, as the U.S. seeks to maintain leadership in global financial markets.

+ CHAMBERS SHORTLISTS GREENBERG TRAURIG AS DUAL ENVIRONMENT AND REAL ESTATE LAW FIRM OF THE YEAR CONTENDER

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Environmental and Real Estate practices have been shortlisted by Chambers for the 2026 Environment Law Firm of the Year and Real Estate Law Firm of the Year awards, Chambers announced on 23rd April 2026. The winners will be revealed on 11th June 2026 at The Plaza Hotel in New York.The firm is among six finalists in each category. The Chambers awards are highly regarded, recognizing outstanding legal work, strategic growth, and client service, based on research by more than 250 analysts.With about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations worldwide, Greenberg Traurig's cross-border capabilities support innovative and strategic legal services. The firm was named a 2025 BTI Best of the Best Recommended Law Firm for trust and relationship management.

+ LONDON REAL ESTATE FINANCE PRACTICE HITS £5 BILLION MILESTONE AT GREENBERG TRAURIG

Greenberg Traurig's London Real Estate Finance team surpassed £5 billion in transaction value across asset classes and financing structures as of 23 April 2026. Promotions within the team include William Lo to shareholder and Priyanka Verma to senior associate, increasing the group to four shareholders and nine attorneys. The team is shortlisted for Real Estate Team of the Year at The Lawyer Awards 2026 for advising on the £1.1 billion take-private of The PRS REIT plc, and is also in contention for Lawyer of the Year at the PROPS Awards on 14 May 2026. Duncan Hubbard, chair of the London Real Estate Finance team, highlighted the market's opportunities and the trust clients place in the team, while Greenberg Traurig's global platform of about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations supports its growth.

+ MEXICO CITY UNIT OF GREENBERG TRAURIG EARNS COMPETITION AND ANTITRUST FIRM OF THE YEAR

Apr. 23, 2026: Greenberg Traurig’s Mexico City office has earned the Competition and Antitrust Firm of the Year award at the Legal 500 Mexico Awards 2026, announced in Mexico City. The recognition stems from Legal 500’s independent research for its annual directory. Jose Raz Guzman, co-chair of the Latin America Practice and co-managing shareholder in Mexico City, called the award a testament to the firm’s leadership and technical depth. The Antitrust Litigation & Competition Regulation Practice is led by Miguel Flores Bernes, Victor Manuel Frias and Rocio Olea Salgado, with support from associates Valery Garcia Zavala, Ana Ramirez, Natalia Mejia and Martin Lopez, and is known for handling mergers, acquisitions and joint ventures and for managing cross-border merger filings. Greenberg Traurig also helps clients with compliance, internal investigations and competition authority proceedings, while the Mexico City office continues to grow as a cornerstone of the Latin America Practice.

Orrick has added Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. + FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.

+ ORRICK DRIVES CLO GROWTH IN LONDON WITH CADWALDER HIRES

Orrick has added two structured finance lawyers from Cadwalader to its London office, Suzanne Bell and William Bibby; Bell spent more than 13 years at Cadwalader, and Bibby will be elevated to partner after arriving from Linklaters a year ago, bringing the total Cadwalader hires to 42 since last year’s drive. The expansion that began in September 2025 formed a transatlantic CLO and asset-backed lending team across London and the US, totalling 37 lawyers, including 10 partners. In Q1 2026, Orrick ranked second among arranger counsel for CLO deals globally, with its London team advising on four European private credit CLOs. Leah Sanzari said the group’s performance has exceeded expectations as the firm pursues innovative financial products.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-1D73-S5JS-C062-00000-00The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:47ZLexisNexisWEB EXTRASThe Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?Juanpablo RamirezFranco for Grist

The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?

There are no wind turbines on the Great Lakes and it isn't for lack of wind.

This titanic network of interconnected freshwater lakes with a surface area larger than New England, New York, and New Jersey combined is theoretically ideal for wind farms. Winds sweeping across the lakes are stronger, more consistent, and less turbulent than those over land. The National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimated that the Great Lakes states have enough offshore wind potential to generate more than three times their combined annual electricity consumption.

Plus, unlike proposed wind farms along the East Coast, where the federal government controls the seabed, Great Lakes states have jurisdiction over their lakebeds, Grist reports. While projects would still face federal oversight, some proponents see wind development in the Great Lakes as a potential workaround to President Trump's attacks on the industry.

"If it's done correctly and we're able to harness even a fraction of that, we could offset a lot of electricity demand," said Melissa Scanlan, director of the Center for Water Policy at the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee.

Still, the vast inland lakes remain untapped largely due to a lack of streamlined permitting processes at the state level and economic hurdles. Offshore wind is expensive, and unlike the East Coast, where a mature wind industry has built specialized ships and ports to build turbines in the sea, the Great Lakes states lack the infrastructure. Ultimately, renewable energy experts say the region doesn't have the legal framework or technical capacity to support such massive projects.

So far, offshore wind power which involves installing turbines in large bodies of water, typically the ocean in the United States, has been concentrated along the Eastern seaboard. Projects are still very rare: Just three are currently operating, with several more under construction, all along the East Coast.

More than a decade ago, President Barack Obama's push for renewable energy buoyed a short-lived boom in offshore wind projects particularly in the Great Lakes. The New York Power Authority and cities such as Toronto explored boosting their energy supply with wind power from Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. However, the initial boost only propelled one project forward: Ohio's Icebreaker Wind in Lake Erie. After clashing with Ohio regulators over whether turbines could run through the night and battling a court case filed by local residents over impacts to bats and migratory birds, the developers eventually ran out of money and called the project off in 2023.

Building renewable energy offshore in federal waters has now become harder than ever. On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed a memorandum shutting down all offshore wind permitting, effectively suspending five large-scale East Coast projects. Although courts later overturned those cancellations, the uncertainty around offshore wind development under the Trump administration has persisted. And since any project on the Great Lakes would still need federal permits and reviews under the Clean Air Act and the Endangered Species Act, among others, wind developers have largely backed off their plans.

"That told us that this was not the time to reintroduce floating offshore wind in Illinois," said Jim Lanard, co-founder of Magellan Wind, an offshore wind developer focused on the Great Lakes. "Rather, we needed to face the existential threat to the industry."

Lanard said the regulatory uncertainty means it's a bad time for business. His company, which in the past has submitted comments to the Illinois power regulators related to the deployment of offshore wind on Lake Michigan, has temporarily paused pursuing work on the Great Lakes.

"Governors and legislatures are so focused on trying to figure out how to run their governments with the reduced support coming from WashingtonD.C.," Lanard said. "It's not time for them to add additional burdens onto their staff, who don't know how they're going to balance their budgets."

Though Illinois' jurisdiction over part of Lake Michigan presents some opportunities to work around Trump, Lanard said developers are not going to come to Illinois until there is a federal policy to protect the industry from whiplash elections. Moreover, Illinois and the other Great Lakes states have yet to establish a substantial legal framework to make local offshore wind farms a reality. Given these challenges, he estimated it will be some time before there are any wind farms on the Great Lakes.

"I'm looking at five to seven years from 2029," Lanard said.

For years, state Representative Marcus Evans Jr. has championed a plan to make Illinois' shores home to the first offshore wind farm in the Great Lakes. The legislation, known as the Illinois Rust Belt to Green Belt Pilot Program Act, was designed to direct state planners to solicit proposals for a new utility-scale offshore wind farm and begin purchasing offshore wind power. First introduced by Evans in 2022, the measure has long failed to pick up enough momentum to break out of the statehouse. In 2025, Illinois Senator Robert Peters refiled the bill, but it didn't receive a committee assignment. Evans said he decided against refiling the bill this year.

"I didn't have the support," said Evans, a South Side Chicago Democrat. "I used my energy this year to focus on some other items, but it's still something that I think is important," he added.

Since the bill would require a localized industry to build, transport, and upkeep the turbines, Evans saw the push as a huge boon for the region's clean energy workforce one that the state of Illinois has long been trying to prop up.

Illinois passed the Climate and Equitable Jobs Act in 2021, which committed the state to investing $80 million annually to build out workforce training hubs focused on clean energy-related jobs. The same bill also put the state on track to transition its energy sector to 100 percent clean energy by 2050. Evans had hoped his initiative could help achieve both goals at once.

He is still optimistic that Lake Michigan will be next.

"I will file [a bill] in the future," he said.

This story is a partnership between Grist and WBEZ, a public radio station serving the Chicago metropolitan region.

This story was produced by Grist and reviewed and distributed by Stacker.

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH $180 MILLION INVESTMENT

Pure Salmon Japan secures a significant preferred note investment of over USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. This investment marks a pivotal milestone for Pure Salmon Japan, as it continues to develop its state-of-the-art land-based salmon farming facility in Japan. The transaction was completed on 1st April 2026 and is expected to support the construction of the facility, which is designed to meet the growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The partnership with Fortress Investment Group highlights the institutional backing that Pure Salmon Japan has garnered for its ambitious project. With a target production of 10,000 tonnes, this facility aims to become the largest land-based salmon farm in Japan. By utilizing advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology, the facility is set to contribute to Japan's food security by reducing reliance on imports and ensuring a stable supply of locally produced salmon.Erol Emed, CEO of Pure Salmon Japan, expressed gratitude for the investment, emphasizing the strategic importance of the project amidst Japan's focus on enhancing domestic seafood production. He stated, 'We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan.'Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding history in Japan, is not only bringing capital but also valuable expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, noted the importance of the facility in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure. This investment aligns with Fortress’s broader strategy to support sustainable initiatives that address pressing global challenges such as food security and environmental sustainability.As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan aims to engage with additional long-term equity partners to further bolster its operational capabilities. The successful closing of this financing not only showcases the increasing institutional investor interest in sustainable aquaculture but also positions Pure Salmon Japan as a key player in the evolving seafood supply chain in Japan.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. This acquisition, made on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by affiliates of Fortress, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.The Tuscan Gardens community features 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus approximately one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, catering to an affluent and growing senior population along Florida’s northeast coast. Under AgeWell’s operational leadership, occupancy and performance metrics have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, expressed enthusiasm about the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast features thoughtfully designed residences, diverse dining options, wellness and fitness amenities, landscaped courtyards, and personalized care programs tailored to individual resident needs. The community benefits from strong demographic trends and limited new senior housing supply locally, as well as proximity to healthcare, retail, and coastal amenities.This acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP FACILITATES $96 MILLION LOAN FOR JASPER MULTIFAMILY COMMUNITY

NEW YORK – Fortress Investment Group today announced that funds managed by its affiliates have closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a newly developed 296-unit Class A multifamily community located in Downtown Los Angeles, California. This financing transaction was completed on the 9th of February, 2026, highlighting Fortress's commitment to supporting high-quality real estate developments.Jasper, owned and developed by Cityview, is strategically positioned near the University of Southern California campus. The property boasts a mix of studio, one-, and two-bedroom residences, featuring high-end finishes and a comprehensive amenity offering, which includes affordable housing units. The proceeds from the loan will be used to refinance existing construction debt and to support the ongoing leasing efforts of this new community.Damian Gancman, Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer at Cityview, remarked on the efficiency and solution-oriented approach of Fortress Investment Group, stating, 'It was a pleasure working with Fortress Investment Group on this transaction. The team delivered a seamless execution from start to finish.' This partnership exemplifies Fortress's ability to facilitate significant transactions in the multifamily housing sector.Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt at Fortress Investment Group, emphasized the importance of the Jasper project, noting, 'Jasper represents the type of asset we are focused on supporting: high-quality, newly built multifamily housing backed by an experienced sponsor with significant equity invested in the project.' This financing reflects a compelling investment opportunity for Fortress's investors.Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group LLC is a leading, highly diversified global investment manager, managing approximately $54 billion in assets as of September 30, 2025. The firm specializes in various investment strategies, including credit and real estate, private equity, and permanent capital investment. This latest transaction underscores Fortress's ongoing commitment to investing in multifamily housing projects across the United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR PARTNERS WITH UAE'S ALT RRA TO PROPEL CLIMATE INVESTMENTS

KKR & Co. Inc. has secured a significant commitment from the UAE's $30 billion blended finance vehicle, ALT RRA, to enhance its global climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on 22nd April 2026, highlighting KKR's ongoing efforts to mobilize capital for sustainable energy projects. Though the financial terms of the commitment were not disclosed, the partnership signifies a robust alignment with ALT RRA's mission to attract private investments into the energy transition sector.ALT RRA was launched at COP28 with a substantial commitment from the UAE, aiming to de-risk investments in climate-related initiatives. The vehicle has already formed partnerships with several major asset managers, including BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management, and TPG. With KKR's inclusion, ALT RRA now boasts a formidable array of investment expertise, targeting an ambitious $250 billion in mobilized climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed towards developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, focuses on a diverse range of sectors including renewables, energy efficiency, and sustainable fuels. The firm brings over 15 years of infrastructure investment experience, managing more than $100 billion in assets. Since 2011, KKR has invested over $44 billion specifically in climate and environmental sustainability projects, underscoring its commitment to addressing global climate challenges.The strategy has already made notable investments, including in UK-based transport electrification firm Zenob , German energy services provider EGC, and US-based solar and storage developer Avantus. Additionally, KKR has invested in IGNIS P2X, an industrial decarbonization platform. This portfolio reflects KKR's strategic approach to enhancing energy security and affordability while promoting decarbonization across various sectors.Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue emphasized the partnership's potential, stating, "We see a significant opportunity to invest in solutions that enhance energy security, improve affordability, and ensure reliability, while supporting decarbonization across the real economy." They believe that combining ALT RRA’s global perspective with KKR’s extensive experience in climate and infrastructure will enable them to scale proven technologies and build more resilient and efficient energy systems for the long term.

+ ACCEL-KKR INVESTS IN ECR SPIN-OUT STARITAS AMID GROWTH POTENTIAL

Accel-KKR has announced its backing of Staritas, a spin-out from ECR, as the company seeks to capitalize on new market opportunities. This strategic investment reflects Accel-KKR's commitment to enhancing its portfolio with innovative technology firms. The announcement was made on 22nd April 2026, marking a significant step for both Accel-KKR and Staritas in their respective growth trajectories.Staritas, which focuses on delivering cutting-edge solutions in the digital space, aims to leverage its independence from ECR to accelerate product development and market penetration. The support from KKR & Co. Inc. is expected to provide the necessary resources and expertise to help Staritas navigate the evolving landscape of digital technology. This partnership is seen as a pivotal moment for Staritas as it transitions to a standalone entity.The collaboration between Accel-KKR and Staritas underscores the growing interest in technology-driven companies, particularly those that can adapt rapidly to changing consumer demands. Investors are keenly observing how this spin-out will perform in a competitive market, especially given the backing of a well-regarded firm like KKR & Co. Inc. This relationship may also pave the way for future investments and collaborations within the tech sector.As part of its growth strategy, Staritas plans to expand its offerings and enhance its service capabilities. The management team is optimistic that the investment from Accel-KKR will not only bolster its operational framework but also provide the necessary capital to innovate and scale effectively. This move aligns with broader trends in the technology industry, where agility and innovation are paramount.In conclusion, the backing of Staritas by KKR & Co. Inc. represents a significant milestone for both the spin-out and Accel-KKR. As they embark on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring how this partnership unfolds and its impact on the market. The investment is poised to attract further attention from investors looking for opportunities in the tech space, particularly in companies that exhibit strong growth potential.

+ KKR NAMES JOEL TRAUT AS GLOBAL HEAD OF CRE LENDING

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced the promotion of Joel Traut to the position of global head of direct lending within its real estate division. This strategic move reflects the firm’s commitment to expanding its presence in the complex global real estate credit market. Traut, who has been instrumental in scaling KKR Real Estate's credit business from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by the end of 2025, will now oversee growth across a broader geographic scope. The announcement comes on the 20th of April, 2026.Traut's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career at KKR, where he has served as head of originations since joining the firm. His new role is seen as a response to the increasing scale and complexity of KKR's global real estate credit operations, which currently sees the firm investing nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe. The firm is poised to explore new opportunities in the coming years, leveraging Traut's extensive experience in the industry.Paul Fine, who has also been with KKR since 2015, has been promoted to succeed Traut as head of originations. Fine, previously a managing director at KKR, aims to build upon the strong foundation laid by Traut, with a focus on enhancing client relationships and expanding the firm's pool of capital. Under Fine's leadership, KKR's capital pools have grown from two in 2017 to eight today, including significant commitments such as the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.The new structure of KKR Real Estate's credit business reflects a significant evolution since Traut and Fine joined the firm, with the team expanding from just 10 employees in 2015 to a robust 130 today. Traut's experience, including his previous tenure at GE Capital Real Estate, positions him well to lead this dynamic team in enhancing KKR's European real estate credit business, which will be supported by Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.As KKR continues to navigate the complexities of the real estate credit landscape, the leadership transitions signal a strategic focus on growth and innovation. Investors and market participants will be keenly watching how these changes impact KKR’s operations and its ability to seize new opportunities in the global real estate sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP STRENGTHENS LEADERSHIP WITH THREE NEW CO-PRESIDENTS

Carlyle Group L.P. has announced the elevation of three senior executives to the role of co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz. This strategic move, revealed on 22nd April 2026, aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations at the private equity firm as it seeks to refine its business strategy in a challenging economic environment.The appointments of these co-presidents signal a significant shift in Carlyle Group's leadership structure, enhancing the company's focus on execution across its various business lines. With rising interest rates and a slowdown in deal flow, the firm is adapting to new market realities that require a more cohesive and agile leadership approach. By consolidating power within a closer circle around Schwartz, Carlyle Group is positioning itself to navigate these challenges effectively.Harvey Schwartz, who took the helm during a transitional leadership period, has been vocal about the need for clearer accountability and expedited decision-making processes. The introduction of three co-presidents reflects a strategy that has been successfully employed by other major firms, allowing for shared oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management. This approach is expected to enhance the firm's responsiveness to market dynamics and improve overall performance.As Carlyle Group continues to adapt to the evolving landscape of private equity, the new leadership structure is expected to play a crucial role in guiding the firm through the complexities of the current economic climate. Investors will be keenly observing how these changes impact Carlyle Group's investment strategies and financial performance in the coming quarters.In conclusion, Carlyle Group's decision to elevate three executives to co-presidents underlines a commitment to a more unified leadership approach, aimed at fostering efficiency and effectiveness. As the firm navigates through an era marked by economic uncertainty, the focus will be on how well this new leadership structure translates into tangible results for investors and stakeholders alike.

+ CARLYLE GROUP (CG) EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH $2.8B MAI CAPITAL STAKE

The Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) is poised to strengthen its foothold in the wealth management sector with its recent agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, a deal that values the firm at over $2.8 billion. On 31st March 2026, Carlyle confirmed it would be taking majority ownership from existing investors Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners, and Oak Hill Capital, marking a significant development since its initial investment in MAI back in 2021.Under the terms of the acquisition, MAI employees will maintain a meaningful minority equity stake, ensuring that the firm continues to operate independently under its established leadership, spearheaded by Chairman and CEO Rick Buoncore. This strategic investment is aimed at fortifying MAI's capital base while facilitating the growth of its comprehensive services, which encompass financial planning, investment management, and family office capabilities.As of January 1, MAI and its affiliates boast management or advisory responsibilities over $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States. The acquisition is designed to provide continuity for both clients and advisors, leveraging The Carlyle Group Inc.'s extensive global resources. This move is particularly opportune, considering the favorable industry trends that are currently supporting scaled, advisor-led wealth management platforms.The transaction is set to be completed in the second quarter of 2026, contingent upon standard regulatory approvals. Carlyle Group Inc. has a history of originating, structuring, and leading equity investments in various transactions, which aligns with its strategic objectives in the wealth management sector.While analysts recognize the potential of Carlyle Group (CG) as a solid investment, some investors may find that certain AI stocks present greater upside potential with lower risk. For those interested in diversifying their portfolio, a report detailing undervalued AI stocks is available, highlighting opportunities that could benefit from current market dynamics.

+ CARLYLE GROUP MOVES TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL FOR $2.8 BILLION

CLEVELAND - Global investment firm Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) has signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the registered investment advisor at more than $2.8 billion. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, as Carlyle seeks to strengthen its investment portfolio amidst a challenging period that has seen its stock decline 25% over the past six months.Under the terms of the transaction, Carlyle will assume majority ownership, while existing stakeholders, including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners, and Oak Hill Capital, will exit their positions. MAI employees will retain significant minority equity ownership. Carlyle initially invested in MAI in 2021 through its stake in Galway Holdings, which acquired the firm during that year. As of January 1, 2026, MAI manages $72.6 billion in total assets, operating 40 offices across the United States with a workforce exceeding 700 employees.Despite the recent volatility in Carlyle's stock performance, the firm maintains solid financial fundamentals, with liquid assets exceeding its short-term obligations and a track record of dividend payments for 15 consecutive years, currently yielding 3%. According to financial analyses, Carlyle's overall financial health is rated as 'FAIR'. Rick Buoncore, Chairman and CEO of MAI, emphasized the importance of Carlyle's alignment with their culture and values, stating, 'As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise.'The acquisition is set to be finalized in the second quarter of 2026, pending customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Analysts predict continued profitability for MAI, bolstered by Carlyle's support. Harvest Partners, which facilitated MAI's growth through more than 30 strategic acquisitions during its ownership, expressed confidence in the firm's future potential, with Stephen Carlson, President of Private Equity at Harvest Partners, stating, 'We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing.'In addition to this acquisition, Carlyle Group is actively pursuing new investment opportunities, including a defense-focused fund aimed at expanding the U.S. industrial base and a $1 billion investment in Airtel’s data center subsidiary, Nxtra Data Ltd. Furthermore, Carlyle has partnered with CVC and UBS to facilitate the distribution of their products to affluent clients. BMO Capital has reaffirmed its Outperform rating for Carlyle Group, maintaining a $65 price target following discussions with company executives, who expressed optimism about the company's growth trajectory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK WITH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a significant Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks an important milestone in the company's efforts to manage its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026, and it includes both the amendment of existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders.The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, introducing new provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, allowing lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, highlighting the company's strategic approach to financing.In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel has committed to filing registration statements with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This move is essential for maintaining investor confidence, as it reinforces the company's intention to provide liquidity and transparency to its stakeholders. The lenders have indicated their intent to hold the securities for investment purposes only, which could prevent immediate market fluctuations due to rapid selling.Investors should be aware of the potential implications of these developments. The issuance of a substantial number of new warrants could lead to dilution of existing shareholders' equity if exercised. Additionally, the company’s obligations to register these shares for resale could increase the total share count, which may affect the stock's market price. Such financing strategies can signal to the market the company's liquidity needs or strategic objectives.BioXcel Therapeutics has clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company, thereby adhering to full reporting and compliance requirements. The registration rights agreement, which includes various Oaktree entities among other institutional holders, reflects a commitment to ongoing compliance with securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor upcoming filings and press releases closely, as these changes may significantly influence BioXcel's financial landscape and market performance.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL FOR $355.8 MILLION SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management has successfully closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization in collaboration with North Mill Equipment Finance (NMEF). The transaction, known as OAKEF 2026-1, was completed on 8th April 2026 and showcases the strong investor appetite for high-quality equipment finance assets.The securitization is backed by equipment finance assets that were acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal finalized in November 2025. The offering, rated from AAA to single A, experienced significant demand, achieving peak subscription levels of approximately 6.3 times. This robust interest allowed for efficient execution across the capital stack, with a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities acted as the lead structuring agent for the transaction, while Deutsche Bank served as co-manager. Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree’s asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, expressed satisfaction with the market's reception, indicating that it reflects investor confidence in Oaktree's strategic approach to the equipment sector. Beer highlighted the collaboration with North Mill as a key factor in this successful execution.David Lee, chairman and CEO of NMEF, emphasized the importance of the partnership with Oaktree, stating that the execution underscores both the quality of the underlying assets and the strength of NMEF’s servicing platform. He noted that NMEF will continue to serve as the primary servicer for the assets included in the transaction, ensuring ongoing portfolio management and operational support.This latest securitization further cements Oaktree Capital Management's position in the asset-backed finance space, showcasing its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet the financing objectives of its partners. As the market for equipment financing evolves, Oaktree and North Mill are poised to capitalize on differentiated investment opportunities.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT AND NORTH MILL COMPLETE $355.8 MILLION SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have successfully closed a $355.8 million securitization backed by equipment finance assets, indicating robust investor interest in the equipment finance sector. As announced on 10th April 2026, the transaction was oversubscribed by approximately 6.3 times, reflecting a strong demand for such financial products.The securitization includes equipment loans and leases acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in November 2025, with North Mill Equipment Finance (NMEF) taking on the servicing responsibilities. This strategic partnership allows NMEF to leverage its expertise in managing small- to mid-ticket equipment loans and leases, typically valued between $15,000 and $3 million.The notes issued in the transaction received ratings ranging from AAA to A, with an average yield of 4.7% and a spread of 99 basis points. Truist Securities led the transaction, with Deutsche Bank serving as co-manager, underscoring the confidence of major financial institutions in the equipment finance market.Headquartered in Norwalk, Connecticut, North Mill Equipment Finance will continue to manage the portfolio post-securitization, ensuring ongoing operational support and loan management. Meanwhile, Los Angeles-based Oaktree Capital Management, which oversees approximately $223 billion in assets, is expanding its presence in the asset-backed securities (ABS) finance space.As the equipment finance landscape evolves, industry professionals will gather at the fourth annual Equipment Finance Connect, scheduled for May 18-19 at the C. Baldwin Hotel in Houston. This event will serve as a platform for equipment lenders and dealers to discuss trends, challenges, and opportunities in the sector, further highlighting the significance of partnerships like the one between Oaktree and North Mill.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, Llc announced a significant funding milestone, revealing it has secured $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc. This investment, which will grant Apollo a 13% stake in the company, was formalized on April 20, 2026. The transaction is structured through the issuance of convertible preferred shares and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.The funding values Medical Surgical Solutions, Llc at a post-money valuation of approximately $9.62 billion, a notable achievement for the company. As it prepares for this infusion of capital, the firm aims to leverage the new resources to enhance its operational capabilities and expand its market presence. Investors will be keen to monitor how this funding will influence the company's growth trajectory and overall performance.Apollo Global Management, known for its strategic investments across various sectors, has a history of backing companies with strong growth potential. The firm’s involvement with Medical Surgical Solutions, Llc could signal confidence in the healthcare sector, particularly in surgical solutions, which have seen increasing demand amid a rapidly evolving medical landscape. Apollo's insights into valuation challenges, especially in the software sector, may also provide valuable guidance as Medical Surgical Solutions navigates its expansion.This funding round is part of a broader trend of private equity firms investing heavily in healthcare, as they seek to capitalize on the sector's resilience and growth potential. With healthcare technologies increasingly being integrated into surgical procedures, Medical Surgical Solutions, Llc could be well-positioned to capitalize on these trends, potentially leading to enhanced shareholder value.As the transaction progresses through the necessary regulatory channels, stakeholders will await updates that could further clarify the implications of this partnership. Investors are advised to keep an eye on upcoming announcements, as the collaboration between Medical Surgical Solutions, Llc and Apollo Global Management may reshape the competitive landscape within the medical solutions market.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT TAKES 13% STAKE IN MCKESSON'S MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS

Apollo Global Management LLC has entered into a significant agreement to acquire a 13% minority stake in McKesson's Medical-Surgical Solutions division. On 20th April 2026, Apollo-affiliated funds committed $1.25 billion in convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. This investment is seen as a critical component of McKesson's strategy to separate its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and is expected to enhance operational efficiencies across the board.The recent investment by Apollo Global Management represents more than just a financial transaction; it signals a strategic alignment that could benefit both companies. McKesson's CEO has emphasized the importance of this deal in achieving long-term growth objectives, particularly in the context of the planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions division. This move is expected to provide McKesson with the necessary capital to bolster its strategic initiatives and focus on core business segments.Apollo Global Management, a prominent player in the asset management industry with $938.4 billion in total assets under management, is recognized for its diverse portfolio that includes private equity, real estate, and credit. The firm, which has a market capitalization of approximately $72.06 billion, has a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating that investors are willing to pay a premium for its earnings. This valuation metric suggests that Apollo is anticipated to deliver future growth, aligning with the strategic investment in McKesson.Despite the positive outlook, recent insider trading activity raises some caution. Over the past year, there have been six insider sells compared to just one insider buy, which may indicate a more conservative approach among company insiders regarding the stock's future performance. Additionally, Apollo's financial strength is rated at 6 out of 10, highlighting areas for improvement, particularly in managing debt levels.In light of the current market dynamics and the strategic investment in McKesson, Apollo Global Management appears well-positioned for potential growth. However, investors should remain vigilant, considering the insider selling activity and financial strength indicators. As Apollo continues to navigate its investment strategies, the implications of this deal with McKesson will be closely monitored by market participants.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT EXPANDS UK PRESENCE WITH GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management Inc. (NYSE: APO) has taken a significant step in enhancing its presence in the UK housing market with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group (GLG) from Gatehouse Bank. This transaction, which occurred on 1st April 2026, includes both Gatehouse Investment Management (GIM) and Ascend Properties, marking a strategic move for Apollo as it seeks to expand its footprint in residential investments.The financial specifics of the transaction have not been publicly disclosed, but the acquisition aligns with Apollo's strategy of investing in high-potential sectors. The addition of GLG complements Apollo Global Management Inc.'s existing interests in the UK housing ecosystem, which includes Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans. GLG is recognized as a leader in the Build-to-Rent single-family housing sector, managing a portfolio of over 10,000 properties nationwide and having acquired more than 5,000 homes.Under Apollo's ownership, GLG will continue to be directed by CEO Paul Stockwell and the existing management team, ensuring continuity in operations. The platform intends to leverage both its own capital and that of third-party partners to expedite the development of high-quality rental housing. Ascend Properties will also maintain its role in providing management services for external institutional portfolios, further solidifying GLG's position in the market.For Gatehouse Bank, the divestiture is part of a broader strategy to concentrate on its core retail growth and the demand for Shariah-compliant home finance products, which have gained traction among both UK and international homebuyers. By selling GLG, Gatehouse Bank aims to enhance its focus on these growing areas while benefiting from the increased operational capabilities of Apollo Global Management Inc.As Apollo Global Management Inc. continues to diversify its investment portfolio, the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group signifies its commitment to the UK housing market. Investors will be keen to observe how this strategic move unfolds, particularly in light of the ongoing demand for rental properties and the evolving landscape of residential real estate.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH WHITESTONE REIT ACQUISITION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has announced an agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, effectively taking the retail-focused real estate investment trust private. This strategic move is aimed at expanding Ares' portfolio in necessity-based retail centers located in rapidly growing metropolitan areas across the United States.The announcement, made on 14th April 2026, also included significant leadership changes within Ares Management's Asia operations. E.G. Morse has been appointed as Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team. These appointments reflect Ares' commitment to enhancing its credit and special situations strategies in the region, aligning with the company's broader growth objectives.The acquisition of Whitestone REIT is particularly noteworthy as it allows Ares Management to bolster its position in the retail real estate sector, catering to essential consumer needs. Investors monitoring NYSE:ARES should consider how this acquisition fits into Ares' overall strategy, which increasingly emphasizes private credit, real assets, and special situations. The move underscores Ares' intent to diversify its capital allocation across various geographies and asset types.Key considerations for investors include the integration of Whitestone into Ares’ existing operational framework, as well as the execution of fundraising initiatives in Asia. The new leadership team is expected to play a critical role in determining how capital is deployed and managed across regions. Nonetheless, investors should be mindful of potential risks, including profit margin compression, high levels of debt, and the current dividend yield of 5.38%, which is not fully supported by earnings.As Ares Management embarks on this significant acquisition and leadership restructuring, the implications for its future growth trajectory will be closely watched. The market's response to these developments could provide insights into how effectively Ares can leverage its new assets and leadership to enhance shareholder value over time.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (ARES) RAISES $5.4 BILLION, TARGETS VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE:ARES) has recently announced a significant fundraising achievement, securing approximately $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in both the U.S. and Europe. As of 9th April 2026, the company aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities in supply-constrained property markets, which are anticipated to rebound in the near future. This fundraising effort underscores Ares Management's strategic focus on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage investments.The $5.4 billion raised includes $1.9 billion allocated for the EPEP IV fund and about $3.5 billion directed towards Ares' U.S. value-add strategy. Each of these strategies has already deployed or identified around $1.1 billion of initial investments, reflecting a proactive approach to tapping into potential growth sectors. Analysts have shown optimism towards Ares Management, with approximately 75% maintaining a bullish sentiment, and a consensus price target of $161 suggesting a potential upside of around 60%.However, not all market observers share this bullish outlook. On 24th March 2026, BMO Capital revised its price target for Ares Management from $140 to $112, maintaining a 'Market Perform' rating. This adjustment highlights concerns regarding BDC redemptions, credit issues in asset-based finance, and broader market volatility. Despite these challenges, Ares Management continues to demonstrate resilience and adaptability in a fluctuating economic landscape.Founded as a global alternative investment manager, Ares Management specializes in providing tailored solutions across various sectors, including credit, private equity, and real estate. As of the end of 2025, the firm had attracted significant institutional investment, notably from BlackRock Inc., which holds a 7.12% stake in the company. This backing reflects a strong confidence in Ares Management's long-term strategies and market positioning.While Ares Management shows promise as a solid investment choice, the competitive landscape suggests that certain AI stocks may present greater upside potential with reduced downside risks. + ARES MANAGEMENT L.P EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH WHITESTONE REIT ACQUISITION

Ares Management L.P has entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT for approximately $990 million. This strategic acquisition aims to enhance Ares Management L.P's real estate investment capabilities and expand its portfolio in the retail and mixed-use property sectors. The announcement was made on the 9th of April 2026, with the transaction expected to close in the coming months, pending regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.The acquisition of Whitestone REIT marks a significant move for Ares Management L.P, which is known for its diversified investment strategies across various asset classes. Whitestone REIT, which operates a portfolio of retail properties primarily located in high-density metropolitan areas, presents an opportunity for Ares Management L.P to leverage its expertise in real estate management and capitalize on the growing demand for well-located retail spaces.Ares Management L.P's decision to acquire Whitestone REIT aligns with its long-term growth strategy, focusing on high-quality assets that can generate stable cash flows. Analysts have noted that the merger could provide Ares Management L.P with increased scale and operational efficiencies, allowing the firm to better navigate the evolving landscape of real estate investment. The deal is expected to be accretive to Ares Management L.P's earnings in the near term.Investors are keenly watching this development, as it reflects Ares Management L.P's commitment to expanding its footprint in the real estate sector. The firm has a strong track record of successful acquisitions, and this latest move is anticipated to enhance its competitive positioning within the market. The financial community is particularly interested in how this acquisition will impact Ares Management L.P's overall portfolio performance and growth trajectory in the coming years.As Ares Management L.P integrates Whitestone REIT into its operations, stakeholders will be looking for updates on the progress of the merger and its implications for the company's strategic objectives.
