Investors are encouraged to remain vigilant and consider diverse opportunities within a rapidly evolving market, including sectors benefitting from emerging trends and macroeconomic shifts.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH WHITESTONE REIT ACQUISITION

Ares Management L.P has entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT for approximately $990 million. This strategic acquisition aims to enhance Ares Management L.P's real estate investment capabilities and expand its portfolio in the retail and mixed-use property sectors. The announcement was made on the 9th of April 2026, with the transaction expected to close in the coming months, pending regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.The acquisition of Whitestone REIT marks a significant move for Ares Management L.P, which is known for its diversified investment strategies across various asset classes. Whitestone REIT, which operates a portfolio of retail properties primarily located in high-density metropolitan areas, presents an opportunity for Ares Management L.P to leverage its expertise in real estate management and capitalize on the growing demand for well-located retail spaces.Ares Management L.P's decision to acquire Whitestone REIT aligns with its long-term growth strategy, focusing on high-quality assets that can generate stable cash flows. Analysts have noted that the merger could provide Ares Management L.P with increased scale and operational efficiencies, allowing the firm to better navigate the evolving landscape of real estate investment. The deal is expected to be accretive to Ares Management L.P's earnings in the near term.Investors are keenly watching this development, as it reflects Ares Management L.P's commitment to expanding its footprint in the real estate sector. The firm has a strong track record of successful acquisitions, and this latest move is anticipated to enhance its competitive positioning within the market. The financial community is particularly interested in how this acquisition will impact Ares Management L.P's overall portfolio performance and growth trajectory in the coming years.As Ares Management L.P integrates Whitestone REIT into its operations, stakeholders will be looking for updates on the progress of the merger and its implications for the company's strategic objectives. With the real estate market showing signs of recovery and growth, this acquisition could serve as a pivotal moment for Ares Management L.P, solidifying its status as a leading player in the investment management industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES CARGO OPERATIONS TO UAE AMID SAFETY CONCERNS

Qatar Airways Cargo has officially resumed its freighter and belly operations to and from Doha, following a temporary halt in services due to safety concerns. As of 28th April 2026, the airline has published its new schedule, which includes the resumption of freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly, starting from 26th April.In addition to the Dubai route, Qatar Airways Cargo is set to launch freighter flights to Sharjah (SHJ) three times a week, commencing on 1st May. The airline is also restarting daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ), with plans to expand its freighter destinations to over 50 by 1st May. Furthermore, the passenger network is expected to grow to encompass over 150 destinations starting from 16th June.As shipment restrictions have been lifted on specific urgent freight services to routes such as Victoria, Australia, Qatar Airways Cargo will now offer products under special handling codes, effective 1st May. These include QRLIV codes for commodities like fish, mammals, and diplomatic cargo, as well as LHO codes for the transportation of live human organs and blood.Qatar Airways Cargo has ensured that the remaining products remain accessible for booking through standard sales channels. Customers can make reservations via the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, reflecting the airline's commitment to maintaining robust service levels despite previous operational challenges.The resumption of operations by Qatar Airways Cargo is a significant development for logistics and transportation in the region, signaling a recovery in freight services to the UAE. Investors and stakeholders are closely monitoring the airline's expansion plans, which may enhance connectivity and service offerings in the coming months.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS UNVEILS NEXT-GEN QSUITE TO ELEVATE BUSINESS TRAVEL EXPERIENCE

Qatar Airways, the flag carrier of Qatar, is poised to revolutionize the business class experience with its upcoming Qsuite Next Generation. This innovative product is slated to launch later this year, following delays in the delivery of the airline's new Boeing 777-9 fleet. As of 23rd April 2026, the new Qsuite will first be available on the Airbus A350-1000, further enhancing the airline's reputation in the premium travel sector.The Qsuite has been a hallmark of Qatar Airways' service since its introduction nearly a decade ago, offering features such as sliding doors, double beds, and adaptable seating arrangements that cater to families and small groups. The airline is doubling down on these elements with the Next Generation Qsuite, which will incorporate advanced technology and a focus on space management, thereby appealing to business travelers who value flexibility and privacy during flights.The new suite reflects Qatar Airways' broader strategy of positioning itself as a leader in premium air travel. With its hub at Doha Hamad International Airport consistently recognized as one of the world's best, the airline has established a comprehensive premium ecosystem. Passengers flying in Qsuite enjoy exclusive benefits, including dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and access to luxurious lounges that enhance their overall travel experience.Originally launched in 2017, the Qsuite's modular design allows for a variety of seating configurations, transforming traditional business class norms. The Next Generation Qsuite aims to build on this concept, offering features like a movable 4K OLED screen and enhanced privacy options. This product is not merely an upgrade; it represents Qatar Airways' commitment to staying ahead of competitors who are increasingly introducing their own high-end business class products.As the demand for premium travel continues to grow, the introduction of the Qsuite Next Generation is a strategic move for Qatar Airways, designed to secure its position in a competitive market. While the existing Qsuite has performed well, the airline recognizes that innovation is key to maintaining its edge. This investment in product development is part of a broader strategy to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure passengers, ensuring that Qatar Airways remains a top choice for premium air travel.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS OPERATIONS AS NETWORK RESTORATION CONTINUES

Qatar Airways continues to enhance its operational capabilities, as reflected in its latest revised schedule. The airline has been progressively increasing both belly and freighter operations to and from Doha. As of the 20th of April 2026, Qatar Airways has successfully restored its passenger network to over 150 destinations, while its freighter services have expanded to cover more than 50 locations worldwide.The airline's gradual network restoration is part of its larger strategy to rebuild capacity and meet growing demand in the air cargo market. Qatar Airways Cargo emphasizes that, while the network is expanding, certain shipments remain under embargo. Specifically, special handling codes such as AVF, AVM, and DOC are still restricted, along with live human organs and blood. However, customers can book QRLIV shipments under different special handling codes, provided the transit time in Doha does not exceed 24 hours.Qatar Airways Cargo has also announced that all other products are available for booking through standard sales channels, which include the Digital Lounge and local sales teams. The airline's commitment to restoring its network reflects its adaptability and responsiveness to the evolving logistics landscape, as global demand for air freight continues to rise.As Qatar Airways continues to lift embargoes in a phased manner, the airline is focused on enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency. The phased removal of restrictions on certain cargo types demonstrates Qatar Airways' strategic approach to balancing safety and operational capability in a competitive market.With a strong commitment to service excellence, Qatar Airways is positioning itself to capture a significant share of the air cargo market as it navigates the complexities of recovery and expansion. The airline's ongoing efforts in network restoration are expected to further bolster its reputation as a leading global cargo carrier.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Environmental Power OTCMKTS:EPGRQ - Get Free Report and Montauk Renewables NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report are both energy companies, but which is the better investment? We will contrast the two businesses based on the strength of their valuation, profitability, risk, analyst recommendations, institutional ownership, dividends and earnings.

Analyst Recommendations

This is a breakdown of recent ratings and recommmendations for Environmental Power and Montauk Renewables, as provided by MarketBeat. Sell RatingsHold RatingsBuy RatingsStrong Buy RatingsRating ScoreEnvironmental Power00000.00Montauk Renewables13012.20

Summary

Montauk Renewables beats Environmental Power on 9 of the 9 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Environmental Power Get Free Report

Environmental Power Corporation engages in the development, ownership, and operation of renewable energy production facilities in the United States. The company develops renewable energy facilities for the production and commercial application of methane-rich biogas produced from animal, food industry, and other organic wastes. The biogas could be sold to end-users or used to produce pipeline-grade methane, which is referred as renewable natural gas, liquefied natural gas, compressed natural gas, and renewable electrical energy or thermal energy, as well as to produce other useful by-products. Environmental Power Corporation operates three single digester facilities in Wisconsin. The company was founded in 1982 and is headquartered in Tarrytown, New York.

About Montauk Renewables Get Free Report

Montauk Renewables, Inc., a renewable energy company, engages in recovery and processing of biogas from landfills and other non-fossil fuel sources. It operates in two segments, Renewable Natural Gas and Renewable Electricity Generation. The company develops, owns, and operates renewable natural gas RNG projects that captures methane and prevents it from being released into the atmosphere by converting it into either RNG or electrical power for the electrical grid. Its customers for RNG and renewable identification numbers RIN include large, long-term owner-operators of landfills and livestock farms, local utilities, and large refiners in the natural gas and refining sectors. Montauk Renewables, Inc. was founded in 1980 and is headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.

value.Title: April 23, 2026: Foundation Signals Strategic Investment in Reskilling as AI Advances

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG8-T4N3-RS3P-10SJ-00000-00April 23, 2026: Foundation Signals Strategic Investment in Reskilling as AI Advances2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 23, 2026: Foundation Signals Strategic Investment in Reskilling as AI Advances

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

SECTION 2 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RSGT-S018-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION INVESTS IN WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI CHALLENGES

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking a proactive stance against the impending challenges posed by artificial intelligence on the workforce, committing significant funding to a national initiative aimed at enhancing workforce resilience. On 28th April 2026, the century-old philanthropic institution announced its strategy to address the disruption caused by automation, focusing on preventing job displacement and helping workers transition into AI-complementary roles.The initiative is designed as a grant-based program that prioritizes workforce interventions over product development. The Rockefeller Foundation's approach emphasizes the importance of program design, metrics, and evaluation. Central to this effort will be the measurement of displacement risk, deployment outcomes, and labor-market transitions, which are crucial for understanding the impact of automation on employment.Funding for the initiative is expected to come through various instruments, including grants, programmatic pilots, and partnerships with employers, training providers, and local governments. The choices made during the implementation phase will be critical in determining whether the Rockefeller Foundation's funding leads to scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects with limited long-term impact.This move by the Rockefeller Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative. By investing at this scale, the foundation aims to catalyze innovative models for retraining and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as universal basic income, sectoral training, and apprenticeship programs. This initiative is poised to increase the demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.As the Rockefeller Foundation rolls out this substantial program, stakeholders will be keenly observing its funding priorities, evaluation criteria, and partnership dynamics. Key considerations will include whether the initiative emphasizes durable credentialing, employer engagement, or short-term placement metrics. Ultimately, the decisions made now will be pivotal in determining whether this initiative can effectively influence labor-market outcomes or become primarily a source of funding for pilot programs.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION CHAMPIONS ECONOMIC PATHWAYS AT BALTIMORE EVENT

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking significant strides to address economic challenges in the United States, hosting the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event that gathered over 250 leaders from various sectors. On 21st April 2026, the Foundation convened policymakers, philanthropists, and private-sector representatives in Baltimore to discuss and develop transformative solutions aimed at revitalizing the economy and fostering opportunity across communities.Key among the announcements made at the event, the Rockefeller Foundation launched a three-year, $100 million initiative designed to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America’s most distressed communities. This effort aims to create approximately 1.6 million additional good jobs across sectors with the strongest growth potential, including healthcare, energy transition, food systems, and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, President of the Rockefeller Foundation, emphasized the importance of restoring the promise of a stable life through hard work, underscoring the Foundation's commitment to the resilience of American workers.In addition to the job initiative, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a supplementary $12 million support for its 'Invest in Our Future' program, which focuses on mobilizing clean energy opportunities and fostering economic growth in underserved communities. Maryland Governor Wes Moore, who participated in the event, declared $1.5 million in philanthropic awards aimed at enhancing education and childcare in areas of high childhood poverty, further demonstrating the collaborative efforts being made to address systemic issues.The event also saw the unveiling of the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, consisting of ten innovators dedicated to expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas. Moreover, the Rockefeller Foundation's collaboration with various partners, including the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator, highlighted the importance of cross-sector partnerships in tackling climate and technology challenges, as well as enhancing community resilience and economic opportunities.Looking ahead, the next 'Big Bets for America' event is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026, promising to further the dialogue and action initiated in Baltimore. The Rockefeller Foundation's ongoing commitment to fostering economic growth and opportunity reflects a strategic approach to addressing the pressing economic challenges faced by communities nationwide.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S SIMON WINTER ADVOCATES FOR UNIFIED DATA AT ONE HEALTH SUMMIT

Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, emphasized the urgent need for data unification at the One Health Summit. Speaking on April 21st, 2026, Winter addressed the growing challenge of fragmented data systems across humanitarian, nutritional, and climate sectors, which he believes hinders effective decision-making and resource allocation.During the panel discussion titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children," Winter articulated that despite having access to unprecedented amounts of data, it remains trapped in silos. He noted, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." His remarks reflect a broader concern about the inefficiencies within various data systems that serve humanitarian and environmental needs.Winter's perspective resonates with earlier comments made by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025. Ellison urged for a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations, asserting that the fragmented nature of data—scattered across approximately 3,000 databases—limits its utility for AI and decision-making. The Rockefeller Foundation's call for data integration mirrors this sentiment, advocating for collaboration among local and international stakeholders.To illustrate the potential benefits of unified data, Winter cited the ongoing drought crisis in Somalia. The Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator to create a food and nutrition systems dashboard that integrates various data sets. This tool aims to provide timely information to local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually to support local farmers and pastoralists.Winter concluded his address by emphasizing the necessity of empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, which can be combined with local information. This approach, he believes, could lead to a more effective and cost-efficient solution to the complex challenges posed by fragmented data systems, ultimately enhancing humanitarian responses in crisis situations.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION RECOGNIZED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO HUMAN RIGHTS IN NIGERIA

The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has honored the Ford Foundation for its sustained contributions to advancing human rights in Nigeria, reinforcing a partnership that has played a critical role in strengthening access to justice and institutional capacity. On 28th April 2026, during a ceremony in Abuja, the NHRC presented an Award of Appreciation to the Foundation in recognition of its longstanding support.The award was received by the Foundation’s Regional Director for West Africa, ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, during a high-level engagement with directors and senior officials of the Commission. Speaking at the event, the Executive Secretary of the NHRC, Dr. Tony Ojukwu, commended the Ford Foundation’s consistent backing, particularly in initiatives aimed at expanding access to justice and strengthening the Commission’s operational capacity.Dr. Ojukwu emphasized that given the breadth and complexity of human rights challenges in Nigeria, the Commission cannot function in isolation. He stated that sustained collaboration with development partners remains essential to transforming human rights protection from policy intentions into tangible outcomes for citizens.In her remarks, Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as both significant and encouraging, reaffirming the Foundation’s commitment to promoting human rights. She noted that a key focus of the Foundation’s work is ensuring that citizens are not only aware of their rights but are also supported in seeking justice when those rights are violated. However, she emphasized that awareness alone is insufficient without strong and effective enforcement systems.The engagement underscored the importance of continued cooperation between the Commission and its partners in addressing evolving human rights challenges across the country. In a vote of thanks, Senior Human Rights Adviser to the Commission, Hilary Ogbonna, expressed appreciation for the Ford Foundation’s enduring partnership, highlighting key institutional advancements, including initiatives such as the MOVE project and the Commission’s dashboard system, which benefitted from the Foundation’s early support.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO ADDRESS GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE

The Ford Foundation has called on First Ladies across the ECOWAS region to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence (GBV). The Foundation described the First Ladies’ 'soft power' as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. This call was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, while delivering a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies’ Forum on Zero Tolerance for Gender-Based Violence, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, she noted, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of Gross Domestic Product in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose 'Hands Off Our Girls' campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how 'soft power,' when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.The Ford Foundation has continued to invest in strengthening GBV prevention and response systems across West Africa, including partnerships involving First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria. These interventions have aimed to advance advocacy efforts, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu emphasized that laws and policies are insufficient without public trust and accessibility, pointing out the persistent silence among survivors driven by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.In light of recent regional initiatives such as the GBV Roadmap (2026–2028), Legal Scorecard, and Joint Communiqu , Aniagolu urged First Ladies to move beyond mere commitments by promoting safe reporting, challenging harmful norms, and strengthening accountability systems. 'No girl should grow up believing silence is her only option, and no woman should be denied justice because of where she lives,' she asserted, reaffirming the Ford Foundation’s commitment to supporting advocacy, strengthening systems, and amplifying women’s voices across the region.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ADVOCATES FOR WOMEN-CENTRIC JUSTICE REFORMS IN NIGERIA

In a recent dialogue organized by the Ford Foundation, stakeholders and gender advocates emphasized the urgent need for a comprehensive redesign of Nigeria's justice system to better serve women and girls. The event, held on the sidelines of CSW70 in New York, took place on the 16th of April, 2026, and focused on the effectiveness of the nation’s plural justice system.The discussion extended beyond legal frameworks to address the lived realities of survivors, highlighting that justice is only achievable when legal literacy is enhanced and economic and cultural barriers that silence women are dismantled. Nigeria's justice landscape is shaped by statutory, customary, and religious systems, which ideally should work in harmony. However, in practice, many women fall through the gaps of these systems, often facing costly and protracted legal processes.His Royal Majesty, Obi Benjamin Ikenchukwu Keagboruzi, the Dein of Agbor, shared insights on the practical obstacles hindering women's access to formal justice. He pointed out that court fees, legal representation, and lost work hours cumulatively push justice further out of reach for many women. As a result, women often resort to more accessible customary systems, which may not provide adequate protection.Justice Bukunola Adebiyi of the Lagos State High Court echoed similar sentiments, stressing the importance of strengthening investigative and evidentiary processes to ensure justice is delivered effectively. The dialogue also featured poignant reflections from Ngozi Valentina Enih, Commissioner for Children, Gender Affairs, and Social Development in Enugu State, who, as a survivor herself, highlighted the devastating trade-offs families face when seeking justice.Elsa Stamatopoulou, an Adjunct Professor of Anthropology, offered a nuanced perspective on cultural barriers, suggesting they can also serve as resources for empowerment. Moderator Professor Joy Ezeilo, Executive Director of the Women’s Aid Collective (WACOL), concluded that Nigeria's plural legal system presents not a weakness but an opportunity for comprehensive reforms. Dr. ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, Regional Director for West Africa at the Ford Foundation, reiterated that genuine progress will come from understanding and addressing the realities faced by women and girls in Nigeria.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGC-03H3-RS95-R0MH-00000-00New Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy Study Findings Have Been Reported by Investigators at Texas State University (Strategic Assessment of Socio-environmental Conflicts In Solar Hydrogen Production: a Case of Antofagasta, Chile)2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisECOLOGY - CLEAN TECHNOLOGIES AND ENVIRONMENTAL POLICYNew Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy Study Findings Have Been Reported by Investigators at Texas State University (Strategic Assessment of Socio-environmental Conflicts In Solar Hydrogen Production: a Case of Antofagasta, Chile)


2026 APR 01 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Ecology Daily News -- Investigators discuss new findings in Ecology - Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy. According to news reporting out of San Marcos, Texas, by NewsRx editors, research stated, "Chile stands out for its exceptional potential for non-conventional renewable energy production, positioning the country as a leading global hub for green hydrogen generation. However, this opportunity carries the critical responsibility of addressing the socio-environmental conflicts (SEC) that have historically affected energy projects in the country."


Financial support for this research came from Agencia Nacional de Investigacin y Desarrollo.
Our news journalists obtained a quote from the research from Texas State University, "This study proposes a novel self-organizing map (SOM)-based methodology to identify and classify optimal areas for the installation of photovoltaic plants dedicated to green hydrogen production in the Antofagasta region of northern Chile. This methodology explicitly integrates socio-environmental and technical factors within a single unsupervised learning approach. The SOM clusters potential areas into three conflict-sensitive typologies: (i) ideal zones, areas close to industrial infrastructure but distant from socio-environmental hotspots; (ii) promising zones, areas with moderate proximity to both industrial and conflict zones; and (iii) challenging zones, areas near SEC hotspots that present significant barriers for development. This typology supports early-stage, conflict-aware planning by highlighting areas where photovoltaic (PV)-based hydrogen projects can help decarbonize sacrifice zones while reducing the risk of new conflicts."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "The methodology delivers a transferable decision-support tool for the strategic placement of solar-powered hydrogen production facilities that aligns with principles of just energy transition and environmental justice, ensuring that socio-environmental considerations are systematically incorporated into green hydrogen planning."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Strategic Assessment of Socio-environmental Conflicts In Solar Hydrogen Production: a Case of Antofagasta, Chile. Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy, 2026;28(5). Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy can be contacted at: Springer, One New York Plaza, Suite 4600, New York, Ny, United States. (Springer - www.springer.com; Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy - www.springerlink.com/content/1618-954x/)
Our news journalists report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Alejandro Fernandez Gil, Texas State University, Ingram School of Engineering, San Marcos, TX 78666, United States. Additional authors for this research include Isidora Abasolo Farfan, Pablo Rodriguez Machuca, Rene Garrido Lazo, Carolina Bonacic Castro, Roberto Carmona Campos, Angel Rodriguez Soto, Yunesky Masip Macia, Daniel Serafini, Marcelo Mena-Carrasco Soto, Luis Ramirez Camargo and Javier Valdes.
The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi.org/10.1007/s10098-026-03440-9. This DOI is a link to an online electronic document that is either free or for purchase, and can be your direct source for a journal article and its citation.
Keywords for this news article include: San Marcos, Texas, United States, North and Central America, Clean Technologies and Environmental Policy, Ecology, Electronics, Elements, Gases, Hydrogen, Inorganic Chemicals, Photovoltaic, Texas State University.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK TRUSTS & ESTATES GROUP RANKED BAND 1 IN 2026 LEGAL 500 US PRIVATE CLIENT GUIDE

Milbank LLP's Trusts, Estates and Exempt Organizations Group is top-ranked for Private Wealth in New York in the 2026 Legal 500 US Private Client Guide. In addition to the firm recognition, co-practice group leader Austin Bramwell is featured as a "Leading Partner," and co-practice group leader Jessica Soojian is "Recommended."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED ELLIOTT IN CONNECTION WITH ITS INVESTMENT IN STRATOLAUNCH

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ WILLKIE EMEA RESTRUCTURING PRACTICE EXCELS IN Q1 2026 LEAGUE TABLES

Willkie is recognized as a market leader in both the Octus and 9Fin restructuring league tables for the first quarter of 2026, earning multiple top-five rankings for its client work across an array of European restructuring matters.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE ADVISES DELEK LOGISTICS PARTNERS ON NEW $1.3 BILLION CREDIT FACILITY

Willkie advised Delek Logistics Partners, LP, a public company specializing in the provision of midstream energy services, in connection with a $1.3 billion revolving credit facility with Truist Bank, the proceeds of which were used to refinance its existing credit facility with Fifth Third Bank, National Association, as well as for other general corporate purposes.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP: HOW FAR DOES PRESIDENTIAL REMOVAL AUTHORITY GO?

On May 7 at 12:15 pm (ET), partner and Chair of the Appeals & Strategic Motions Practice Group Erica Ross will speak on the virtual panel titled "How Far Does Presidential Removal Authority Go?" hosted by the New York University School of Law. Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES LEADING SEMICONDUCTOR INFRASTRUCTURE INVESTOR MAVERICK SILICON ON FINANCING FOR MMTRON

mmTron, a semiconductor startup redefining the RF layer powering next‑generation connectivity, has announced US$45 million in Series A financing. Kenneth Safar at Maverick Silicon led the round with participation from Kelvin Khoo at Tipping Point Ventures, J&M, and other investors. This funding milestone endorses mmTron's demonstrated capabilities and its product‑market mission to redefine high‑performance RF connectivity.

Latham & Watkins LLP represented Maverick Silicon in the transaction with a corporate deal team led by partners John Miller and Haim Zaltzman, with associates Mason Taylor, Samantha McGarvey, and William Brown.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES BLACKSTONE INFRASTRUCTURE IN INVESTMENT OF UP TO 2 BILLION IN EUROWIND ENERGY

Latham & Watkins has advised Blackstone Infrastructure on an agreement to invest up to EUR2 billion in Eurowind Energy, a leading pan-European renewables developer and independent power producer. The transaction is expected to close before the end of the year, subject to customary conditions.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES CONCORD ON COMBINATION WITH BMG TO CREATE WORLDS LEADING INDEPENDENT MUSIC COMPANY

BMG and Concord have announced a definitive agreement to combine their businesses, creating the leading independent music company in the world. Operating under the BMG name, the combined company will bring together two highly complementary businesses to establish a scaled, global company delivering superior service to artists, songwriters, and partners - firmly grounded in an independent, artist-first model.

Latham & Watkins LLP advised Concord in the transaction with a corporate deal team led by partners Nadia Sager, Javier Stark, and Shanta Chirravuri.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES ON ASIAN INFRASTRUCTURE INVESTMENT BANKS US$2 BILLION BOND OFFERING

Latham & Watkins has advised the underwriters on Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank's (AIIB) issuance of US$2 billion 3.875% notes due 2031. The firm previously advised on AIIB's US$1 billion offering in January 2026.

The Latham team was led by London corporate partner Ryan Benedict, with associates Valera Bamgbala, Caroline Koller Hill, and Tom Breheny.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [4 Press Releases]

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: LIFE SCIENCES ANTITRUST: RECENT EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENFORCEMENT AND TRENDS

The European Commission appears to be advancing a more expansive antitrust agenda in life sciences - one increasingly focused on innovation, exclusionary conduct and strategic interference with future competition. Counsel Michael Frese and European counsel Georgios Zacharodimos discuss how recent cases highlight the EC's efforts to prevent innovation suppression, unfair practices and anticompetitive conduct across the pharmaceutical sector, where merger reviews, abuse investigations and cartel enforcement are reshaping industry dynamics.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NINTH CIRCUIT ENJOINS CALIFORNIA CORPORATE CLIMATE RISK DISCLOSURE LAW, BUT THE WAITING GAME CONTINUES

On November 18, 2025, the Ninth Circuit stayed the enforcement of a California law requiring certain companies to publish a climate risk report. Whether companies will need to disclose their greenhouse gas emissions in 2026 remains an open question. Partners Raquel Fox and Virginia Milstead, counsel Caroline Kim and Liz Malone and associate Sydney Smith discuss the legal challenges, ongoing regulatory uncertainties and implications for companies' compliance planning.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NEW YEAR, NEW POLITICAL LAW TRENDS

Spending on federal lobbying reached an all-time high of approximately $4.5 billion in 2024 and continues to increase. Federal political action committees (PACs) raised $25.3 billion in the 2020 election cycle and $25.4 billion in the 2024 cycle, both more than doubling any previous election cycle, according to Federal Election Commission data. This upward trend continues in the 2026 cycle.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: BANK OF ENGLAND REVISES ITS PROPOSED REGIME FOR REGULATING SYSTEMICSTABLECOIN

Bank of England proposals for regulating sterling-denominated stablecoins have shifted since 2023, but remain significantly more onerous than the FCA's proposals for non-systemic stablecoins in many respects. Partners Simon Toms and Sebastian Barling and associate Joseph Kamyar discuss the bank's latest proposals - including criteria for systemic importance, asset composition, capital and reserve standards, holding limits, legal claims and safeguarding measures - as well as the next steps for consultation and implementation.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ SIMPSON THACHER REPRESENTS INITIAL PURCHASERS IN $350 MILLION SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING BY TXNM

Simpson Thacher recently represented the initial purchasers, led by MUFG, Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets, in connection with an offering of $350 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.000% Fixed-to-Fixed Reset Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056 by TXNM Energy, Inc. ("TXNM"). TXNM intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay short-term debt and for general corporate purposes.

TXNM is an energy holding company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Through its regulated utilities, Public Service Company of New Mexico and Texas-New Mexico Power Company, TXNM delivers energy to more than 800,000 homes and businesses across New Mexico and Texas.

The Simpson Thacher team for the transaction included Mark Brod, Barry Lau and Emily Anne Owen (Capital Markets); Jon Cantor and Jemma Li (Tax); and Jennie Getsin (FINRA and Blue Sky).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT MARK SKERRY REFERENCED IN MLEX ON CFIUS PRIORITIES AND OUTLOOK FOR 2026

Partner Mark Skerry was referenced in an MLex article examining the priorities and policy direction of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) last year and the trends to look for in 2026. Mark provided insights on emerging regulatory measures regarding foreign investment, noting that while CFIUS will continue to address national security threats, other federal and state-level measures shouldn't be overlooked. These measures, Mark observed, will have a significant impact on investment into and out of the United States in the coming year.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT GEOFF BAILHACHE AND JAMES HOWE QUOTED IN LAW.COM ON M&A OUTLOOK IN EUROPE

Geoff Bailhache and James R. Howe, co-heads of the Firm's European M&A practice, were quoted in Law.com on what to expect from dealmaking activity in 2026. James explained how "European deal activity strengthened notably through the second half of 2025, with large cap and mid-market transactions rebounding as assets that had been held back earlier in the year finally came to market." Geoff also highlighted "despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges, an improving IPO market in both the U.S. and Europe has provided an important tailwind, reinforcing confidence and helping to stimulate M&A activity."

Source: Company Website
