urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2455-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATED NEWS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATED NEWS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP ARRANGES $96 MILLION FINANCING FOR JASPER DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES MULTIFAMILY PROJECT

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a newly developed 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing, facilitated by Fortress affiliates, was completed on Feb. 9, 2026, highlighting Fortress's ongoing commitment to supporting high-quality real estate developments.Jasper is owned and developed by Cityview and sits near the University of Southern California campus. The property comprises studios, one- and two-bedroom residences featuring upscale finishes and a comprehensive amenity package, including affordable housing units. Proceeds from the loan will refinance existing construction debt and back the ongoing leasing efforts for the new community.Cityview's Damian Gancman described Fortress's involvement as efficient and solution-oriented, noting a seamless execution from start to finish. Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt at Fortress, said the project aligns with the firm's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily housing with significant sponsor equity. Fortress, founded in 1998, manages about $54 billion in assets as of Sept. 30, 2025, and continues to invest across credit, real estate, private equity and permanent capital strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) PARTNERS WITH ALTERRA TO ACCELERATE GLOBAL CLIMATE INVESTMENTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Partners With ALTERRA to Accelerate Global Climate InvestmentsKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) secured a commitment from the UAE's ALTERRA, a $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to bolster its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made Apr. 22, 2026, as KKR seeks to mobilize private capital for sustainable energy projects. The terms of the commitment were not disclosed.ALTERRA, launched at COP28 with UAE backing, aims to de-risk climate investments and has already formed partnerships with BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG. With KKR’s inclusion, ALTERRA now counts a broad set of investment expertise and targets up to $250 billion in mobilized climate finance by 2030, a substantial share of which is expected to flow to developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, spans renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. The firm manages more than $100 billion in assets and has invested over $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable bets include Zenobe in the United Kingdom, EGC in Germany, Avantus in the United States and IGNIS P2X for industrial decarbonization. Gailliot and Lagarrigue highlighted that ALTERRA’s global reach combined with KKR’s infrastructure expertise could scale proven technologies and build more resilient energy systems.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) INVESTS IN STARITAS THROUGH ACCEL-KKR SPIN-OUT

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through its Accel-KKR unit to back the spin-out from ECR as it seeks growth in digital technology markets. The announcement signals KKR's continued focus on technology investments through Accel-KKR and aligns with Staritas' standalone growth plan. Staritas will operate independently from ECR to accelerate product development and market penetration, aided by the resources and expertise of Accel-KKR and KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR). The collaboration aims to position Staritas to respond quickly to evolving customer demands in the digital space. Investors will watch how the Staritas partnership unfolds in a competitive tech landscape, as the management team plans to expand offerings and strengthen service capabilities with this new capital and guidance.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT GLOBAL HEAD OF DIRECT LENDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut Global Head of Direct LendingTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending, effective Apr. 20, 2026. He has led KKR Real Estate's credit business, growing the platform from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by the end of 2025, and will oversee growth across a broader geographic footprint.Paul Fine, a 2015 hire, is named head of originations to succeed Traut. Fine, previously a managing director, will focus on strengthening client relationships and expanding the firm's capital base. The real estate credit team has grown from 10 employees in 2015 to 130 today, with Ali Imraan leading a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.The leadership transition signals a continued focus on growth and innovation in global real estate credit as KKR looks to capture opportunities across the U.S. and Europe.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ELEVATES THREE EXECUTIVES TO CO-PRESIDENTS TO STRENGTHEN LEADERSHIP

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective from 22nd April 2026. The move aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as Carlyle refines its strategy in a challenging economic environment.The three co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s franchises, positioning the firm to respond more quickly to market dynamics. By concentrating authority around Schwartz, Carlyle Group seeks greater execution discipline and improved performance in the coming quarters.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL FOR $2.8 BILLION, EXPANDING WEALTH-MANAGEMENT PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. confirmed on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The deal will transfer majority ownership to Carlyle while MAI employees retain a meaningful minority stake and the firm will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, the chairman and chief executive.The transaction is designed to strengthen MAI's capital base and support growth in its financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities. As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, a scale Carlyle will leverage with its global resources to maintain continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a history of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. While some investors may see AI stocks offering greater upside, a report on undervalued AI stocks is available.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL IN $2.8 BILLION DEAL

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the registered investment adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, Carlyle will assume majority ownership, while existing stakeholders including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital will exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle previously invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which acquired the firm that year. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices across the United States and has a workforce of more than 700. Despite volatility in Carlyle's stock, the parent firm maintains solid fundamentals, with liquid assets exceeding its near-term obligations and a 15-year streak of dividend payments, currently yielding around 3%. Financial analyses rate Carlyle's overall health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, Chairman and CEO of MAI, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, which facilitated MAI's growth through more than 30 strategic acquisitions during its ownership, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

+ OAKTREE AND NORTH MILL CLOSE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization with North Mill Equipment Finance, known as OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026. The deal underscores investor appetite for high-quality equipment finance assets.The securitization pools assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal finalized in November 2025. The offering was rated from AAA to single A and drew peak subscriptions of about 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack with a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%. Truist Securities acted as lead structuring agent and Deutsche Bank served as co-manager.

+ OAKTREE TO HONOR ALL REDEMPTIONS ON ABOUT $7.7 BILLION PRIVATE CREDIT FUND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would honor all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion. Investors will be allowed to redeem up to 8.5% of net assets, or roughly $400 million, with Brookfield contributing about $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund, which targets retail investors, is set to repurchase 6.8% of its own shares, with Brookfield agreeing to buy an additional 1.7% from a single investor, according to a letter to fund holders. The firm is also reducing its quarterly dividend by two cents, bringing the total to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, the fund reported $1.8 billion in liquidity from cash and undrawn facilities. Oaktree has also been selling portions of publicly traded loans and bonds this year to mitigate exposure to software investments, reflecting a broader portfolio-management push during a volatile period. The deal comes amid rising scrutiny of private credit markets, where peers such as Blackstone have faced higher-than-usual repurchase requests and have had to adjust buyback terms. Blackstone's $82 billion private credit fund faced repurchase requests exceeding the typical 5% threshold, prompting the board to raise the buyback to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO)

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured a $1.25 billion investment from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc. The deal grants Apollo a 13% stake and was formalized on April 20, 2026 through the issuance of convertible preferred shares, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The funding values Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC at a post-money valuation of approximately $9.62 billion and aims to strengthen operations and expand market presence. Apollo Global Management, Inc., via Apollo Funds, brings strategic backing to the company as demand for surgical solutions grows, with analysts watching how the partnership could influence growth and shareholder value as the transaction proceeds through regulatory review

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) INVESTS $1.25 BILLION IN MCKESSON CORPORATION (NYSE:MCK) FOR 13% STAKE IN MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS DIVISION

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The investment supports McKesson's plan to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division and is expected to bolster operational efficiencies across the group. McKesson intends to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division, and the capital from Apollo is expected to bolster the strategic plan and fund initiatives across the business. The investment signals a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the company pursues the separation and optimization of its core operations. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating investors anticipate growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the deal as it unfolds and its impact on McKesson's separation plans.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT EXPANDS UK PRESENCE WITH GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a deal completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed. The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans. GLG is a national leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired over 5,000 homes. Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios, enabling GLG to leverage both Apollo capital and third-party partners to accelerate development of high-quality rental housing. Gatehouse Bank's divestiture will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN US$1.7 BILLION TRANSACTION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding the firm’s footprint in necessity-based retail centers across rapidly growing metropolitan areas in the United States. The deal supports Ares' strategy to diversify across geographies and asset types and align with its emphasis on private credit, real assets, and special situations. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares Management named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring the firm’s focus on strengthening credit and special situations capabilities in Asia. The Whitestone purchase broadens Ares’ exposure to essential retail real estate and supports its strategy to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types. Investors should heed risks including profit margin compression, high debt levels, and a dividend yield of 5.38% that is not fully supported by earnings

+ ARES MANAGEMENT TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN $1.7 BILLION ALL-CASH DEAL

The announcement, made on Apr. 10, 2026, confirms that certain Ares Real Estate funds will acquire all common shares and operating partnership units of Whitestone REIT in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. Whitestone’s portfolio comprises 56 convenience-focused retail properties totaling about 4.9 million square feet in Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. After the deal closes, Whitestone will be private and will cease trading on the NYSE. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman said, "We believe Whitestone has shown the value of high-return smaller spaces occupied by a well-diversified mix of tenants. Our investment strategy is designed to allow businesses to fuel connection and convenience within thriving, dynamic communities. We believe this transaction with Ares is a testament to the value that strategy has created for our business and, ultimately, for our shareholders." David Roth, global head of real estate strategy and growth at Ares Real Estate, added, "This transaction reflects our high conviction in New Economy real estate as today’s consumers are increasingly seeking convenient experiences for their grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness, and dining needs." Ares Management said it expects to support and expand Whitestone's portfolio to create value for communities and investors.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT RAISES $5.4 BILLION FOR VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE:ARES) has raised approximately $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, as of 9th April 2026. The fundraising underscores the firm's focus on opportunities in supply-constrained markets, with emphasis on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage assets that could rebound in the near term.The $5.4 billion package comprises about $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion aimed at Ares' U.S. value-add strategy. Both lines have already deployed or identified around $1.1 billion in initial investments, signaling a proactive stance toward potential growth sectors.Analysts are broadly constructive, with about 75% of coverage bullish and a consensus price target near $161, implying a potential upside of roughly 60%. In a related note, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital trimmed its price target for Ares from $140 to $112 and maintained a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions, asset-based credit issues, and market volatility. Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, also counts BlackRock among its significant backers, with a stake of about 7.12% as of the end of 2025. While Ares shows promise, some investors may find greater upside in certain AI stocks and other sectors in a rapidly shifting market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESUMES UAE FREIGHT OPERATIONS FOLLOWING SAFETY REVIEW

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed its freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. As of 28th April 2026, the carrier published its new schedule, which includes the resumption of freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly, starting from 26th April. In addition, freighter services to Sharjah (SHJ) will operate three times weekly beginning on 1st May, while daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with plans to expand freighter destinations to more than 50 by 1st May. The passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June.Shipment restrictions have also been lifted on certain urgent freight services to routes such as Victoria, Australia, with Qatar Airways Cargo offering products under new handling codes starting 1st May. The QRLIV codes cover commodities such as fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, while LHO codes cover the transport of live human organs and blood.Reservations remain accessible through the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, in addition to the standard sales channels. The airline says it remains committed to maintaining robust service levels as it ramps back up its operations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS REVEALS NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE TO ELEVATE BUSINESS CLASS

Qatar Airways has announced the upcoming Qsuite Next Generation, slated to launch later this year after delays in its Boeing 777-9 arrivals. The upgraded business-class product will first appear on the Airbus A350-1000, as of 23rd April 2026. Building on the long-running Qsuite introduced nearly a decade ago, the Next Generation preserves sliding doors, double beds, and configurable seating while integrating advanced technology and improved space management to appeal to business travelers seeking privacy and flexibility. The move underscores Qatar Airways' strategy to lead premium travel, leveraging Doha Hamad International Airport's reputation and its premium ecosystem, including dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and lounge access.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS NETWORK AS RESTORATIONS CONTINUE

Qatar Airways has been expanding its operations, with belly and freighter services increasing to and from Doha. As of April 20, 2026, the carrier has restored its passenger network to more than 150 destinations and expanded freighter coverage to over 50 locations worldwide.The network restoration is part of a broader plan to rebuild capacity in the air cargo market. While the network grows, some shipments remain embargoed; AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes are restricted, and live organs and blood are not accepted. Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternative handling codes, provided the Doha transit time does not exceed 24 hours. All other products remain bookable through standard channels, including the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, underscoring the airline's adaptability to rising demand and shifting logistics conditions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGF-YBS3-S2WN-K0N3-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

+ COOLEY LLP FACES A LEADERSHIP CHALLENGE AS LITIGATION PRACTICE EXPANDS

Cooley LLP's litigation practice is advancing into the spotlight as a finalist for The American Lawyer's General Litigation Department of the Year. The firm is reputed for excellence and is now negotiating what its partners describe as a 'test of leadership.' Led by Ian Shapiro, the team is addressing pivotal challenges in a rapidly evolving legal environment.A recent interview with Shapiro, joined by Michael Attanasio and Kathleen Hartnett, shed light on the firm’s strategic decision-making. They highlighted Cooley’s work representing Jenner & Block in its high-profile bid against the administration, underscoring the commitment to meaningful advocacy. The interview was published roughly 10 hours ago, offering a timely snapshot of the firm’s operations.In parallel with Jenner & Block matters, Cooley has taken on a range of pro bono matters that emphasize public service and social justice. This dedication has fueled growth for the firm over the last year. As the litigation group expands, Cooley intends to deploy its expertise to address complex legal challenges while upholding its core values.The expansion of Cooley’s litigation practice reflects a larger industry shift toward specialized services. This strategic move enhances Cooley's competitive standing and positions it for future opportunities. With the fiscal year ending soon, observers will monitor Cooley's next moves.During this unfolding 'test of leadership,' the legal community and investors will look to see how Cooley navigates these challenges. The firm’s proactive approach and commitment to excellence suggest it could emerge stronger in a crowded market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH ANNOUNCES PHILADELPHIA OFFICE LEADER FROM WITHIN

ReedSmith LLP has tapped an internal attorney to lead its Philadelphia office, signaling a strategic shift in a key market. The new leader has more than a decade with the firm and has contributed to notable cases and regional growth, with focus on client relationships and innovative solutions. Effective from 27th April 2026, the appointment reinforces Reed Smith's plan to bolster its local presence and maintain continuity across leadership, while expanding capabilities in the Philadelphia market.

+ REED SMITH ESTABLISHES FIRST BOSTON MANAGING PARTNER POSITION TO DEEPEN MARKET PRESENCE

ReedSmith LLP announced the appointment of its first Managing Partner in Boston, a move designed to intensify the firm's regional focus. The appointment is effective from 23rd April 2026. The Boston leadership will oversee local operations, with a mandate to deepen client relationships and drive growth across sectors in the Northeast. The firm said the decision reflects the region's rising demand for specialized legal services and its readiness to reinforce its presence with a dedicated leadership role. With extensive experience and a broad network, the new Managing Partner is expected to accelerate operational efficiency and strengthen Reed Smith's reputation in Boston, positioning the office as a key driver of the firm's long-term strategy.

+ REED SMITH WELCOMES MCDERMOTT FUND FINANCE EXPERT TO MIAMI OFFICE

ReedSmith LLP has added a fund finance veteran from McDermott Will & Emery to its Miami office, cementing its footing in the fund finance sector. The hire, announced after the firm’s stated push to expand in key markets, arrives as of 17th April 2026 and reflects a strategy to attract specialized talent to better serve clients in dynamic financial markets. The new recruit comes with a track record in structuring complex financing arrangements for financial institutions and private equity firms, a capability expected to benefit Reed Smith’s clients. The move supports Reed Smith’s plan to provide comprehensive legal services to fund managers and investors and aligns with Miami’s rising demand for fund finance advisory as the city strengthens its role as a regional financial hub.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ WILSON SONSINI WELCOMES NEW COO FROM GLOBAL FIRM TO DRIVE EFFICIENCY

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. has appointed a Chief Operating Officer from Baker McKenzie to bolster operations and support growth. The new leader's name has not been disclosed.The firm announced the move on 22-04-2026, signaling a strategic shift to streamline processes and advance technology integration and client relationship management.Wilson Sonsini is known for its focus on technology and innovation, serving high growth companies and venture capital clients. The incoming COO's experience at Baker McKenzie in running operational initiatives is expected to align with the firm's objectives and support expansion into biotechnology, cybersecurity, and intellectual property sectors.Industry analysts view the change positively, suggesting it could strengthen strategic direction and execution. Investors will likely monitor the impact on the firm's competitive positioning and performance in the coming years.

+ RAY THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES $125 MILLION SERIES B TO ACCELERATE RTX-015 AND RTX-021

Ray Therapeutics announced it closed a $125 million Series B that was upsized and oversubscribed, with Janus Henderson Investors leading and a group of new investors including Adage Capital Management, Franklin Templeton, Invus, and Marshall Wace.Existing backers such as 4BIO Capital, Deerfield Management, MRL Ventures Fund, Novo Holdings A/S, Norwest, and Platanus participated, with a leading law firm providing advisory support.The funds are earmarked to support late-stage development for RTx-015 targeting Retinitis Pigmentosa and RTx-021 for Stargardt Disease and Geographic Atrophy, as RayTx moves toward commercial readiness.The advisory team members named were Dan Koeppen, Kassandra Castillo, and Nimit Dhir, signaling the value placed on strategic partnerships.

+ PRO BONO LEGAL TEAM BACKS P-REC AGGREGATION FACILITY ADVANCING RURAL ELECTRIFICATION

On March 25, 2026, the African Development Bank Group and Nordic Development Fund unveiled an $11.3 million plan to pilot the P-REC Aggregation Facility, the first use of RECs as direct funding for a mini-grid portfolio in 14 African countries.Co-managed by Camco Clean Energy and Energy Peace Partners (EPP), the facility aims to sign long-term purchase agreements with mini-grid developers in Burundi, Ethiopia, and Nigeria, to deliver electricity access to about 856,000 people.A pro bono legal team, including Scott Zimmermann, Nadia Senter, and Ethan Miller, supported EPP through the transaction, illustrating the firm's commitment to innovative climate finance.By aggregating RECs, the initiative seeks a sustainable financial model supporting local developers and contributing to climate resilience and energy equity; the AFDB announcement is available.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

+ REGULATOR ALIGNS WITH TOP FIRMS AS CFTC CREATES INNOVATION TASK FORCE

The CFTC has formed an Innovation Task Force with Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to strengthen the regulatory framework for derivatives markets as technology reshapes the sector.The 11th April 2026 announcement notes the collaboration aims to incorporate insights from both firms to reflect evolving market realities, including digital assets and new trading technologies.Sidley Austin will provide regulatory guidance while Latham & Watkins contributes practical policy experience, helping the commission evaluate how tools like blockchain and AI intersect with oversight objectives.The task force is part of a wider effort to balance prudent supervision with fostering innovation, as the U.S. seeks to maintain leadership in global financial markets.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LONDON REAL ESTATE FINANCE PRACTICE HITS £5 BILLION MILESTONE AT GREENBERG TRAURIG

Greenberg Traurig's London Real Estate Finance team surpassed £5 billion in transaction value across asset classes and financing structures as of 23 April 2026. Promotions within the team include William Lo to shareholder and Priyanka Verma to senior associate, increasing the group to four shareholders and nine attorneys. The team is shortlisted for Real Estate Team of the Year at The Lawyer Awards 2026 for advising on the £1.1 billion take-private of The PRS REIT plc, and is also in contention for Lawyer of the Year at the PROPS Awards on 14 May 2026. Duncan Hubbard, chair of the London Real Estate Finance team, highlighted the market's opportunities and the trust clients place in the team, while Greenberg Traurig's global platform of about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations supports its growth.

+ CHAMBERS SHORTLISTS GREENBERG TRAURIG AS DUAL ENVIRONMENT AND REAL ESTATE LAW FIRM OF THE YEAR CONTENDER

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Environmental and Real Estate practices have been shortlisted by Chambers for the 2026 Environment Law Firm of the Year and Real Estate Law Firm of the Year awards, Chambers announced on 23rd April 2026. The winners will be revealed on 11th June 2026 at The Plaza Hotel in New York.The firm is among six finalists in each category. The Chambers awards are highly regarded, recognizing outstanding legal work, strategic growth, and client service, based on research by more than 250 analysts.With about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations worldwide, Greenberg Traurig's cross-border capabilities support innovative and strategic legal services. The firm was named a 2025 BTI Best of the Best Recommended Law Firm for trust and relationship management.

+ MEXICO CITY UNIT OF GREENBERG TRAURIG EARNS COMPETITION AND ANTITRUST FIRM OF THE YEAR

Apr. 23, 2026: Greenberg Traurig’s Mexico City office has earned the Competition and Antitrust Firm of the Year award at the Legal 500 Mexico Awards 2026, announced in Mexico City. The recognition stems from Legal 500’s independent research for its annual directory. Jose Raz Guzman, co-chair of the Latin America Practice and co-managing shareholder in Mexico City, called the award a testament to the firm’s leadership and technical depth. The Antitrust Litigation & Competition Regulation Practice is led by Miguel Flores Bernes, Victor Manuel Frias and Rocio Olea Salgado, with support from associates Valery Garcia Zavala, Ana Ramirez, Natalia Mejia and Martin Lopez, and is known for handling mergers, acquisitions and joint ventures and for managing cross-border merger filings. Greenberg Traurig also helps clients with compliance, internal investigations and competition authority proceedings, while the Mexico City office continues to grow as a cornerstone of the Latin America Practice.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FBT GIBBONS TAPS GOODWIN PROCTER VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN VC CAPABILITIES

FBT Gibbons is expanding its capabilities in venture capital by hiring from Goodwin Procter LLP for its New Jersey office. On 21st April 2026, the firm confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney. The attorney comes with extensive venture capital experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a leader in the field, and the firm plans to leverage that expertise for clients seeking guidance on capital raises. Industry observers note the hire reflects a broader trend of law firms sharpening niche practices to compete in a growing market.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ENABLES CLOSURE OF $328 MILLION CONSUMER-FOCUSED FUND FOR TOPSPIN

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing a $328 million commitment for a consumer-focused fund. The deal underscores a growing trend in private equity toward consumer-oriented strategies, with Goodwin Procter providing crucial legal support throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s closure, designed to pursue opportunities in the consumer sector. The firm’s counsel was instrumental in navigating fundraising requirements, maintaining regulatory compliance, and simplifying investor workflows.The fund intends to target a broad spectrum of opportunities across consumer industries, emphasizing segments with strong growth trajectories. With Goodwin Procter’s guidance, Topspin Consumer Partners aims to back innovative ventures well positioned to compete in a dynamic market.This partnership reinforces Goodwin Procter’s leadership in private equity work and its dedication to client success, with potential implications for investor confidence as the fund progresses.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER MOVES TOWARD AI-DRIVEN TRANSFORMATION ACROSS ITS LEGAL SERVICES

Goodwin Procter LLP is pursuing an AI-native strategy, expanding its technology footprint to boost efficiency in its private equity and technology practices. The move aligns with industry momentum toward AI-driven legal services.On 10th April 2026, the firm announced plans to integrate AI into its practice, aligning with a wider industry trend of AI adoption across BigLaw.AI tools are expected to enhance research, document review and analytics in case management, helping attorneys focus on higher level work.The move underscores the legal sector's shift toward innovation as clients demand faster and more capable service.

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK EXPANDS ONLINE SAFETY CAPABILITIES WITH NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick has added Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a significant position at Meta, and on Apr. 28, 2026, she expressed enthusiasm for the new role.Her hiring comes as 25 state legislatures weigh measures to safeguard minors online, a shift Kudon calls a watershed moment. "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large," Kudon said.Aravind Swaminathan praised Lopez as a multifaceted asset—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and highlighted the Online Safety Law Center as a hub for guiding clients through rapid changes in the digital space.

+ FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.

+ ORRICK DRIVES CLO GROWTH IN LONDON WITH CADWALDER HIRES

Orrick has added two structured finance lawyers from Cadwalader to its London office, Suzanne Bell and William Bibby; Bell spent more than 13 years at Cadwalader, and Bibby will be elevated to partner after arriving from Linklaters a year ago, bringing the total Cadwalader hires to 42 since last year’s drive. The expansion that began in September 2025 formed a transatlantic CLO and asset-backed lending team across London and the US, totalling 37 lawyers, including 10 partners. In Q1 2026, Orrick ranked second among arranger counsel for CLO deals globally, with its London team advising on four European private credit CLOs. Leah Sanzari said the group’s performance has exceeded expectations as the firm pursues innovative financial products.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R49F-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT NEWS OF FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT NEWS OF FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION INVESTS IN WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI CHALLENGES

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking a proactive stance against the impending challenges posed by artificial intelligence on the workforce, committing significant funding to a national initiative aimed at enhancing workforce resilience. On 28th April 2026, the century-old philanthropic institution announced its strategy to address the disruption caused by automation, focusing on preventing job displacement and helping workers transition into AI-complementary roles.The initiative is designed as a grant-based program that prioritizes workforce interventions over product development. The Rockefeller Foundation's approach emphasizes the importance of program design, metrics, and evaluation. Central to this effort will be the measurement of displacement risk, deployment outcomes, and labor-market transitions, which are crucial for understanding the impact of automation on employment.Funding for the initiative is expected to come through various instruments, including grants, programmatic pilots, and partnerships with employers, training providers, and local governments. The choices made during the implementation phase will be critical in determining whether the Rockefeller Foundation's funding leads to scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects with limited long-term impact.This move by the Rockefeller Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative. By investing at this scale, the foundation aims to catalyze innovative models for retraining and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as universal basic income, sectoral training, and apprenticeship programs. This initiative is poised to increase the demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.As the Rockefeller Foundation rolls out this substantial program, stakeholders will be keenly observing its funding priorities, evaluation criteria, and partnership dynamics. Key considerations will include whether the initiative emphasizes durable credentialing, employer engagement, or short-term placement metrics. Ultimately, the decisions made now will be pivotal in determining whether this initiative can effectively influence labor-market outcomes or become primarily a source of funding for pilot programs.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION CHAMPIONS ECONOMIC PATHWAYS AT BALTIMORE EVENT

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking significant strides to address economic challenges in the United States, hosting the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event that gathered over 250 leaders from various sectors. On 21st April 2026, the Foundation convened policymakers, philanthropists, and private-sector representatives in Baltimore to discuss and develop transformative solutions aimed at revitalizing the economy and fostering opportunity across communities.Key among the announcements made at the event, the Rockefeller Foundation launched a three-year, $100 million initiative designed to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America’s most distressed communities. This effort aims to create approximately 1.6 million additional good jobs across sectors with the strongest growth potential, including healthcare, energy transition, food systems, and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, President of the Rockefeller Foundation, emphasized the importance of restoring the promise of a stable life through hard work, underscoring the Foundation's commitment to the resilience of American workers.In addition to the job initiative, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a supplementary $12 million support for its 'Invest in Our Future' program, which focuses on mobilizing clean energy opportunities and fostering economic growth in underserved communities. Maryland Governor Wes Moore, who participated in the event, declared $1.5 million in philanthropic awards aimed at enhancing education and childcare in areas of high childhood poverty, further demonstrating the collaborative efforts being made to address systemic issues.The event also saw the unveiling of the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, consisting of ten innovators dedicated to expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas. Moreover, the Rockefeller Foundation's collaboration with various partners, including the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator, highlighted the importance of cross-sector partnerships in tackling climate and technology challenges, as well as enhancing community resilience and economic opportunities.Looking ahead, the next 'Big Bets for America' event is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026, promising to further the dialogue and action initiated in Baltimore. The Rockefeller Foundation's ongoing commitment to fostering economic growth and opportunity reflects a strategic approach to addressing the pressing economic challenges faced by communities nationwide.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S SIMON WINTER ADVOCATES FOR UNIFIED DATA AT ONE HEALTH SUMMIT

Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, emphasized the urgent need for data unification at the One Health Summit. Speaking on April 21st, 2026, Winter addressed the growing challenge of fragmented data systems across humanitarian, nutritional, and climate sectors, which he believes hinders effective decision-making and resource allocation.During the panel discussion titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children," Winter articulated that despite having access to unprecedented amounts of data, it remains trapped in silos. He noted, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." His remarks reflect a broader concern about the inefficiencies within various data systems that serve humanitarian and environmental needs.Winter's perspective resonates with earlier comments made by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025. Ellison urged for a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations, asserting that the fragmented nature of data—scattered across approximately 3,000 databases—limits its utility for AI and decision-making. The Rockefeller Foundation's call for data integration mirrors this sentiment, advocating for collaboration among local and international stakeholders.To illustrate the potential benefits of unified data, Winter cited the ongoing drought crisis in Somalia. The Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator to create a food and nutrition systems dashboard that integrates various data sets. This tool aims to provide timely information to local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually to support local farmers and pastoralists.Winter concluded his address by emphasizing the necessity of empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, which can be combined with local information. This approach, he believes, could lead to a more effective and cost-efficient solution to the complex challenges posed by fragmented data systems, ultimately enhancing humanitarian responses in crisis situations.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION RECOGNIZED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO HUMAN RIGHTS IN NIGERIA

The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has honored the Ford Foundation for its sustained contributions to advancing human rights in Nigeria, reinforcing a partnership that has played a critical role in strengthening access to justice and institutional capacity. On 28th April 2026, during a ceremony in Abuja, the NHRC presented an Award of Appreciation to the Foundation in recognition of its longstanding support.The award was received by the Foundation’s Regional Director for West Africa, ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, during a high-level engagement with directors and senior officials of the Commission. Speaking at the event, the Executive Secretary of the NHRC, Dr. Tony Ojukwu, commended the Ford Foundation’s consistent backing, particularly in initiatives aimed at expanding access to justice and strengthening the Commission’s operational capacity.Dr. Ojukwu emphasized that given the breadth and complexity of human rights challenges in Nigeria, the Commission cannot function in isolation. He stated that sustained collaboration with development partners remains essential to transforming human rights protection from policy intentions into tangible outcomes for citizens.In her remarks, Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as both significant and encouraging, reaffirming the Foundation’s commitment to promoting human rights. She noted that a key focus of the Foundation’s work is ensuring that citizens are not only aware of their rights but are also supported in seeking justice when those rights are violated. However, she emphasized that awareness alone is insufficient without strong and effective enforcement systems.The engagement underscored the importance of continued cooperation between the Commission and its partners in addressing evolving human rights challenges across the country. In a vote of thanks, Senior Human Rights Adviser to the Commission, Hilary Ogbonna, expressed appreciation for the Ford Foundation’s enduring partnership, highlighting key institutional advancements, including initiatives such as the MOVE project and the Commission’s dashboard system, which benefitted from the Foundation’s early support.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO ADDRESS GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE

The Ford Foundation has called on First Ladies across the ECOWAS region to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence (GBV). The Foundation described the First Ladies’ 'soft power' as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. This call was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, while delivering a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies’ Forum on Zero Tolerance for Gender-Based Violence, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, she noted, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of Gross Domestic Product in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose 'Hands Off Our Girls' campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how 'soft power,' when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.The Ford Foundation has continued to invest in strengthening GBV prevention and response systems across West Africa, including partnerships involving First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria. These interventions have aimed to advance advocacy efforts, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu emphasized that laws and policies are insufficient without public trust and accessibility, pointing out the persistent silence among survivors driven by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.In light of recent regional initiatives such as the GBV Roadmap (2026–2028), Legal Scorecard, and Joint Communiqu , Aniagolu urged First Ladies to move beyond mere commitments by promoting safe reporting, challenging harmful norms, and strengthening accountability systems. 'No girl should grow up believing silence is her only option, and no woman should be denied justice because of where she lives,' she asserted, reaffirming the Ford Foundation’s commitment to supporting advocacy, strengthening systems, and amplifying women’s voices across the region.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ADVOCATES FOR WOMEN-CENTRIC JUSTICE REFORMS IN NIGERIA

In a recent dialogue organized by the Ford Foundation, stakeholders and gender advocates emphasized the urgent need for a comprehensive redesign of Nigeria's justice system to better serve women and girls. The event, held on the sidelines of CSW70 in New York, took place on the 16th of April, 2026, and focused on the effectiveness of the nation’s plural justice system.The discussion extended beyond legal frameworks to address the lived realities of survivors, highlighting that justice is only achievable when legal literacy is enhanced and economic and cultural barriers that silence women are dismantled. Nigeria's justice landscape is shaped by statutory, customary, and religious systems, which ideally should work in harmony. However, in practice, many women fall through the gaps of these systems, often facing costly and protracted legal processes.His Royal Majesty, Obi Benjamin Ikenchukwu Keagboruzi, the Dein of Agbor, shared insights on the practical obstacles hindering women's access to formal justice. He pointed out that court fees, legal representation, and lost work hours cumulatively push justice further out of reach for many women. As a result, women often resort to more accessible customary systems, which may not provide adequate protection.Justice Bukunola Adebiyi of the Lagos State High Court echoed similar sentiments, stressing the importance of strengthening investigative and evidentiary processes to ensure justice is delivered effectively. The dialogue also featured poignant reflections from Ngozi Valentina Enih, Commissioner for Children, Gender Affairs, and Social Development in Enugu State, who, as a survivor herself, highlighted the devastating trade-offs families face when seeking justice.Elsa Stamatopoulou, an Adjunct Professor of Anthropology, offered a nuanced perspective on cultural barriers, suggesting they can also serve as resources for empowerment. Moderator Professor Joy Ezeilo, Executive Director of the Women’s Aid Collective (WACOL), concluded that Nigeria's plural legal system presents not a weakness but an opportunity for comprehensive reforms. Dr. ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, Regional Director for West Africa at the Ford Foundation, reiterated that genuine progress will come from understanding and addressing the realities faced by women and girls in Nigeria.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
