Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGF-YBS3-S2WN-K0J1-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in DowntownLos Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TIES WITH ALTERRA TO EXPAND CLIMATE TRANSITION FUNDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Ties With ALTERRA to Expand Climate Transition FundingKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) partners with ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to support its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on Apr. 22, 2026. ALTERRA launched at COP28 and aims to mobilize up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed to developing markets. ALTERRA already counts BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG as partners.KKR’s climate strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. KKR manages over $100 billion in assets and has invested more than $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable investments include Zenobe, EGC, Avantus and IGNIS P2X. The collaboration with ALTERRA could help scale technologies and improve energy security and decarbonization.Gailliot and Lagarrigue see strong potential to combine ALTERRA’s reach with KKR’s infrastructure know-how to advance decarbonization across sectors.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) FUNDS STARITAS AS IT BECOMES STANDALONE GROWTH VEHICLE

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through Accel-KKR to support the spin-out from ECR as it pursues growth opportunities in digital technology markets. Staritas will operate as an independent company, receiving resources and guidance from Accel-KKR and KKR to accelerate development and market entry in the digital space. The move highlights investor interest in technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly and scale within competitive markets.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending within KKR's real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as the firm scales its real estate credit platform. Traut has led the unit since 2015, growing it from a seed investment of $400 million to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025.Traut's leadership comes as KKR invests nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe and expands its European real estate credit business with support from Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.Paul Fine is named head of originations, succeeding Traut. Fine, a former managing director, will work to deepen client relationships and widen KKR's capital pools. Since 2017 the firm has expanded its capital pools from two to eight, including the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

+ OAKTREE NAVIGATES PRIVATE CREDIT PRESSURES BY FULFILLING $7.7B REDEMPTIONS AND CUTTING DIVIDEND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would fulfill all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion, allowing withdrawals up to 8.5% of net assets.Brookfield will contribute roughly $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund will repurchase 6.8% of its shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor; the fund's dividend will be cut by two cents to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, liquidity stood at $1.8 billion from cash and undrawn facilities, while the firm has sold publicly traded loans and bonds to reduce exposure to software investments. The broader private credit sector remains under pressure, with peers such as Blackstone raising buyback terms to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

+ WHITESTONE REIT TO BECOME PRIVATE AS ARES COMPLETES $1.7B ACQUISITION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced plans to acquire Whitestone REIT in an all-cash deal valued at about $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The transaction will render Whitestone private and remove it from the NYSE. Whitestone’s 56 properties total around 4.9 million square feet across Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. The move aims to broaden Ares Real Estate’s footprint with necessity-based retail centers in high-demand Arizona and Texas markets. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman stated the company’s strategy centers on high-return, smaller spaces with a diversified tenant base, adding that the agreement with Ares validates this approach for shareholders. Ares’ global head of real estate strategy and growth, David Roth, emphasized the appeal of New Economy real estate and convenient consumer experiences, highlighting the growing demand for grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness and dining options.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT DEPLOYS $5.4 BILLION TO ACCELERATE VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation has secured about $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, a move aimed at capitalizing on supply-constrained markets likely to rebound soon, with a focus on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage sectors.The fundraising includes $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion for the U.S. value-add program, with initial investments identified or deployed totaling around $1.1 billion on each track.Analysts are broadly supportive; about 75% are bullish, and the consensus price target sits near $161, suggesting roughly 60% upside. However, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital reduced its target to $112 from $140 and issued a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions and broader market volatility.Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, had BlackRock as a notable backer with approximately 7.12% stake at end-2025, underscoring strong institutional support. The note also reminds investors that AI-related equities may offer greater upside in some scenarios, reinforcing the case for diversification.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESTARTS FREIGHTER AND PASSENGER LINKS TO UAE ON EXPANDED NETWORK

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed both freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after safety concerns. The new schedule confirms freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly from 26th April and to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly from 1st May, while daily passenger services to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with freighter reach planned to exceed 50 destinations by 1st May and the overall passenger network expected to top 150 by 16th June.Urgent freight restrictions have been lifted on routes such as Victoria, Australia, with the carrier introducing special handling codes QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, and LHO for live human organs and blood, effective 1st May.Reservations remain accessible via the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, alongside standard sales channels. The resumption is described as a key milestone for regional logistics and supports ongoing expansion.Industry stakeholders are watching the pace of growth, as the UAE freight market benefits from improved connectivity and service levels.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INTRODUCES NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE, AIMING TO SUSTAIN PREMIUM EDGE

Qatar Airways announced the Qsuite Next Generation, with a launch planned later this year on the Airbus A350-1000 after delays with the 777-9, as of 23rd April 2026. The Next Generation builds on the iconic Qsuite introduced in 2017, maintaining sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating while adding a movable 4K OLED screen and stronger privacy options. The initiative reinforces Qatar Airways' premium strategy and its Doha hub, where exclusive check-in, fast-track security, and premium lounges contribute to a seamless travel experience. By investing in product development, the airline seeks to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure customers as competition in luxury cabins intensifies.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS SETS OUT PHASED EMBARGO LIFTS DURING RECOVERY

Qatar Airways is steadily expanding its operations as it rebuilds its network, with passenger routes restored to more than 150 destinations and freighter coverage over 50 locations as of April 20, 2026.The restoration is part of a wider plan to increase capacity amid rising air freight demand. Some shipments remain embargoed, with AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes restricted, and live organs and blood not accepted.QRLIV shipments can be booked under different codes, as long as Doha transit time stays within 24 hours. Other products remain bookable via standard sales channels, and the carrier emphasizes adaptability and a commitment to service as it expands.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-B2C3-S487-92MG-00000-00The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:47ZLexisNexisBEYOND ST. LAWRENCE COUNTYThe Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimatedJUANPABLO RAMIREZ-FRANCO FOR GRIST

The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?

There are no wind turbines on the Great Lakes and it isn't for lack of wind.

This titanic network of interconnected freshwater lakes with a surface area larger than New England, New York, and New Jersey combined is theoretically ideal for wind farms. Winds sweeping across the lakes are stronger, more consistent, and less turbulent than those over land. The National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimated that the Great Lakes states have enough offshore wind potential to generate more than three times their combined annual electricity consumption.

Plus, unlike proposed wind farms along the East Coast, where the federal government controls the seabed, Great Lakes states have jurisdiction over their lakebeds, Grist reports. While projects would still face federal oversight, some proponents see wind development in the Great Lakes as a potential workaround to President Trump's attacks on the industry.

"If it's done correctly and we're able to harness even a fraction of that, we could offset a lot of electricity demand," said Melissa Scanlan, director of the Center for Water Policy at the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee.

Still, the vast inland lakes remain untapped largely due to a lack of streamlined permitting processes at the state level and economic hurdles. Offshore wind is expensive, and unlike the East Coast, where a mature wind industry has built specialized ships and ports to build turbines in the sea, the Great Lakes states lack the infrastructure. Ultimately, renewable energy experts say the region doesn't have the legal framework or technical capacity to support such massive projects.

So far, offshore wind power which involves installing turbines in large bodies of water, typically the ocean in the United States, has been concentrated along the Eastern seaboard. Projects are still very rare: Just three are currently operating, with several more under construction, all along the East Coast.

More than a decade ago, President Barack Obama's push for renewable energy buoyed a short-lived boom in offshore wind projects particularly in the Great Lakes. The New York Power Authority and cities such as Toronto explored boosting their energy supply with wind power from Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. However, the initial boost only propelled one project forward: Ohio's Icebreaker Wind in Lake Erie. After clashing with Ohio regulators over whether turbines could run through the night and battling a court case filed by local residents over impacts to bats and migratory birds, the developers eventually ran out of money and called the project off in 2023.

Building renewable energy offshore in federal waters has now become harder than ever. On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed a memorandum shutting down all offshore wind permitting, effectively suspending five large-scale East Coast projects. Although courts later overturned those cancellations, the uncertainty around offshore wind development under the Trump administration has persisted. And since any project on the Great Lakes would still need federal permits and reviews under the Clean Air Act and the Endangered Species Act, among others, wind developers have largely backed off their plans.

"That told us that this was not the time to reintroduce floating offshore wind in Illinois," said Jim Lanard, co-founder of Magellan Wind, an offshore wind developer focused on the Great Lakes. "Rather, we needed to face the existential threat to the industry."

Lanard said the regulatory uncertainty means it's a bad time for business. His company, which in the past has submitted comments to the Illinois power regulators related to the deployment of offshore wind on Lake Michigan, has temporarily paused pursuing work on the Great Lakes.

"Governors and legislatures are so focused on trying to figure out how to run their governments with the reduced support coming from WashingtonD.C.," Lanard said. "It's not time for them to add additional burdens onto their staff, who don't know how they're going to balance their budgets."

Though Illinois' jurisdiction over part of Lake Michigan presents some opportunities to work around Trump, Lanard said developers are not going to come to Illinois until there is a federal policy to protect the industry from whiplash elections. Moreover, Illinois and the other Great Lakes states have yet to establish a substantial legal framework to make local offshore wind farms a reality. Given these challenges, he estimated it will be some time before there are any wind farms on the Great Lakes.

"I'm looking at five to seven years from 2029," Lanard said.

For years, state Representative Marcus Evans Jr. has championed a plan to make Illinois' shores home to the first offshore wind farm in the Great Lakes. The legislation, known as the Illinois Rust Belt to Green Belt Pilot Program Act, was designed to direct state planners to solicit proposals for a new utility-scale offshore wind farm and begin purchasing offshore wind power. First introduced by Evans in 2022, the measure has long failed to pick up enough momentum to break out of the statehouse. In 2025, Illinois Senator Robert Peters refiled the bill, but it didn't receive a committee assignment. Evans said he decided against refiling the bill this year.

"I didn't have the support," said Evans, a South Side Chicago Democrat. "I used my energy this year to focus on some other items, but it's still something that I think is important," he added.

Since the bill would require a localized industry to build, transport, and upkeep the turbines, Evans saw the push as a huge boon for the region's clean energy workforce one that the state of Illinois has long been trying to prop up.

Illinois passed the Climate and Equitable Jobs Act in 2021, which committed the state to investing $80 million annually to build out workforce training hubs focused on clean energy-related jobs. The same bill also put the state on track to transition its energy sector to 100 percent clean energy by 2050. Evans had hoped his initiative could help achieve both goals at once.

He is still optimistic that Lake Michigan will be next.

"I will file [a bill] in the future," he said.

This story is a partnership between Grist and WBEZ, a public radio station serving the Chicago metropolitan region.

This story was produced by Grist and reviewed and distributed by Stacker.

value.Jurisdiction: International; New York
value.Title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-JWP3-RTMT-24H2-00000-00US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisUS NEWSVersion:1US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyerGaya Gupta

Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials

The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports.

In an undisclosed April meeting at the justice department, Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J Giuffra Jr, said that Adani would invest $10bn in the US economy and create 15,000 jobs if prosecutors dropped the charges against him, according to the New York Times and Bloomberg.

Guiffra also reportedly had a presentation of 100 slides that explained why prosecutors lacked evidence and jurisdiction over the case, according to the Times.

Prosecutors said that the potential investment would not sway the outcome of the case, but the offer received favorable response from one senior justice department official, the outlets reported.

Adani was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials and misleading American and international investors with false statements. He and two other executives of an Indian renewable energy company were indicted in New York and charged with multiple counts of fraud in November 2024.

At the time, the justice department said that in addition to paying $250m in bribes, Adani also schemed “to lie to investors and banks to raise billions of dollars, and to obstruct justice”.

“These offenses were allegedly committed by senior executives and directors to obtain and finance massive state energy supply contracts through corruption and fraud at the expense of US investors,” said Lisa Miller, the deputy assistant attorney general at the time.

Adani is considered one of the wealthiest and most powerful men in the world as the founder of Adani Group, a conglomerate that operated India’s ports, built coal-fired power stations and opened coalmines in the country. According to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index , he is the richest man in Asia, with an estimated net worth of $104bn.

He holds close ties to the country’s nationalist prime minister, Narendra Modi, whose party members granted the Adani group contracts that allowed the group to monopolize and expand. This resulted in allegations of favoritism and “crony capitalism”, which the Adani Group has denied. Journalists who tried to investigate the company were often harassed and charged.

Giuffra is the co-chair of Sullivan & Cromwell, a powerful New York law firm. Shortly before Trump started his second term, Giuffra agreed to represent Trump in the appeals of two personal cases against him, including the Stormy Daniels hush-money case.

The justice department and Sullivan & Cromwell did not immediately reply to a request for comment.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-2HJ3-SDM5-R3NC-00000-00MILBANK LLP - RECENT HIGHLIGHTS OF THE INDUSTRY AND THEIR PEERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - RECENT HIGHLIGHTS OF THE INDUSTRY AND THEIR PEERS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK ATTORNEYS CONTRIBUTE TO PROJECT FINANCE 2026 INTERNATIONAL COMPARATIVE LEGAL GUIDE

Milbank LLP's London-based attorneys contributed two chapters to Project Finance 2026 (15th Edition), an International Comparative Legal Guide published by Global Legal Group.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK GLOBAL RISK & NATIONAL SECURITY ATTORNEYS DISCUSS NATIONAL SECURITY RISK ENVIRONMENT AND RELATED LEGAL MATTERS AT CYBER-AI EVENT

On May 5, 2026, Milbank LLP Global Risk & National Security partners John Beahn and Bijan Ganji and special counsel Pinky Mehta delivered remarks as panelists at a cybersecurity and artificial intelligence (AI) event held in Washington, DC for leading professionals from the cybersecurity, biotech, finance and defense sectors.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK SPECIAL COUNSEL KELLY GARCIA TO SPEAK AT IBAS 2026 ALGORITHMIC PRICING WEBINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration special counsel Kelly Dodge Garcia will speak at the International Bar Association (IBA) webinar on algorithmic pricing and competition law on May 13.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED ELLIOTT IN CONNECTION WITH ITS INVESTMENT IN STRATOLAUNCH

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ PROMINENT INTERNATIONAL WHITE COLLAR LAWYER JOINS LATHAM & WATKINS IN CHICAGO

Former federal prosecutor Ryan Rohlfsen brings deep experience in global investigations, compliance, and crisis management

CHICAGO, May 12, 2026 -- Latham & Watkins LLP1 is pleased to announce that Ryan Rohlfsen has joined the firm's Chicago office as a partner in the White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and member of the Litigation & Trial Department. Rohlfsen advises multinational companies, boards, and senior executives in high-stakes government-facing civil and criminal white collar matters, internal investigations, litigation, and related crisis management. He has represented clients in matters on six continents and in all major global markets.

Continue Reading

Ryan Rohlfsen, Partner, Latham & Watkins LLP

"We are thrilled to welcome Ryan to Latham," said Mary Rose Alexander, Managing Partner of the Chicago office. "Ryan is a highly regarded international white collar lawyer with extensive experience helping clients manage risk, navigate enforcement challenges, and respond to sensitive matters. He will be an important resource for clients in the United States and around the world."

Rohlfsen has particular experience advising clients on international and domestic corruption matters, securities and accounting fraud, healthcare fraud, money laundering, sanctions and export controls, data privacy, and state consumer protection matters. He routinely guides public and private companies through complex and fast moving government-facing investigations, and has secured numerous declinations. Beyond advising companies and individuals in crisis, Rohlfsen also counsels global companies on building and strengthening sophisticated compliance programs.

His experience includes serving as a federal prosecutor in the US Department of Justice's Criminal Fraud Section in Washington, D.C. and Chicago. At DOJ, Rohlfsen led complex securities fraud, healthcare fraud, and domestic corruption matters, and served in the Department's specialized unit responsible exclusively for corporate criminal enforcement of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA).

"With over two decades of experience around the world, including as a former senior US federal prosecutor and trial lawyer, Ryan brings exceptional credentials and deep investigations, compliance, and government enforcement defense expertise to Latham," said Terra Reynolds, Global Vice Chair of Latham's White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and Healthcare & Life Sciences Industry Group.

"Ryan's judgment, litigation experience, and deep understanding of complex cross-border investigations and corporate fraud make him a strong addition to the firm," said Sean Berkowitz, a member of Latham's Executive Committee. "He has the kind of practical perspective clients value when the stakes are high, and we are excited to welcome him to the team."

+ SIMPSON THACHER SECURES REVERSAL FOR NORTH AMERICAN FIRE IN DELAWARE SUPREME COURT

On February 3, 2026, Simpson Thacher secured a victory for North American Fire Ultimate Holdings LP ("North American Fire") when the Delaware Supreme Court reversed and remanded a Court of Chancery judgement that had dismissed North American Fire's complaint against a former employee for violating restrictive covenants found in an Incentive Unit Grant Agreement. In its order, the Court adopted the Firm's position that consideration is measured at the time of contract formation, not at the time of enforcement. Applying Newell Rubbermaid Inc. v. Storm, the Court held that while the value of the Incentive Units was "somewhat contingent," it was not illusory at the time of formation and therefore constituted adequate consideration to support the Agreement.

The Simpson Thacher team included Michael Garvey, Alison Sher, Ryerson Symons, Nicole Palmadesso and Sarah Heintz.

Source: Company Website

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW PACT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. Fortress will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The revelation came on 5th May 2026.The arrangement deepens a partnership that began in 2025. It underscores Upstart’s push to diversify its capital base, now drawing on more than 100 banks and credit unions that use its AI-driven models and cloud applications. Upstart’s platform supports a range of products, including personal loans, automotive loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit option.

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) AND HA SUSTAINABLE INFRASTRUCTURE CAPITAL (HASI) RAISE $508 MILLION FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1 SENIOR NOTES

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through Private Offering of Senior Unsecured Notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1) as of 11th May 2026. The proceeds will be deployed to acquire or invest in both new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States. CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, renewable natural gas, and transportation infrastructure projects.The platform began with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and in December 2025 the firms added another $500 million. The current round marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million offering completed in June 2025, and lifts the platform’s total investment capacity toward $5 billion. Cecilio Velasco, Managing Director at KKR, said the strong investor reception underscores the quality and diversity of CCH1’s asset base, and noted that with over $4 billion of investment capacity the platform is well-positioned to meet rising demand for sustainable energy infrastructure. Dan McMahon, Senior Managing Director of Syndications at HASI, welcomed the expansion and highlighted CCH1's role in accelerating sustainable infrastructure investment. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities served as Joint Lead Placement Agents for the transaction

KKR will redeploy capital toward higher-return opportunities, including private credit, as part of its portfolio optimization strategy.Pears Global Real Estate, known for strategic acquisitions in high-value markets, plans to leverage its expertise to enhance the acquired properties, supporting its European expansion during a period of rising urbanization and housing demand. + CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI EXPAND PRIVATE MARKETS PARTNERSHIP TO BROADEN ACCESS FOR WEALTH AND RETIREMENT INVESTORS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) have expanded their partnership to develop private market investment solutions aimed at wealth and retirement investors as demand for alternative assets continues to grow across advisory and retirement channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the firms said their enhanced collaboration would focus on broadening access to institutional-style private market strategies, which include model portfolios and defined contribution retirement offerings. This builds on a multi-year relationship centered on fund administration and technology services, with Carlyle’s private markets platform integrated with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing capabilities to deliver tailored solutions for navigating private market investments. Michael Lane, head of asset management at SEI, said, "One of the most common questions we hear from clients is how to allocate to private markets. Our objective with Carlyle is to help simplify that decision by providing more streamlined access to a broader range of strategies." Jeff Nedelman, Carlyle’s co-president and global head of client business, added, "This partnership reflects the increasing role of private markets across the wealth and retirement landscape." As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported approximately $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, while Carlyle managed $477 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. The expanded collaboration is expected to enhance both firms’ capabilities in delivering sought-after private market investment solutions.

(NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating two healthcare service providers, Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The combined platform is expected to support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers with tailored operational assistance.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL BOOSTS INTER MILAN WITH €5 MILLION CASH INJECTION

Oaktree Capital has injected €5 million into Inter Milan, bringing total cash infused into the club’s equity to €52 million, per a semiannual statement dated 11th May 2026.Since taking ownership nearly a year ago after acquiring Inter from Suning following a loan default, Oaktree has pursued a blend of stability and growth, keeping CEO Giuseppe Marotta in place.The injection supports plans to retain the core of last season’s Serie A title-winning squad while pursuing younger talents with resale value and advancing work on a new stadium to replace San Siro. It also follows a similar €5 million tranche in December, underscoring Oaktree’s ongoing commitment to Inter’s financial health and strategic aims.

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS SECURES $300 MILLION SENIOR LOAN FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL TO RESTRUCTURE FINANCING

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility with Oaktree Capital Management, a move that is set to reshape the company's capital structure. The financing refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives, with the deal completed on 3rd May 2026.The loan features a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure, flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, strengthening the company's funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as it pursues expansion.Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, implying growth of roughly 25-26% year over year. The loan alleviates refinancing pressure and provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without requiring an immediate equity raise, though investors should weigh ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts project about 30% annual revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a path that could support or temper profitability expectations.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) EXPANDS FRESH FOOD FOOTPRINT WITH PROSOL GROUP STAKE

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Closes Majority Stake In Prosol Group To Expand France Fresh Food FootprintThe transaction closed on May 7, 2026, and was financed through Apollo-managed funds, with Ardian as the seller. Prosol Group operates nearly 450 stores across France, primarily under the Grand Frais and fresh banners, and maintains a vertically integrated supply chain focused on quality and freshness. Jean-Paul Mochet will continue as Chief Executive Officer, guiding the company’s growth under Apollo’s ownership.Apollo, which manages about $1.03 trillion in assets, said the investment will leverage Prosol’s established supplier network and customer base to accelerate expansion in its French fresh food platform. Financial advisors UBS AG, together with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard, supported the transaction.While the investment presents growth opportunities, Apollo notes sector risks such as supply chain disruptions and shifting consumer preferences that could affect returns. The terms of the deal were not disclosed, leaving valuation details for disclosure at a later stage

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS DAILY PRICING PILOT FOR PRIVATE ASSETS

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is launching a pilot to introduce daily pricing for its private asset portfolios as it reports that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive, cited record fee-related earnings as contributing to the growth. The firm said total assets under management stood at $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent year over year, with first-quarter inflows of $115 billion and inflows of $300 billion over the past twelve months. The asset management business generated $95 billion in inflows, helped by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, along with institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. The retirement services business added $20 billion in inflows. Apollo also noted the Financial Stability Board report on vulnerabilities in private credit issued the same day and defended its approach, emphasizing prudent underwriting. The company described a pilot launched last year to produce estimated daily values for its investment-grade fixed income products, with daily pricing for corporate investment-grade fixed income assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Rowan said robust processes support transparency as public markets adjust and private markets follow. Looking ahead, Apollo targets 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE for 2026, supported by a strong origination pipeline and disciplined capital management.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS ANNOUNCES NEW CARACAS AND BOGOTA ROUTES, EXPANDING ITS SOUTH AMERICAN FOOTPRINT

Qatar Airways announced new scheduled flights to Caracas, Venezuela, and Bogota, Colombia, expanding its global network. On 12th May 2026, the carrier disclosed that services would begin on 22 July 2026, marking a milestone in its Americas expansion. The Doha based airline will become the first Gulf carrier to operate regular flights to Venezuela and will open a direct route between the Middle East and Colombia. The routes will operate twice weekly, linking Hamad International Airport in Doha with El Dorado International Airport in Bogota and Simon Bolivar International Airport in Caracas. The schedules have been designed to offer smooth connections to major hubs, enabling transfers to East Asia, the Gulf region and Australasia, thus broadening options for travelers between South America and global destinations. The move underscores Qatar Airways emphasis on serving business and leisure travelers seeking wider connectivity, following its 2010 Sao Paulo service and highlighting its ongoing push to expand its Latin American footprint and support tourism and trade opportunities in Venezuela and Colombia.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS LAUNCHES DIRECT SERVICES TO CARACAS AND BOGOTA, EXPANDING LATIN AMERICAN FOOTPRINT

Qatar Airways has unveiled plans to add direct services to Caracas in Venezuela and Bogota in Colombia, marking a major expansion of its network. The move was revealed on 12th May 2026 and is set to begin on 22nd July 2026.The new routes position Doha as a gateway for South America, with the airline becoming the first Gulf carrier to serve Venezuela and the first Middle Eastern airline to operate nonstop flights to Bogota. Direct services are expected to shorten travel times and improve connectivity to Asia, Africa, and Europe via Hamad International Airport.Flights will operate twice weekly, on Wednesdays and Sundays, giving travelers added flexibility and supporting increased tourism and business exchanges as Caracas and Bogota broaden their appeal.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS ANNOUNCES DIRECT FLIGHTS TO CARACAS AND BOGOTA

Qatar Airways announced a new service expansion to Caracas, Venezuela, and Bogota, Colombia, as part of its ongoing network growth. The twice weekly flights from Hamad International Airport in Doha are scheduled to begin on 22nd July 2026, according to a statement dated 12th May 2026. The carrier says it will be the first Gulf airline to serve Venezuela and the first to offer direct links from the Middle East to both capitals.The new route operates under flight QR783 on Wednesdays and Sundays with a tag stop format that allows passengers to board in Doha and travel directly to either Bogota or Caracas. The return leg from Caracas to Doha is a direct service, enabling onward connections to markets including Australia, China, Japan, and the UAE.With this addition, Qatar Airways increases its Americas footprint to 16 destinations, part of a broader plan to restore its network to more than 160 destinations worldwide. The move builds on the airline's South American operations that began in 2010 with Sao Paulo and reinforces its strategy to tap underserved long-haul markets in northern South America and the Caribbean region. The Caracas service also positions Qatar Airways as a key gateway for Gulf travelers seeking access to Colombia and neighboring markets.
