DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, has announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group, under which Fortress has agreed to purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026.This forward-flow agreement marks a significant expansion of the existing partnership between Upstart and Fortress, which initially began with a transaction in 2025. The collaboration illustrates Upstart’s ongoing efforts to broaden its network of capital partners, which now includes over 100 banks and credit unions leveraging its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The platform offers a wide range of financial products, including personal loans, automotive retail and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product."We’re excited to expand our partnership with Fortress with this latest agreement. Our wide array of capital partners strengthens our resilient and stable foundation to continue driving down the cost and complexity of borrowing," said Sanjay Datta, President of Capital and Enterprise at Upstart. This sentiment underscores Upstart's commitment to enhancing its service offerings while fostering relationships with key financial institutions.Commenting on the expanded agreement, Matt Biczak, Managing Director at Fortress Investment Group, stated, "This expanded agreement with Upstart underscores our focus on sourcing differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for our investors. Upstart’s data-driven platform enables efficient access to scaled origination, and we believe this partnership positions us well to continue generating durable, compelling risk-adjusted returns across market environments."The partnership between Upstart and Fortress Investment Group reflects a growing trend in the financial sector, where technology-driven solutions are increasingly being embraced to enhance the efficiency and accessibility of consumer credit. As Upstart continues to innovate and expand its offerings, the strategic alignment with Fortress positions both companies to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH $180 MILLION INVESTMENT

Pure Salmon Japan secures a significant preferred note investment of over USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. This investment marks a pivotal milestone for Pure Salmon Japan, as it continues to develop its state-of-the-art land-based salmon farming facility in Japan. The transaction was completed on 1st April 2026 and is expected to support the construction of the facility, which is designed to meet the growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The partnership with Fortress Investment Group highlights the institutional backing that Pure Salmon Japan has garnered for its ambitious project. With a target production of 10,000 tonnes, this facility aims to become the largest land-based salmon farm in Japan. By utilizing advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology, the facility is set to contribute to Japan's food security by reducing reliance on imports and ensuring a stable supply of locally produced salmon.Erol Emed, CEO of Pure Salmon Japan, expressed gratitude for the investment, emphasizing the strategic importance of the project amidst Japan's focus on enhancing domestic seafood production. He stated, 'We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan.'Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding history in Japan, is not only bringing capital but also valuable expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, noted the importance of the facility in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure. This investment aligns with Fortress’s broader strategy to support sustainable initiatives that address pressing global challenges such as food security and environmental sustainability.As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan aims to engage with additional long-term equity partners to further bolster its operational capabilities. The successful closing of this financing not only showcases the increasing institutional investor interest in sustainable aquaculture but also positions Pure Salmon Japan as a key player in the evolving seafood supply chain in Japan.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. This acquisition, made on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by affiliates of Fortress, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.The Tuscan Gardens community features 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus approximately one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, catering to an affluent and growing senior population along Florida’s northeast coast. Under AgeWell’s operational leadership, occupancy and performance metrics have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, expressed enthusiasm about the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast features thoughtfully designed residences, diverse dining options, wellness and fitness amenities, landscaped courtyards, and personalized care programs tailored to individual resident needs. The community benefits from strong demographic trends and limited new senior housing supply locally, as well as proximity to healthcare, retail, and coastal amenities.This acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR AND HASI'S CARBONCOUNT SECURES $508 MILLION FOR SUSTAINABLE INFRASTRUCTURE

KKR & Co. Inc. and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) have announced a significant milestone for their clean energy infrastructure platform, CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1), which has raised $508 million through a private offering of senior unsecured notes. This announcement comes as of 11th May 2026, further solidifying the platform's commitment to sustainable infrastructure investments across the United States.The proceeds from this latest fundraising effort will be deployed to acquire or invest in both new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects. CarbonCount was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI, aiming to provide long-term capital solutions for projects focused on renewable energy, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, renewable natural gas (RNG), and transportation infrastructure initiatives.Originally, CCH1 was established with commitments of $1 billion from both KKR and HASI, intended to be invested over an 18-month period. In December 2025, both firms agreed to add another $500 million to the platform. The current transaction marks CCH1's second issuance of senior notes, following a $592 million issuance completed in June 2025. With this new round of financing, the platform's total investment capacity now approaches $5 billion.Cecilio Velasco, Managing Director at KKR, noted that the strong investor reception for the latest issuance underscores the quality and diversity of CCH1’s underlying asset base. He emphasized that with over $4 billion of investment capacity, the platform is well-positioned to meet the growing demand for sustainable and affordable energy infrastructure across the U.S.Dan McMahon, Senior Managing Director of Syndications at HASI, expressed enthusiasm about expanding CCH1’s investment capacity, highlighting the platform's role in supporting the increasing activity in sustainable infrastructure investment. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities acted as Joint Lead Placement Agents for this transaction, which further demonstrates the collaborative efforts of KKR and HASI in driving sustainable growth.

+ KKR DIVESTS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced the sale of its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, marking a significant transaction in the European real estate market. The announcement was made on 11th May 2026, at 21:07:57.692343, and is part of KKR's ongoing strategy to streamline its investment focus while capitalizing on current market conditions.The Danish portfolio consists of several residential units located in key urban areas, providing Pears Global with an opportunity to enhance its real estate footprint in Northern Europe. This transaction underscores KKR's commitment to optimizing its asset management by divesting non-core holdings in favor of more strategic investments that align with its long-term objectives.KKR has been actively reshaping its investment portfolio in response to shifting market dynamics and potential global economic risks. By selling the Danish residential properties, KKR can reallocate capital towards sectors that promise higher returns, particularly in private credit, which the firm views as resilient in the current economic climate.Pears Global Real Estate, known for its strategic acquisitions in high-value markets, is expected to leverage its expertise to enhance the residential properties acquired from KKR. This acquisition aligns with Pears Global's strategy to expand its portfolio in Europe, where demand for residential properties continues to rise amid ongoing urbanization trends.As KKR continues to adapt its investment strategy, market observers will be keen to see how this sale impacts both firms in the long term. Investors will likely be watching closely for further developments from KKR as it navigates the complexities of the current economic landscape.

+ KKR COMPLETES SALE OF DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced the sale of its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move that underscores KKR's strategic focus on optimizing its investment holdings. This transaction, disclosed on May 11, 2026, at 21:07:57.692343, highlights KKR's ongoing efforts to streamline its assets in response to evolving market conditions.The divestment of the Danish residential portfolio aligns with KKR's broader strategy to enhance its investment focus and capitalize on emerging opportunities within the real estate sector. By transferring these assets to Pears Global Real Estate, KKR aims to sharpen its concentration on core investments while navigating the complexities of the current global financial landscape.KKR's decision to sell its residential holdings is indicative of a robust approach to asset management, especially in light of the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties. The firm has consistently demonstrated resilience in private credit markets, positioning itself favorably amidst fluctuating market dynamics and investor sentiment.In conjunction with this sale, KKR has maintained a strong emphasis on sustainability practices, as reflected in its MSCI ESG score, which assesses environmental, social, and governance performance relative to industry peers. This focus on responsible investing not only enhances KKR's market reputation but also aligns with the growing investor demand for sustainable investment options.As KKR moves forward with this transaction, stakeholders will be keenly observing the implications for the company's future investment strategies and overall portfolio performance. The sale to Pears Global Real Estate not only marks a significant milestone for KKR but also reinforces its commitment to adapting to changing market conditions and maximizing shareholder value.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP AND SEI STRENGTHEN TIES FOR PRIVATE MARKET SOLUTIONS

OAKS, PA — SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) and Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) have expanded their partnership to develop private market investment solutions aimed at wealth and retirement investors as demand for alternative assets continues to grow across advisory and retirement channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the firms announced that their enhanced collaboration will focus on broadening access to institutional-style private market strategies, which include model portfolios and defined contribution retirement offerings.This partnership builds on an existing multi-year relationship that has centered on fund administration and technology services. SEI and Carlyle stated that the collaboration uniquely combines Carlyle’s private markets investment platform with SEI’s robust research, implementation, and client servicing capabilities. This integration aims to provide investors with tailored solutions to navigate the complexities of private market investments.Michael Lane, head of asset management at SEI, emphasized that financial advisors and investors are increasingly seeking simpler methods to incorporate private market exposure into their portfolios. Lane noted, 'One of the most common questions we hear from clients is how to allocate to private markets. Our objective with Carlyle is to help simplify that decision by providing more streamlined access to a broader range of strategies.'Jeff Nedelman, Carlyle’s co-president and global head of client business, reiterated the firms' focus on the growing demand for private market investments among individual investors and retirement savers. Nedelman remarked, 'This partnership reflects the increasing role of private markets across the wealth and retirement landscape.' The collaboration aims to meet this demand head-on, offering innovative solutions designed for a diversifying investor base.As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported approximately $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement. Meanwhile, Carlyle managed $477 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025, across its private equity, credit, and investment solutions businesses. The expanded partnership is expected to enhance both firms' capabilities in delivering sought-after private market investment solutions.

+ CARLYLE GROUP EXPANDS HEALTHCARE PORTFOLIO WITH KNACK AND EQUALIZERCM ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group Inc. has announced its acquisition of a majority stake in healthcare revenue cycle management (RCM) firms Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, marking a significant expansion in its healthcare portfolio. The announcement was made in a statement on May 4th, 2026, although specific financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.The acquisition allows Carlyle Group to integrate the expertise of Knack's founder Rajiv Sharma and EqualizeRCM's Nagi Rao, both of whom will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the newly formed RCM platform. This collaboration aims to enhance Carlyle's advisory services by leveraging advanced analytics and global operations, as emphasized by Rao in the statement.Knack and EqualizeRCM serve a diverse clientele, including physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals. The partnership is expected to tackle complex aspects of the revenue cycle, such as rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack's Chief Executive Officer Gautam Barai. This strategic move aligns with Carlyle's vision to broaden its reach in the RCM industry.Carlyle Group has expressed intentions to pursue further opportunities within the RCM sector, indicating a continued focus on enhancing its capabilities in healthcare management. The acquisition of Knack and EqualizeRCM is seen as a crucial step in solidifying Carlyle's position in a rapidly evolving market, where efficient revenue management is essential for healthcare providers.As the healthcare landscape continues to change, the integration of advanced technologies and strategic partnerships will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of revenue cycle management. Carlyle Group’s acquisition of Knack and EqualizeRCM is poised to deliver innovative solutions that address the challenges faced by healthcare providers today.

+ CARLYLE GROUP'S STRATEGIC MOVE INTO HEALTHCARE TECHNOLOGY SERVICES

The Carlyle Group L.P. has garnered attention following its recent strategic move to integrate two healthcare service providers into a unified platform focused on advanced digital capabilities. This development, announced on the 4th of May, 2026, highlights a broader shift towards automation and data-driven systems within the healthcare services landscape across the United States.Carlyle's expansion into healthcare services is a response to the ongoing structural changes faced by healthcare systems across the United States, which are influenced by administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The integration of Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM aims to address these challenges by creating a platform capable of supporting various healthcare segments, including physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers that require tailored operational assistance.The significance of the combined platform lies in its ability to enhance revenue cycle management through the integration of complementary strengths from both Knack and Equalize. Knack offers a globally distributed operational framework, while Equalize brings proprietary tools for predictive analytics and process optimization. This unified approach is designed to improve efficiency in billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, ultimately reshaping how healthcare organizations manage their financial workflows.Carlyle's move aligns with broader market trends as healthcare providers increasingly seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities. The demand for integrated platforms that can streamline billing processes while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements has led to a growing interest in specialized service providers. By positioning itself within this evolving landscape, Carlyle is tapping into a sector that is experiencing significant transformation.As the healthcare services sector continues to embrace digital infrastructure advancements, Carlyle's focus on integrating advanced technology within its platform underscores the importance of innovation in addressing industry challenges. With automation and data-driven systems becoming critical components of healthcare administration, Carlyle is poised to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of healthcare services.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL REINFORCES INTER MILAN WITH €5M CASH INJECTION

Oaktree Capital Management has injected an additional €5 million into Inter Milan, further solidifying their financial support for the club. This investment was confirmed in a semiannual statement released on 11th May 2026, revealing that the total cash infusion into the club's equity reserves has now reached €52 million.Oaktree Capital, having taken ownership of Inter Milan nearly a year ago, acquired the club from former owners Suning after the Chinese company defaulted on a loan. Since the acquisition, Oaktree has initiated a strategic shift within the club, focusing on a blend of stability and future growth, while retaining key personnel such as CEO Giuseppe Marotta.Under Oaktree's stewardship, the Nerazzurri have maintained the core of last season's Serie A title-winning squad, indicating a careful approach to team management. However, there is a clear intention from Oaktree to rejuvenate the squad by investing in younger players with potential resale value, aligning with their longer-term vision for the club.Moreover, Oaktree is advancing plans for a new stadium to replace the iconic San Siro, a move that is anticipated to significantly enhance the club's revenue streams. The recent €5 million cash injection is part of a broader strategy to ensure that Inter Milan remains competitive both on and off the pitch as they navigate the complexities of modern football.This latest funding round follows a previous injection of similar magnitude in December last year, underscoring Oaktree Capital's ongoing commitment to the club's financial health and strategic objectives. As Inter Milan continues to evolve under Oaktree's guidance, all eyes will be on how these investments translate into on-field success and enhanced commercial prospects.

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS HOLDINGS SECURES $300 MILLION LOAN FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility with Oaktree Capital Management, which is poised to significantly reshape the company's capital structure. This financing arrangement, which refinances existing debt through 2031 while reserving US$35 million for future growth initiatives, was established on 3rd May 2026.The loan features a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure complemented by flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets. This arrangement enhances Establishment Labs Holdings' funding capacity and provides greater balance sheet flexibility as it embarks on operational and expansion plans. The infusion of capital could also serve to bolster its investment narrative in a competitive market.Central to the investment case for Establishment Labs is the belief that its Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures will translate into significant global market adoption. Recent management guidance projects 2026 revenues between US$264 million and US$266 million, suggesting a growth rate of approximately 25% to 26% compared to the previous year. The new loan not only alleviates refinancing pressures but also offers liquidity that assists in tackling ongoing losses and cash burn without necessitating an immediate equity raise.While the improved balance sheet flexibility presents a positive outlook, investors are advised to remain cautious about potential risks associated with continued cash burn and negative free cash flow. The new facility comes at a time when some analysts have projected an optimistic revenue growth trajectory of about 30% annually, with earnings reaching US$73.5 million by 2029. This could either reinforce bullish sentiments surrounding the company's future or prompt a more tempered view of its path to profitability.In summary, Establishment Labs Holdings' recent agreement with Oaktree Capital Management marks a pivotal moment in its financial strategy. Investors are encouraged to evaluate the potential impact of this loan on the company's long-term growth objectives, while remaining aware of the inherent risks in its operational execution and market demand.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK WITH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a significant Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks an important milestone in the company's efforts to manage its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026, and it includes both the amendment of existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders.The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, introducing new provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, allowing lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, highlighting the company's strategic approach to financing.In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel has committed to filing registration statements with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This move is essential for maintaining investor confidence, as it reinforces the company's intention to provide liquidity and transparency to its stakeholders. The lenders have indicated their intent to hold the securities for investment purposes only, which could prevent immediate market fluctuations due to rapid selling.Investors should be aware of the potential implications of these developments. The issuance of a substantial number of new warrants could lead to dilution of existing shareholders' equity if exercised. Additionally, the company’s obligations to register these shares for resale could increase the total share count, which may affect the stock's market price. Such financing strategies can signal to the market the company's liquidity needs or strategic objectives.BioXcel Therapeutics has clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company, thereby adhering to full reporting and compliance requirements. The registration rights agreement, which includes various Oaktree entities among other institutional holders, reflects a commitment to ongoing compliance with securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor upcoming filings and press releases closely, as these changes may significantly influence BioXcel's financial landscape and market performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT IN TALKS TO SELL MIDCAP FINANCIAL

Apollo Global Management is reportedly in discussions to divest its business development company, MidCap Financial. On 11th May 2026, sources indicated that Apollo estimates MidCap's portfolio of investments to be valued at approximately $3 billion. The anticipated buyer is expected to be another company specializing in business development, potentially using its own stock shares as part of the transaction to acquire MidCap. This strategic move reflects Apollo's ongoing adjustments within its financial operations.The decision to divest MidCap Financial is significant for Apollo Global Management as it seeks to streamline its operations and focus on its core asset management business. The move aligns with the company's strategy to optimize its portfolio and enhance shareholder value. By potentially selling MidCap, Apollo may be looking to free up capital and resources to invest in other areas of growth within its expansive asset management operations.As one of the world's largest alternative asset managers, Apollo Global Management reported total assets under management (AUM) of $938.4 billion as of the end of 2025. The company operates primarily in the financial services sector and has a market capitalization of approximately $76.79 billion. Apollo's diverse portfolio encompasses private equity, real estate, and credit, solidifying its position as a key player in the financial landscape.Currently, Apollo Global Management's P/E ratio stands at 84.84, a notably high figure compared to historical averages, suggesting that the stock is trading at a premium relative to its earnings. Such elevated valuations may warrant consideration from potential investors, especially as the GF Score(TradeMark) ranks stocks based on financial strength, profitability, growth, valuation, and momentum. Apollo's solid GF Score(TradeMark) indicates a favorable outlook for long-term returns, although moderate financial strength may require attention.In the past 12 months, insider activity at Apollo Global has seen five sells totaling approximately $127.57 million, contrasted with just one reported buy. This trend of insider selling could be indicative of the sentiment among those closest to the company. As Apollo's potential divestiture of MidCap Financial unfolds, investors should closely monitor the company's upcoming earnings report and any developments regarding this transaction.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT ENGAGES IN $3B SALE TALKS FOR MIDCAP FINANCIAL

Apollo Global Management is reportedly negotiating the sale of its publicly traded private credit fund, MidCap Financial Investment, in a deal valued at approximately $3 billion. The discussions, which took place on 11th May 2026, may involve a business development company as the buyer, potentially including shares in the transaction rather than a cash-only arrangement.The potential sale underscores Apollo Global Management's strategic maneuvering in response to ongoing challenges in the private credit market. MidCap Financial Investment has experienced a troubling increase in its default rate, which surged to 5.3% in the first quarter of 2026, up from 3.9% at the end of 2025. Additionally, the fund reported a quarterly loss of $61 million, raising concerns about its long-term viability and the broader implications for Apollo's asset management strategy.As one of the largest alternative asset managers globally, Apollo Global Management boasts a market capitalization of approximately $76.79 billion and manages a total of $938.4 billion in assets, with $709.1 billion in fee-earning assets. The firm’s diverse portfolio spans private equity, real estate, and credit, solidifying its position as a key player in the alternative investment sector. However, the company's high P/E ratio of 84.84x, nearing its five-year peak, suggests that its stock may be overvalued relative to its earnings potential.In evaluating Apollo Global Management's investment prospects, potential investors should consider its GF Score(TradeMark) of 80, which ranks stocks based on financial strength, profitability, growth, valuation, and momentum. While the company demonstrates strong profitability with a rank of 8/10, its financial strength is rated at 6/10, indicating caution due to recent debt issuance and a concerning Altman Z-score of 0.04. These factors could affect the stock's long-term outlook as the firm navigates the current market landscape.Additionally, insider selling trends may raise red flags for investors. In the past 12 months, Apollo Global Management has seen five insider sell transactions totaling approximately $127.57 million, contrasted by a single insider buy. This trend, combined with the elevated valuation metrics and challenges faced by its private credit fund, suggests that investors should approach Apollo Global Management with caution as they assess potential volatility in the near future.

+ APOLLO EXPANDS FRESH FOOD FOOTPRINT WITH PROSOL GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management LLC has acquired a majority stake in Prosol Group, a prominent player in the French fresh food retail market, marking a significant expansion of its presence in this sector. The transaction, finalized on the 7th of May 2026, was executed through Apollo-managed funds, which have successfully secured this investment from Ardian, a global private investment firm.Founded in 1992, Prosol Group has built a strong reputation in the fresh food sector by establishing a vertically integrated supply chain that emphasizes quality and freshness. The company operates nearly 450 stores across France, primarily under the brands Grand Frais and fresh, which have fostered a loyal customer base and robust growth. Jean-Paul Mochet will continue to lead Prosol as Chief Executive Officer, working towards the company’s long-term growth objectives in collaboration with Apollo.This acquisition represents an opportunity for Apollo to enhance its footprint in the fresh food retail sector, leveraging Prosol's established supply chain and customer loyalty. As Apollo Global Management LLC manages approximately $1.03 trillion in assets, the firm aims to utilize its expertise to support Prosol's expansion and maximize potential returns from this investment. Financial advisory services for this transaction were provided by UBS AG, with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard also involved in the advisory process.While the acquisition presents numerous growth opportunities, it also carries inherent risks, particularly in the volatile fresh food market. Challenges such as supply chain disruptions and shifting consumer preferences could impact the investment. Furthermore, the financial terms of the acquisition have not been disclosed, leading to questions regarding the valuation of Prosol and the expected financial implications for Apollo.As the integration of Prosol into Apollo's portfolio unfolds, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the alignment between Prosol's operational strategy and Apollo's broader investment objectives. With a strong management team in place and a commitment to maintaining high-quality standards, Apollo Global Management is poised to capitalize on the growth potential within the French fresh food retail sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT HOLDS FIRM ON WEALTH GOALS AMID MARKET UNCERTAINTY

Ares Management Corp. is maintaining its ambitious target of raising $125 billion from wealth clients by 2028, despite growing concerns in the broader private credit market. On 9th May 2026, Chief Executive Officer Mike Arougheti reiterated the company's commitment to its fundraising goal, which was increased by $25 billion last September. This confidence comes in the wake of a challenging environment characterized by redemption requests from retail investors, particularly those in non-traded business development companies (BDCs).Arougheti highlighted that most of the redemption activity observed at Ares Management originated from smaller family offices and certain institutions outside the United States, rather than from well-informed high-net-worth investors. He noted that 95% of Ares' investor base in the BDC did not opt to redeem their investments. This statistic suggests that the narrative surrounding widespread redemptions may not accurately reflect the sentiments of the majority of investors in the firm’s funds.The recent selloff in technology stocks, especially within the software sector, has caused anxiety among retail investors regarding exposure to private credit markets. In response to these concerns, Ares Management, along with other alternative credit managers, has taken measures to limit redemptions in its non-traded BDCs. Arougheti emphasized that the company remains focused on long-term growth and views the current market turbulence as an opportunity rather than a setback.Despite the challenges, Ares Management's leadership believes that its diversified investment strategies and strong relationships with clients will enable the firm to achieve its wealth targets. The company continues to engage with its investor base to assure them of the resilience of their investments, particularly in alternative assets. Arougheti's remarks point to a conviction that the broader landscape for alternative investments remains robust, even as some market segments face volatility.As Ares Management moves forward with its fundraising initiatives, investors will be closely monitoring how the firm navigates the current credit environment. The ability to attract significant capital amidst the shifting market dynamics will be critical for Ares as it seeks to solidify its position as a leader in the alternative asset management sector. The company's commitment to its wealth goals amid uncertainty reflects its strategic approach to growth and client engagement.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND AMID STRONG INVESTOR DEMAND

Ares Management L.P has successfully raised $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting a sustained investor appetite for industrial real estate in the country. This fundraising effort comes in the first three months of 2026, with the first close expected to occur in spring, according to chief executive Michael Arougheti during an earnings call.Arougheti emphasized the strong demand for the new fund, stating, "Our fifth Japan Logistics Development Fund is seeing very strong demand following the excellent performance of prior vintages." This fundraising initiative follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund, which amounted to 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP International.The recent deal has also opened doors for Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle, backed by significant investors such as the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. The Canadian LP contributed $1.3 billion to this vehicle, which includes some seed assets. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, mentioned that the firm is currently fundraising to address both the seed assets and the robust pipeline associated with them.In addition to its efforts in Japan, Ares Management raised an additional $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy in the first quarter of 2026. This capital influx is largely attributed to commitments made to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VII. The latest fundraising comes on the heels of a $400 million close in 2025 and follows a successful $2.4 billion raise by the predecessor vehicle just two years prior.Notable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System and the New York State Common Retirement Fund, which committed $300 million and $200 million, respectively. DealStreetAsia previously reported that the International Finance Corporation (IFC) is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account, which shares a mandate similar to Ares’ SSG series. This fund concentrates on acquiring non-performing loans and single-asset distress situations across several Southeast Asian markets.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT REPORTS STRONG Q1 2026 DIRECT LENDING ACTIVITY

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) has reported a significant uptick in its U.S. direct lending activities, closing approximately $9.5 billion across 70 transactions during the first quarter of 2026. This announcement, made on the 4th of May 2026, highlights Ares Management's strong position in the lending market, which has been bolstered by an increase in demand for flexible capital solutions in various sectors.Over the 12 months leading up to March 31, 2026, Ares Management closed approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring its robust performance in the direct lending space. The firm has established itself as a key player by providing capital to businesses seeking growth and expansion, which is evident from the diverse range of transactions it has facilitated in recent months.Among the noteworthy deals closed by Ares Management in the first quarter are senior secured credit facilities supporting various growth initiatives. For instance, Ares acted as the administrative agent for a credit facility aiding Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, a company specializing in fire and life safety solutions. Additionally, Ares was instrumental in Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, a provider of critical electrical and mechanical services across multiple industries.Further emphasizing its pivotal role in facilitating business growth, Ares Management also served as the lead arranger for a credit facility supporting ARCHIMED's plans for the North American Science Association, a leading contract research organization dedicated to the development of medical devices. Such transactions reflect Ares Management's commitment to supporting vital sectors that contribute to economic development.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management's global platform boasts over $644 billion in assets under management, with operations spanning North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East. This diverse portfolio positions Ares Management as a leading global alternative investment manager, dedicated to delivering consistent and attractive returns for its investors while contributing positively to the communities in which it operates.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS MARKS EXPANSION WITH NEW ROUTES TO CARACAS AND BOGOT

Qatar Airways continues to strengthen its global presence, with the announcement of new scheduled flights to two major cities in South America—Caracas, Venezuela, and Bogot , Colombia. On 12th May 2026, the airline revealed that it will commence these services on July 22, 2026, marking a significant milestone in Qatar Airways’ expansion strategy in the Americas. The Doha-based airline is set to become the first Gulf carrier to operate regular flights to Venezuela, while also establishing a new direct route between the Middle East and Colombia, a market that has remained previously underserved by Middle Eastern carriers.The introduction of scheduled services to Caracas and Bogot is a bold step aimed at expanding Qatar Airways’ footprint in South America. The airline has long been committed to enhancing connectivity between the Middle East and Latin America. Its inaugural service to S o Paulo, Brazil, in 2010 marked the beginning of its operations in the region. Since then, Qatar Airways has steadily increased its reach, and the addition of Venezuela and Colombia to its route network further exemplifies its dedication to improving international air travel options.The new routes will operate twice a week, providing a consistent and reliable connection between Hamad International Airport (DOH) in Doha, and El Dorado International Airport (BOG) in Bogot , as well as Sim n Bol var International Airport (CCS) in Caracas. Qatar Airways has meticulously designed the flight schedules to ensure that passengers have access to seamless connections to and from major global hubs. The timing of these flights will facilitate smoother transfers in Doha to destinations across East Asia, the Gulf region, and Australasia, among others, enhancing the airline’s competitive edge by providing more options for passengers traveling between South America and various international destinations.In particular, business travelers and those with cultural ties between the Americas and Asia or the Middle East will benefit from the smooth connections offered by Qatar Airways. The airline’s extensive network allows passengers to continue their journey to major hubs in cities like Tokyo, Hong Kong, Sydney, and Melbourne without lengthy stopovers. Likewise, leisure travelers seeking to explore the Middle East, Europe, or Africa will find these new services beneficial. The addition of Caracas and Bogot is significant for the local economies of both Venezuela and Colombia, with the former welcoming its first Gulf carrier, which could enhance tourism and trade opportunities.As Qatar Airways expands into Venezuela and Colombia, it is part of a larger vision to rebuild and enhance its global network. This move highlights the airline’s commitment to creating more direct flight options between key markets, ensuring that passengers have convenient travel choices that significantly reduce travel time. With its modern fleet, exemplary service standards, and an expanding network, Qatar

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS HORIZONS WITH DIRECT FLIGHTS TO CARACAS AND BOGOT

Qatar Airways has announced a significant expansion of its route network with the introduction of direct flights to Caracas, Venezuela, and Bogot , Colombia. This historic move, which was revealed on 12th May 2026, marks the airline's first-ever direct services to these South American capitals, further solidifying its commitment to enhancing global connectivity.Set to commence on 22nd July 2026, these new routes will enable direct flights between Doha and both Caracas and Bogot , positioning Qatar Airways as a pivotal player in linking the Middle East with South America. This expansion is part of the airline's broader strategy to improve its global presence and provide passengers with enhanced access to diverse markets and cultural experiences.As the first Gulf carrier to serve Venezuela and the first Middle Eastern airline to operate flights to Bogot , Qatar Airways is setting new standards for intercontinental travel. These direct flights promise to significantly reduce travel times, offering seamless connections to key destinations across Asia, Africa, and Europe via Qatar Airways' state-of-the-art hub at Hamad International Airport in Doha.The introduction of these routes is expected to drive increased tourism and business exchanges, as both Caracas and Bogot offer rich cultural landscapes and burgeoning markets. With flights operating twice a week, every Wednesday and Sunday, Qatar Airways is providing passengers with greater flexibility in their travel plans, further enhancing its role as a global gateway.This expansion to Caracas and Bogot underscores Qatar Airways' ongoing commitment to improving connectivity in underserved regions, making travel between the Middle East and South America more accessible than ever. As the airline continues to grow, it aims to connect even more corners of the globe, facilitating new opportunities for business and tourism across continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS REACH WITH NEW FLIGHTS TO VENEZUELA AND COLOMBIA

Qatar Airways is set to enhance its global connectivity by launching new flight operations to Caracas (CCS), Venezuela, and Bogot (BOG), Colombia, starting on 22nd July 2026. This announcement was made on 12th May 2026, marking the airline as the first Gulf carrier to serve Venezuela and the first to operate direct flights connecting the Middle East to both capital cities.The new service will operate twice weekly from Hamad International Airport (DOH) in Doha, bringing the total number of destinations served by Qatar Airways in the Americas to 16. This expansion is part of Qatar Airways' ongoing commitment to enhance its network and strengthen ties between the Middle East and northern South America.Operating every Wednesday and Sunday under a single flight number, QR783, the new route features a tag-stop format, allowing passengers to board in Doha and travel directly to either Bogot or Caracas. The return leg will operate as a direct service from Caracas back to the airline's hub, facilitating seamless onward transfers to key markets including Australia, China, Japan, and the UAE.The addition of Caracas and Bogot builds on Qatar Airways' South American operations that began in 2010 with its inaugural service to S o Paulo, Brazil. As the airline continues to restore its network to over 160 global destinations, these new routes reflect a strategic push to tap into underserved long-haul markets.Venezuela, known for its stunning landscapes and rich culture, has been making strides in improving its security and accessibility, making it an attractive destination for Gulf-based travelers. With this expansion, Qatar Airways not only provides a new gateway for Colombian travelers to Asia and Oceania but also positions itself as a key player in reshaping air travel connections to the region.

urn:contentItem:6JK7-JTR3-RSBB-S0WD-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXTENDS FORWARD-FLOW COMMITMENT WITH UPSTART AT $1.25 BILLION

Upstart Holdings said Fortress Investment Group will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through its platform over 15 months, a forward-flow commitment disclosed on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and comes as Upstart continues to broaden its network of capital partners, now counting more than 100 banks and credit unions using its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The Upstart platform offers personal loans, auto financing, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives framed the arrangement as a scalable opportunity for both firms: a stronger funding base for Upstart and access to differentiated consumer credit opportunities for Fortress, with Upstart’s data-driven platform enabling efficient, scalable origination and the potential for durable returns across market conditions. The collaboration signals a wider trend of technology-enhanced consumer credit solutions.

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) EXTENDS SENIOR NOTES FINANCING WITH $508 MILLION FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through a private offering of senior unsecured notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1) as of 11th May 2026. Proceeds will fund acquisitions or investments in new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States.CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, RNG, and transportation infrastructure projects. The platform started with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and December 2025 added $500 million. This issue marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million issuance in June 2025, expanding total investment capacity toward $5 billion. Velasco of KKR cited strong investor reception and noted the asset base’s quality and diversity underpin the platform’s ability to deploy more than $4 billion of investment capacity. McMahon of HASI highlighted the expansion of CCH1’s investment capacity to meet growing sustainable-infrastructure activity. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities served as Joint Lead Placement Agents.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) DIVESTS DANISH RESIDENTIAL ASSETS AS PEARS GLOBAL EXPANDS NORTHERN EUROPEAN FOOTPRINT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) has sold its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, with the transaction announced on 11th May 2026 at 21:07:57.692343.The portfolio consists of several residential units located in key urban areas, and Pears Global Real Estate intends to expand its Northern European footprint.KKR frames the sale as part of its asset-management optimization, reallocating capital toward higher-return opportunities, including private credit.Pears Global Real Estate will apply its regional expertise to enhance the properties and support its European expansion amid rising housing demand.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TRANSFERS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) transfers its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move disclosed on May 11, 2026 at 21:07:57.692343. The sale aligns with KKR's plan to optimize holdings and concentrate on core investments within the real estate sector. The transaction underscores KKR's disciplined asset-management approach amid global economic uncertainty and reinforces its emphasis on sustainability as reflected in its MSCI ESG score. Stakeholders will monitor potential effects on future strategy and overall portfolio performance.

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) EXPAND PARTNERSHIP TO SCALE PRIVATE MARKET OFFERINGS FOR RETIREMENT PLANS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) expanded their partnership to provide private market investment solutions for wealth and retirement investors, as demand for alternatives grows across advisory and retirement channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the collaboration aims to broaden access to institutional-style private market strategies, including model portfolios and defined contribution offerings. The initiative builds on a long-standing relationship centered on fund administration and technology services, pairing Carlyle’s private markets platform with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing to offer tailored solutions for navigating private market investments. Michael Lane of SEI emphasized the need for simpler access to private markets, while Carlyle’s Jeff Nedelman highlighted the rising role of private markets in wealth and retirement planning. As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported roughly $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, and Carlyle reported $477 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. The partners expect the expanded collaboration to enhance the delivery of private market strategies to a broader investor base.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ADVANCES REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT THROUGH INTEGRATED HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is combining Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The integrated platform targets physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers, aiming to address administrative complexity and evolving care models. Knack supplies a globally distributed operational framework while Equalize adds predictive analytics and process optimization tools to improve revenue cycle management, billing, and payer coordination. The move reflects a broader trend toward technology-driven healthcare services as automation and data-driven systems become central to clinical administration.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD INTEGRATED RCM PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, broadening its healthcare portfolio. The May 4, 2026 release notes the transaction includes a majority stake in both firms, with terms undisclosed. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the platform to support ongoing developmentKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership aims to address rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views this as a step to deepen its presence in the RCM sector and to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementThe deal underscores Carlyle's focus on technology-enabled revenue cycle management and the potential to deliver enhanced, analytics-driven solutions to providers

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL EXPANDS SUPPORT FOR INTER MILAN WITH €5 MILLION FUNDING

Oaktree Capital has injected €5 million into Inter Milan, lifting total cash infusion to €52 million, according to a semiannual statement released on 11th May 2026.Oaktree took control of Inter nearly a year ago, acquiring the club from Suning after the Chinese group defaulted on a loan. The new owners emphasize both stability and growth, and they have kept CEO Giuseppe Marotta in place.Inter has preserved most of the squad that won last season’s Serie A title but is looking to rejuvenate the team by adding younger players with resale value.The group is also pursuing a new stadium to replace San Siro, a move expected to lift revenue. The €5m funding sits within a broader plan, with a similar tranche provided in December.

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS INKS $300 MILLION TERM LOAN WITH OAKTREE TO BACK MOTIVA PLATFORM EXPANSION

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility from Oaktree Capital Management, completed on 3rd May 2026, with refinancing through 2031 and US$35 million reserved for growth.The loan offers fixed-rate, no-amortization terms and a first-priority lien on assets, designed to boost funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues expansion.Analysts view Motiva as a driver of global adoption for its implants and minimally invasive procedures. Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, up about 25-26% year over year. The facility provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without forcing an immediate equity raise, though ongoing cash burn remains a risk.Some watchers project roughly 30% revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could underpin optimism about growth while highlighting the path to profitability.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) WEIGHS ASSET SALE AS MIDCAP FINANCIAL INVESTMENT FACES RISING DEFAULTS

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is weighing a sale of its publicly traded private credit fund MidCap Financial Investment Corp (NYSE:MFIC) in a roughly $3 billion transaction announced on 11th May 2026. The negotiations contemplate a business development company as a potential buyer and could include shares as part of the consideration.The planned sale comes as MidCap Financial Investment contends with rising credit stress, reporting a 5.3% default rate in Q1 2026 after 3.9% at year-end 2025 and a quarterly loss of $61 million. Apollo Global Management, with a market capitalization near $76.79 billion and assets under management of $938.4 billion (including $709.1 billion in fee-earning assets), continues to be a central player in the alternative investment landscape. Its stock trades at 84.84x earnings, near its five-year peak.GF Score stands at 80, with profitability at 8/10 and financial strength at 6/10; Altman Z-score remains a concern at 0.04. Insider activity shows five insider sells totaling $127.57 million over the last 12 months and one insider buy, highlighting potential near-term volatility as the deal evolves.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) PARTNERS WITH PROSOL GROUP TO EXPAND FRANCE GROCERY NETWORK

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Partners With Prosol Group To Expand France Grocery NetworkThe acquisition closes a majority stake in Prosol Group through Apollo-managed funds, with Ardian serving as the seller. The transaction is dated May 7, 2026. Prosol Group operates about 450 stores across France under Grand Frais and fresh banners and maintains a vertically integrated approach to sourcing and distribution. Jean-Paul Mochet will continue as CEO, guiding the business under Apollo’s stewardship.This partnership taps into Prosol’s established network and customer base to extend Apollo’s presence in the French fresh food market. Apollo manages roughly $1.03 trillion in assets, and the deal was advised by UBS AG with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard participating in the advisory process.Terms of the transaction were not disclosed. Investors should consider risks such as supply chain disruption and shifting consumer preferences as the integration progresses, even as the collaboration aims to enhance long-term returns.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) GUIDES ON PRIVATE-ASSET PRICING PILOT AFTER CROSSING $1 TRILLION AUM

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) posted that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time and outlined a daily pricing pilot for private assets. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan highlighted the impact of strong performance and cited record fee-related earnings contributing to the growth. AUM stood at $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent year over year. First-quarter inflows were $115 billion, with $300 billion in inflows over the trailing twelve months. Asset management inflows totaled $95 billion, aided by Athora's UK Pension Insurance Corporation acquisition, while retirement services added $20 billion in inflows. Apollo noted a broader context with the Financial Stability Board report on private credit vulnerabilities issued the same day and reiterated its emphasis on prudent underwriting. The daily valuation pilot, already in place for investment-grade fixed income products, is expanding to daily pricing for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET following a 6:30 PM ET window for investment-grade assets. The company signaled continued progress toward its 2026 goals, including 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE, supported by a healthy origination pipeline and a steady approach to capital management.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT CORP. (NYSE:ARES) REAFFIRMS $125 BILLION WEALTH TARGET AMID MARKET UNCERTAINTY

On 9th May 2026, Ares Management Corp. (NYSE:ARES) reiterates its target to raise $125 billion from wealth clients by 2028, a goal increased by $25 billion last September. Redemption activity comes primarily from smaller family offices and certain overseas institutions, while 95% of the BDC investor base did not redeem.The technology sector pullback has raised concerns among retail investors about private credit exposure. In response, Ares Management and other alternative credit managers have taken measures to limit redemptions in non-traded BDCs, underscoring a focus on sustainable, long-term growth.The firm relies on diversified investment strategies and strong client relationships to pursue its wealth targets. It continues to engage with investors to demonstrate the resilience of its investments, especially in alternative assets, and sees a robust underlying market for alternatives despite volatility in some areas.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND WITH BROAD INVESTMENT MOMENTUM

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, signaling continued investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The first close is expected in spring, as noted during an earnings call by chief executive Michael AroughetiThe fund follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalThe firm’s broader capital activity includes Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets including a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, Ares' co-president, said the fundraising aims to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares also raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, supported by commitments to SSG Fund VIINotable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account with a mandate aligned to the SSG series, targeting non performing loans and single-asset distress across Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) FACILITATES GROWTH THROUGH DIRECT LENDING WITH ENCORE FIRE PROTECTION LOENBRO AND ARCHIMED

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors. The firm also highlights notable Q1 activity.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, Ares Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring the breadth of its direct lending platform. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals over $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS LATIN AMERICA REACH WITH DIRECT DOHA FLIGHTS TO CARACAS AND BOGOTA

Qatar Airways announced new scheduled flights to Caracas and Bogota, with services starting 22 July 2026. On 12th May 2026 the carrier said these routes would mark a milestone in its Americas expansion and that the airline will be the first Gulf carrier to operate regular flights to Venezuela. The routes will operate twice weekly between Doha and Bogota and between Doha and Caracas, with connections to major hubs in East Asia and beyond to enhance travel options. Since its 2010 Sao Paulo service, Qatar Airways has steadily increased its reach in Latin America, and the new routes illustrate its commitment to expanded connectivity enabling transfers to Tokyo, Hong Kong, Sydney and Melbourne. The company notes that the expansion could boost tourism and trade in Venezuela and Colombia and reinforce its role as a growing global network.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS DEBUTS NONSTOP ROUTES TO CARACAS AND BOGOTA, LINKING DOHA HUB WITH SOUTH AMERICA

Qatar Airways is expanding its network with direct flights to Caracas, Venezuela, and Bogota, Colombia. The expansion was announced on 12th May 2026 and will begin on 22nd July 2026, with services launching this summer.The new routes will connect Doha with two major South American capitals, underscoring Qatar Airways ongoing push to broaden access to underserved markets and strengthen global connectivity.As the first Gulf carrier to serve Venezuela and the first Middle Eastern airline to Bogota, the airline is setting new standards for intercontinental travel. Direct flights are designed to shorten travel times and improve access to connections to Asia, Africa, and Europe via Hamad International Airport in Doha.Flights will operate twice weekly, on Wednesdays and Sundays, boosting tourism and business exchanges and reinforcing Qatar Airways position as a global gateway.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS ADDS CARACAS AND BOGOTA, EXPANDING GULF-SOUTH AMERICA LINKS

Qatar Airways is adding Caracas and Bogota to its network, increasing its footprint in the Americas to 16 destinations. The new twice weekly services from Doha will begin on 22nd July 2026, following an announcement on 12th May 2026. It positions the carrier as the first Gulf airline to serve Venezuela and to operate direct links from the Middle East to both capitals.Services will run as QR783 on Wednesdays and Sundays, using a tag-stop format that lets passengers travel from Doha to either Bogota or Caracas, with the return from Caracas to Doha direct. The route enables onward connections to markets including Australia, China, Japan, and the UAE.The Caracas and Bogota routes build on existing South American operations that began in 2010 with Sao Paulo and reinforce a broader strategy to rebuild the network to more than 160 destinations. They also offer a gateway for Colombian travelers seeking access to Asia and Oceania, and reflect Venezuela's improving security and accessibility.

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website
