+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM REPRESENTS FERVO ENERGY COMPANY IN US$1.89 BILLION UPSIZED IPO

Fervo Energy has announced the pricing of its upsized initial public offering (IPO) of 70,000,000 shares of its Class A common stock, at a public offering price of US$27.00 per share. The 70,000,000 share offering represents a 14,444,445 share upsize to the originally proposed 55,555,555 share offering. In addition, the company has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 10,500,000 shares of Class A common stock.

Latham & Watkins LLP represents Fervo Energy Company in the offering with a corporate deal team led by Houston partners Ryan Maierson, Nick Dhesi, and John Slater, with associates Rachel Collier, Nicholas Blake, and Madhavi Nambiar. Advice was also provided on employment and benefits matters by Los Angeles partner Michelle Carpenter and Chicago partner Nineveh Alkhas, with associates Aaron Tso, Jocelyn Wexler, and Maddie Bober; on environmental matters by Los Angeles/Houston partner Joshua Marnitz, with associate Brandon Kerns; on public company matters by Washington, D.C. partner William Hackett and New York counsel Irina Yevmenenko, with associates Sara Almousa and Mary Tursi; on tax matters by Houston partner Christine Mainguy, with associates Chris Garza and Allison Esquen-Roca; on litigation matters by San Diego partner Drew Gardiner; on antitrust matters by New York partner Kyra Bromley and Washington, D.C. partner Jason Cruise; on securities matters by San Diego partner Colleen Smith; and on capital markets matters by New York partners Betty Huber, Gail Neely, and Washington, D.C. partner Paul Dudek, with associate Shira Mendelsohn.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES LOTTOMATICA ON EUR765 MILLION HIGH‑YIELD BOND OFFERING

Latham & Watkins has advised Lottomatica Group S.p.A. in connection with a EUR765 million high‑yield bond offering, consisting of senior secured notes due 2032 with a fixed interest rate of 4.625%.

+ PROMINENT INTERNATIONAL WHITE COLLAR LAWYER JOINS LATHAM & WATKINS IN CHICAGO

Former federal prosecutor Ryan Rohlfsen brings deep experience in global investigations, compliance, and crisis management

CHICAGO, May 12, 2026 -- Latham & Watkins LLP1 is pleased to announce that Ryan Rohlfsen has joined the firm's Chicago office as a partner in the White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and member of the Litigation & Trial Department. Rohlfsen advises multinational companies, boards, and senior executives in high-stakes government-facing civil and criminal white collar matters, internal investigations, litigation, and related crisis management. He has represented clients in matters on six continents and in all major global markets.

Continue Reading

Ryan Rohlfsen, Partner, Latham & Watkins LLP

"We are thrilled to welcome Ryan to Latham," said Mary Rose Alexander, Managing Partner of the Chicago office. "Ryan is a highly regarded international white collar lawyer with extensive experience helping clients manage risk, navigate enforcement challenges, and respond to sensitive matters. He will be an important resource for clients in the United States and around the world."

Rohlfsen has particular experience advising clients on international and domestic corruption matters, securities and accounting fraud, healthcare fraud, money laundering, sanctions and export controls, data privacy, and state consumer protection matters. He routinely guides public and private companies through complex and fast moving government-facing investigations, and has secured numerous declinations. Beyond advising companies and individuals in crisis, Rohlfsen also counsels global companies on building and strengthening sophisticated compliance programs.

His experience includes serving as a federal prosecutor in the US Department of Justice's Criminal Fraud Section in Washington, D.C. and Chicago. At DOJ, Rohlfsen led complex securities fraud, healthcare fraud, and domestic corruption matters, and served in the Department's specialized unit responsible exclusively for corporate criminal enforcement of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA).

"With over two decades of experience around the world, including as a former senior US federal prosecutor and trial lawyer, Ryan brings exceptional credentials and deep investigations, compliance, and government enforcement defense expertise to Latham," said Terra Reynolds, Global Vice Chair of Latham's White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and Healthcare & Life Sciences Industry Group.

"Ryan's judgment, litigation experience, and deep understanding of complex cross-border investigations and corporate fraud make him a strong addition to the firm," said Sean Berkowitz, a member of Latham's Executive Committee. "He has the kind of practical perspective clients value when the stakes are high, and we are excited to welcome him to the team."

"I'm delighted to join Latham and work alongside

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ LAW360 PROFILES SIMPSON THACHER AS A 2025 REAL ESTATE PRACTICE GROUP OF THE YEAR

Simpson Thacher was profiled as a Real Estate Practice Group of the Year for 2025 by Law360. The Firm was recognized for its role in several major transactions over the past year, including Blackstone's landmark $16.2 billion acquisition of Australian data center operator AirTrunk and its $4 billion acquisition of Retail Opportunity Investments Corp.

Sas Mehrara, Head of the Firm's Real Estate Practice, told Law360 that the global scope of the AirTrunk acquisition required seamless cross-border collaboration among Simpson Thacher attorneys across multiple offices and practice areas, enabling Blackstone to execute a complex transaction in a new and distinctive market. Partner Whitney Salinas added that the Firm's Real Estate Practice is adept at translating expertise across regions. She emphasized the value of "being able to use the technology and solutions that we've worked on with our clients in one geographic region and port it into another geographic region, realizing that a lot of the same markets exist in different regions."

The profile also highlighted the rapid expansion of Simpson Thacher's Real Estate Practice and noted how the recent addition of a lender-side practice provides a roadmap for future growth. "As we've grown, we've really been able to still maintain our small feel, but really grow in a way that allows for collaboration, allows the high-level respect amongst each other as we get scale," Sas said.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER ADVISES ACHIEVE TOGETHER ON £400 MILLION ALL-SENIOR DEBT PACKAGE

Simpson Thacher represented Achieve Together (backed by InfraBridge) on its new £400 million all-senior debt package (including a Social Finance Framework), raised from a syndicate of leading U.K. and international banks alongside long-term core infrastructure investors. The new financing package enables the full repayment of existing group indebtedness and enables Achieve Together to continue its next phase of strategic expansion within the specialist care sector in the U.K.

The funding under the financing closed on 4 February 2026.

The Simpson Thacher team included Paul Hibbert, Emma Serginson and Charlie Wilson (Credit); Daniel Deacon, Mark Collier and Eduardo Rodriguez (Derivatives); and Yash Rupal, Oliver Rosshandler and Daniel Griffiths (Tax).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER SECURES REVERSAL FOR NORTH AMERICAN FIRE IN DELAWARE SUPREME COURT

On February 3, 2026, Simpson Thacher secured a victory for North American Fire Ultimate Holdings LP ("North American Fire") when the Delaware Supreme Court reversed and remanded a Court of Chancery judgement that had dismissed North American Fire's complaint against a former employee for violating restrictive covenants found in an Incentive Unit Grant Agreement. In its order, the Court adopted the Firm's position that consideration is measured at the time of contract formation, not at the time of enforcement. Applying Newell Rubbermaid Inc. v. Storm, the Court held that while the value of the Incentive Units was "somewhat contingent," it was not illusory at the time of formation and therefore constituted adequate consideration to support the Agreement.

The Simpson Thacher team included Michael Garvey, Alison Sher, Ryerson Symons, Nicole Palmadesso and Sarah Heintz.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-SSJ3-S421-P103-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIMNEW YORK, May 14, 2026

PR NewswireNEW YORK, May 14, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation ("Primoris" or the "Company") (NYSE: PRIM). Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980.

The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices.[Click here for information about joining the class action]On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects.On this news, Primoris's stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. Seewww.pomlaw.com.Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.CONTACT:Danielle Peyton
Pomerantz LLP

value.Title: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-WX83-S1DX-4036-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ACE US SPACE TECH ACTIVE ETF TOPS 100 BILLION WON IN NET ASSETS AS EARLY INTEREST GROWS

As the space sector evolves, the ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capture emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth space technologies.

+ KIC ANNOUNCES 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY TO STRENGTHEN FINANCE TALENT

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, set to begin on 20th April 2026, with a mission to raise finance professionals' expertise in both public and private sectors.The program comprises eight lectures over four days, starting with a 2026 outlook on the U.S. stock market and covering alternative investments such as infrastructure and private debt, plus post-investment portfolio management from both management and operational perspectives.It will also tackle topics including the U.S. interest-rate environment and the institutionalization of stablecoins, with applications already received from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, said the organization will continue to share its overseas investment experience and global network to foster domestic growth, noting that the International Finance Academy has been expanding since its 2022 inception to strengthen local investment professionals.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION EXPANDS FOCUS ON MID-MARKET INFRA AT NEW YORK FORUM

The 28th New York Finance Forum, hosted by KIC at the Korea Society, took place on April 16, 2026, with over 40 participants focusing on North American mid-market infrastructure investments.Northleaf Capital Partners outlined that mid-market deals are typically USD 1 billion or less, with eligibility expanding to up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics; 86% of North American infra transactions occur in the mid-market, while funds remain biased toward larger vehicles.Jessica Kennedy highlighted the appeal of mid-market investments for value creation and multiple exit options, predicting solid risk-adjusted returns; Yuryoung Kim noted optimism that the forum would help domestic investors sharpen overseas investment strategies and emphasized KIC's global network to support information sharing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ARDIAN AND GOLDMAN EXPAND SECONDARY FOOTPRINT WITH CIC PURCHASE

On 15th April 2026, Goldman Sachs and Ardian acquired a $1 billion CIC portfolio at notable discounts, with Ardian obtaining a double-digit markdown and Goldman a single-digit discount.CIC is reshaping its holdings to reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. By 2024, nearly half of its assets were allocated to alternatives, and about 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally.The sale follows CIC's earlier pause on divesting stakes in Carlyle, KKR and TPG and underscores the buyers' continued expansion of the secondary market. Ardian has just raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions totaling over $80 billion. The move comes as institutions such as Harvard and various Asian managers increasingly turn to secondaries for liquidity and rebalancing in a market where concentration and illiquidity concerns persist.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES GP POSITION IN CICC–HBIS FUND, AIMING TO ACCELERATE CHINA’S ADVANCED MATERIALS, NEW ENERGY AND IT INVESTMENTS

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The announcement was made at a ceremony at HBIS Group’s R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds plans to raise RMB32 billion over a 15-year term and will deploy capital through a blend of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS’s industrial priorities, including traditional sectors and emerging fields such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.Structured around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the vehicle is designed to connect local governments with international capital and drive industrial upgrading. BlueFive will work with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to support the fund’s long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, said the team is bullish on China’s long-term growth potential and sees opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group ranks among the world’s largest steel producers and has appeared on the Fortune Global 500 list for 17 consecutive years, while CICC provides a comprehensive financial services platform in Beijing. The partnership is expected to bolster investment opportunities and regional growth.

+ INVEST ALBERTA NOTES RENEWED CHINESE INTEREST AS CANADA ATTRACTS $60 BILLION IN FDI

China and Hong Kong’s foreign direct investment into Canada surpassed $60 billion by the end of 2023, making them the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta attracted roughly half of the total, with China at $24.9 billion and Hong Kong at $32.8 billion, according to Statistics Canada, placing them behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.The Globe and Mail, in a December 30, 2025 report, highlighted sustained Alberta–China/Hong Kong engagement despite federal tensions with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse noted rising talk with federal authorities and described the period as 'harvest time' for investors who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta says the investments are centered on oil and gas, carbon capture, green-energy projects and tourism in Alberta, with China serving as a key customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline. The relationship has shifted since the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians from 2018 to 2021, with policy steps such as a 2024 visit to Beijing by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly and a potential 2026 visit linked to a broader diplomatic reset. While ties evolve, Canada remains far more exposed to the United States for FDI (about 45.7% in 2023) than to Asia-Oceania (11.8%), where Japan, Hong Kong and China play lead roles.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S0GM-T16G-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART EXPANDS FORTRESS PARTNERSHIP WITH $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT

Upstart Holdings announced a forward-flow commitment from Fortress affiliates to buy up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated on its platform over 15 months, with the disclosure on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and reflects Upstart’s effort to broaden its network of capital partners. Fortress and its affiliates join more than 100 banks and credit unions using Upstart’s AI-driven models and cloud applications to originate loans.Upstart’s platform covers a broad suite of products, including personal loans, automotive finance and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives described the arrangement as a win for both sides, noting that a larger base of capital partners helps reduce borrowing costs and complexity for borrowers, while Fortress seeks differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for its investors. The collaboration signals a broader industry shift toward technology-driven credit solutions and positions both companies to pursue opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: \"We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families.\"The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) JOINS HASI IN $508 MILLION PRIVATE NOTE OFFERING FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through a private offering of senior unsecured notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1), as of 11th May 2026. The proceeds will be deployed to acquire or invest in both new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States.CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, RNG, and transportation infrastructure projects. The platform began with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and in December 2025 the firms added another $500 million. This issuance marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million offering completed in June 2025, and lifts the platform’s total investment capacity toward $5 billion.Cecilio Velasco, Managing Director at KKR, noted the strong investor reception for the latest issuance and highlighted that with over $4 billion of investment capacity the platform is well-positioned to meet the growing demand for sustainable energy infrastructure. Dan McMahon, Senior Managing Director of Syndications at HASI, expressed enthusiasm about expanding CCH1’s investment capacity and its role in supporting rising activity in sustainable infrastructure. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities acted as Joint Lead Placement Agents for the transaction

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) EXITS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO THROUGH SALE TO PEARS GLOBAL

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) has sold its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move that aligns with the firm's ongoing portfolio rationalization. The sale was announced on 11th May 2026 at 21:07:57.692343.The Danish portfolio comprises several residential units located in key urban areas, giving Pears Global Real Estate an opportunity to expand its Northern European footprint.KKR cited its plan to optimize asset management by divesting non-core holdings and reallocating capital toward higher-return opportunities, with private credit highlighted as a core area.Pears Global Real Estate has a track record of strategic acquisitions in high-value markets and intends to leverage its expertise to enhance the properties and expand its Europe-focused portfolio, where demand for residential properties is rising amid urbanization.Investors will monitor the implications for both entities as they continue to adjust to evolving market dynamics and global risks.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) DIVESTS DANISH RESIDENTIAL HOLDINGS TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) sells its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a transaction disclosed on May 11, 2026, at 21:07:57.692343. The move supports KKR's strategy to optimize its investment holdings and concentrate on core assets amid evolving market conditions. Transferring the Danish portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate sharpens KKR's focus on real estate opportunities and helps streamline its asset base. The sale reflects a disciplined asset-management approach as the firm navigates global economic uncertainties and seeks efficiency across its portfolio. KKR highlights its ESG focus, noting an MSCI ESG score aligned with industry peers and investor demand for sustainable investing options. Looking ahead, stakeholders will monitor how the transaction influences KKR's investment mix and overall portfolio performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI EXTEND COLLABORATION TO DELIVER INSTITUTIONAL-STYLE PRIVATE MARKET SOLUTIONS FOR ADVISORS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) broaden their partnership to develop private market investment offerings for wealth and retirement clients, reflecting growing demand for alternative assets across advisory channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the firms said their enhanced collaboration would widen access to institutional-style private market strategies, including model portfolios and defined contribution retirement products. The collaboration builds on a multi-year relationship focused on fund administration and technology services, combining Carlyle’s private markets platform with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing. The integration aims to give advisors and investors a more straightforward path to private market exposure, with tailored solutions designed to navigate this complex asset class. Michael Lane, SEI’s head of asset management, said, "One of the most common questions we hear from clients is how to allocate to private markets. Our objective with Carlyle is to help simplify that decision by providing more streamlined access to a broader range of strategies." Jeff Nedelman, Carlyle’s co-president and global head of client business, remarked that the partnership responds to rising demand from individual investors and retirement savers for private market investments. As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported about $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, and Carlyle reported $477 billion under management as of December 31, 2025.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) UNITES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND DIGITAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a unified platform designed to boost digital capabilities across healthcare services. The move addresses ongoing structural changes in the U.S. healthcare system, including administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The combined platform will support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers requiring tailored operational assistance, leveraging Knack's globally distributed operational framework and Equalize's tools for predictive analytics and optimization. The integration aims to advance revenue cycle management by enhancing billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, potentially improving efficiency and regulatory compliance. The initiative aligns with market trends as providers seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities, and Carlyle positions itself to participate in a sector undergoing substantial transformation.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) EXPANDS HEALTHCARE REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT CAPABILITIES WITH KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM DEAL

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, aligning with Carlyle's plan to fuse Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to bolster advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM count physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals among their clients. The collaboration targets rural cost reports and complex anesthesia cases, as noted by Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle frames the move as a platform for broader participation in the RCM market and signals intent to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementCarlyle sets its course to strengthen capabilities in healthcare management and revenue cycle technology, with the expectation of delivering innovative solutions that support providers in navigating a changing healthcare landscape

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ INTER MILAN SECURES €5 MILLION TOP-UP FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL

Oaktree Capital has injected €5 million into Inter Milan, lifting the total cash infusion into the club's equity reserves to €52 million, according to a semiannual statement released on 11th May 2026.Having taken ownership of Inter nearly a year ago after purchasing the club from Suning following a loan default, Oaktree has pursued a strategy balancing stability with future growth while keeping CEO Giuseppe Marotta in place.The Nerazzurri have retained much of last season’s Serie A title-winning core but aim to rejuvenate the squad by bringing in younger players with resale value, aligning with a longer-term horizon.Plans for a new stadium to replace San Siro are moving forward, a move expected to significantly boost revenue, supported by the latest funding as part of a broader plan to keep Inter competitive on and off the pitch.The €5 million tranche follows a similar injection in December last year, underscoring Oaktree Capital’s ongoing commitment to the club’s financial health and strategic objectives. As Inter continues to evolve under Oaktree, eyes will be on how these investments translate into on-field performance and commercial prospects.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL PROVIDES $300 MILLION FINANCING TO ESTABLISHMENT LABS

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan from Oaktree Capital Management. The facility, finalized on 3rd May 2026, refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives.The loan carries a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure with flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, enhancing funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues its operational and expansion plans.The investment thesis centers on the Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures expected to drive broad adoption.Management guidance for 2026 projects revenue of US$264-266 million, implying roughly 25-26% growth from the prior year. The financing should ease refinancing pressure and provide liquidity to address losses and cash burn without an immediate equity raise.While the capital raise improves flexibility, there are risks linked to ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts call for around 30% revenue growth with earnings near US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could reinforce or temper optimism about profitability. The agreement marks a strategic shift in its financing approach.

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) EXPLORES STRATEGIC SALE OF MIDCAP FINANCIAL INVESTMENT IN A $3 BILLION DEAL

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is exploring a strategic sale of its publicly traded private credit fund MidCap Financial Investment Corp (NYSE:MFIC) in a deal valued around $3 billion. The discussions, held on 11th May 2026, indicate a potential buyer that could be a business development company and may involve some level of stock consideration rather than a cash-only transaction.The contemplated sale reflects Apollo Global Management's efforts to recalibrate its asset mix amid ongoing headwinds in the private credit market. MidCap Financial Investment posted a default rate of 5.3% in the first quarter of 2026, up from 3.9% at the end of 2025, and reported a quarterly loss of $61 million. Apollo Global Management remains one of the largest alternative asset managers with a market capitalization of about $76.79 billion and assets totaling $938.4 billion, including $709.1 billion in fee-earning assets. The stock trades at 84.84x trailing earnings, close to a five-year high, underscoring investor expectations for continued profitability.Investors should consider the GF Score of 80, profitability rating of 8/10, and financial strength rating of 6/10, along with an Altman Z-score of 0.04. Insider activity shows five insider sales totaling $127.57 million over the past 12 months and one insider buy, which may influence risk assessments as the potential deal progresses.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) ACQUIRES MAJORITY STAKE IN PROSOL GROUP TO GROW FRANCE FRESH FOOD NETWORK

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Acquires Majority Stake In Prosol Group To Grow France Fresh Food NetworkApollo closes the purchase of a majority stake in Prosol Group through its managed funds, with Ardian selling its stake. The deal was finalized on May 7, 2026. Prosol Group operates nearly 450 stores across France under Grand Frais and fresh brands and runs a vertically integrated supply chain centered on quality and freshness. Jean-Paul Mochet will remain Chief Executive Officer, leading the company as it integrates into Apollo’s portfolio.The investment aligns with Apollo’s strategy to broaden its footprint in consumer-focused platforms. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reports approximately $1.03 trillion in assets under management, underscoring its scale to support Prosol’s expansion plans. UBS AG provided financial advisory services, with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard also advising.The deal terms were not disclosed, and potential risks include supply chain volatility and changing consumer tastes. Apollo will monitor integration milestones and ensure alignment with its broader investment objectives while pursuing growth in France’s fresh food sector.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS RECORD AUM AT MORE THAN $1 TRILLION AS IT PILOTS DAILY PRIVATE ASSET PRICING

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reported that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time as it advances a daily pricing pilot for its private assets. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive, described activity as reflecting solid demand and noted record fee-related earnings. Total AUM reached $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent from a year earlier. The company posted first-quarter inflows of $115 billion and inflows of $300 billion in the trailing twelve months. The asset management business brought in $95 billion of inflows, supported by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, along with institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. Retirement services added $20 billion in inflows. The Financial Stability Board released a report highlighting vulnerabilities in private credit on the same day, and Apollo defended its approach, citing prudent underwriting. The group also described its ongoing daily valuation pilot, which produced estimated daily values for investment-grade fixed income products last year and will provide daily pricing for corporate investment-grade assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Looking ahead, Apollo sees momentum into 2026 with targets for 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE, supported by a strong origination pipeline and disciplined capital management, as institutional demand for private credit remains elevated.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT CORP. (NYSE:ARES) KEEPS $125 BILLION WEALTH TARGET INTACT AMID PRIVATE CREDIT VOLATILITY

On 9th May 2026, Ares Management Corp. (NYSE:ARES) reaffirms its plan to raise $125 billion from wealth clients by 2028, a goal lifted by $25 billion last September. The company notes redemption activity has come mainly from smaller family offices and certain institutions outside the United States, with 95% of its BDC investor base not redeeming.The tech stock selloff has amplified retail anxiety around private credit exposure, prompting Ares Management and other alternative credit managers to restrict redemptions in non-traded BDCs. The firm emphasizes its long-term growth strategy and views the volatility as an opportunity to deepen client relationships and capital formation.To support its wealth objectives, Ares Management highlights its diversified strategies and strong client partnerships. The company continues to reassure investors about the resilience of its investments, particularly in alternative assets, and maintains an outward view that the broader alternative investment market remains robust.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) RAISES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND; FIRST CLOSE EXPECTED IN SPRING

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting ongoing investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The fund’s first close is expected to occur in spring, per remarks from Chief Executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings callArougheti described the fund as having "very strong demand" after the solid performance of prior vintages. The fundraising comes more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalBeyond Japan, Ares has advanced other capital initiatives, including Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets that include a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, said the firm is fundraising to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, largely tied to commitments to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VIIReturning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is reviewing a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account sharing a mandate similar to the SSG series, focused on non performing loans and single-asset distress in Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) REPORTS $53.0 BILLION OF U.S. DIRECT LENDING CLOSED ACROSS 348 TRANSACTIONS OVER THE 12 MONTHS THROUGH MARCH 31, 2026

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update on the 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors and highlights the breadth of the platform.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, the firm Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, supported Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, the global platform Totals more than $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BECOMES FIRST GULF CARRIER TO VENEZUELA AS IT LAUNCHES DOH-CCS AND DOH-BOG SERVICES

Qatar Airways announced new scheduled flights to Caracas, Venezuela, and Bogota, Colombia, expanding its global footprint. On 12th May 2026 the airline said the services would begin on 22 July 2026, marking a milestone in its Americas expansion. The Doha based carrier will become the first Gulf carrier to operate regular flights to Venezuela and will open a new direct link between the Middle East and Colombia. The introduction of these routes marks a bold step in extending its reach in South America. Qatar Airways has long aimed to improve Middle East Latin America connectivity, following its inaugural service to Sao Paulo in 2010 and steady expansion across the region. The services will operate twice weekly, linking Hamad International Airport (DOH) with El Dorado International Airport (BOG) in Bogota and Simon Bolivar International Airport (CCS) in Caracas. The timetable is designed to facilitate connections to major hubs in East Asia, the Gulf, and Australasia, giving passengers smoother transfers. The airline says business travelers and those with cultural ties between the Americas and Asia or the Middle East will benefit from the network, with connections to Tokyo, Hong Kong, Sydney, and Melbourne. The Caracas and Bogota addition is expected to support local economies by boosting tourism and trade, while reinforcing Qatar Airways plan to rebuild and diversify its global network and provide more direct options between key markets.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS ANNOUNCES FIRST-EVER DIRECT FLIGHTS TO VENEZUELA AND COLOMBIA, STRENGTHENING GLOBAL NETWORK

Qatar Airways has unveiled plans to launch direct flights to Caracas and Bogota, expanding its route network. The move was revealed on 12th May 2026 and the services are scheduled to begin on 22nd July 2026.The two new routes will link Doha with the South American capitals, broadening the airline's footprint in underserved markets and offering passengers shorter, more direct connections.As the first Gulf carrier to serve Venezuela and the first Middle Eastern airline to Bogota, the carrier marks a milestone for intercontinental travel. The nonstop services will reduce travel times and enable easier connections to other destinations across Asia, Africa, and Europe via Hamad International Airport.With services operating twice weekly on Wednesdays and Sundays, Qatar Airways aims to drive tourism and business activity while reinforcing its role as a global gateway.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS AMERICAS FOOTPRINT WITH VENEZUELA AND COLOMBIA ROUTES

Qatar Airways is expanding its global reach with the addition of twice weekly services to Caracas and Bogota, expanding its Americas footprint to 16 destinations. The new flights from Hamad International Airport in Doha will commence on 22nd July 2026, following an announcement on 12th May 2026. The airline says it will be the first Gulf carrier to serve Venezuela and the first to offer direct links from the Middle East to both capitals, underscoring a strategic push to connect Gulf markets with northern South America.The service will operate under flight QR783 on Wednesdays and Sundays, using a tag-stop format that allows travelers to depart Doha and reach either Bogota or Caracas directly. The return leg from Caracas to Doha will be operated as a direct service, enabling onward journeys to markets including Australia, China, Japan, and the UAE.The Caracas and Bogota routes complement Qatar's existing South American operations that began in 2010 with Sao Paulo. As the airline gradually restores its network to more than 160 destinations worldwide, the new routes reflect a deliberate move into underserved long-haul corridors and strengthen links between the Gulf and northern South America.Venezuela's improving security and accessibility make Caracas an attractive gateway, while the addition also boosts options for Colombian travelers heading to Asia and Oceania. Qatar Airways frames the expansion as part of its effort to reshape long-haul connectivity and provide more choices for passengers seeking to connect through Doha.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

Milbank LLP's London-based attorneys contributed two chapters to Project Finance 2026 (15th Edition), an International Comparative Legal Guide published by Global Legal Group.

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration special counsel Kelly Dodge Garcia will speak at the International Bar Association (IBA) webinar on algorithmic pricing and competition law on May 13.

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

Former federal prosecutor Ryan Rohlfsen brings deep experience in global investigations, compliance, and crisis management

CHICAGO, May 12, 2026 -- Latham & Watkins LLP1 is pleased to announce that Ryan Rohlfsen has joined the firm's Chicago office as a partner in the White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and member of the Litigation & Trial Department. Rohlfsen advises multinational companies, boards, and senior executives in high-stakes government-facing civil and criminal white collar matters, internal investigations, litigation, and related crisis management. He has represented clients in matters on six continents and in all major global markets.

Continue Reading

Ryan Rohlfsen, Partner, Latham & Watkins LLP

"We are thrilled to welcome Ryan to Latham," said Mary Rose Alexander, Managing Partner of the Chicago office. "Ryan is a highly regarded international white collar lawyer with extensive experience helping clients manage risk, navigate enforcement challenges, and respond to sensitive matters. He will be an important resource for clients in the United States and around the world."

Rohlfsen has particular experience advising clients on international and domestic corruption matters, securities and accounting fraud, healthcare fraud, money laundering, sanctions and export controls, data privacy, and state consumer protection matters. He routinely guides public and private companies through complex and fast moving government-facing investigations, and has secured numerous declinations. Beyond advising companies and individuals in crisis, Rohlfsen also counsels global companies on building and strengthening sophisticated compliance programs.

His experience includes serving as a federal prosecutor in the US Department of Justice's Criminal Fraud Section in Washington, D.C. and Chicago. At DOJ, Rohlfsen led complex securities fraud, healthcare fraud, and domestic corruption matters, and served in the Department's specialized unit responsible exclusively for corporate criminal enforcement of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA).

"With over two decades of experience around the world, including as a former senior US federal prosecutor and trial lawyer, Ryan brings exceptional credentials and deep investigations, compliance, and government enforcement defense expertise to Latham," said Terra Reynolds, Global Vice Chair of Latham's White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and Healthcare & Life Sciences Industry Group.

"Ryan's judgment, litigation experience, and deep understanding of complex cross-border investigations and corporate fraud make him a strong addition to the firm," said Sean Berkowitz, a member of Latham's Executive Committee. "He has the kind of practical perspective clients value when the stakes are high, and we are excited to welcome him to the team."

+ SIMPSON THACHER SECURES REVERSAL FOR NORTH AMERICAN FIRE IN DELAWARE SUPREME COURT

On February 3, 2026, Simpson Thacher secured a victory for North American Fire Ultimate Holdings LP ("North American Fire") when the Delaware Supreme Court reversed and remanded a Court of Chancery judgement that had dismissed North American Fire's complaint against a former employee for violating restrictive covenants found in an Incentive Unit Grant Agreement. In its order, the Court adopted the Firm's position that consideration is measured at the time of contract formation, not at the time of enforcement. Applying Newell Rubbermaid Inc. v. Storm, the Court held that while the value of the Incentive Units was "somewhat contingent," it was not illusory at the time of formation and therefore constituted adequate consideration to support the Agreement.

The Simpson Thacher team included Michael Garvey, Alison Sher, Ryerson Symons, Nicole Palmadesso and Sarah Heintz.

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM REPRESENTS FERVO ENERGY COMPANY IN US$1.89 BILLION UPSIZED IPO

Fervo Energy has announced the pricing of its upsized initial public offering (IPO) of 70,000,000 shares of its Class A common stock, at a public offering price of US$27.00 per share. The 70,000,000 share offering represents a 14,444,445 share upsize to the originally proposed 55,555,555 share offering. In addition, the company has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 10,500,000 shares of Class A common stock.

Latham & Watkins LLP represents Fervo Energy Company in the offering with a corporate deal team led by Houston partners Ryan Maierson, Nick Dhesi, and John Slater, with associates Rachel Collier, Nicholas Blake, and Madhavi Nambiar. Advice was also provided on employment and benefits matters by Los Angeles partner Michelle Carpenter and Chicago partner Nineveh Alkhas, with associates Aaron Tso, Jocelyn Wexler, and Maddie Bober; on environmental matters by Los Angeles/Houston partner Joshua Marnitz, with associate Brandon Kerns; on public company matters by Washington, D.C. partner William Hackett and New York counsel Irina Yevmenenko, with associates Sara Almousa and Mary Tursi; on tax matters by Houston partner Christine Mainguy, with associates Chris Garza and Allison Esquen-Roca; on litigation matters by San Diego partner Drew Gardiner; on antitrust matters by New York partner Kyra Bromley and Washington, D.C. partner Jason Cruise; on securities matters by San Diego partner Colleen Smith; and on capital markets matters by New York partners Betty Huber, Gail Neely, and Washington, D.C. partner Paul Dudek, with associate Shira Mendelsohn.

+ PROMINENT INTERNATIONAL WHITE COLLAR LAWYER JOINS LATHAM & WATKINS IN CHICAGO

Former federal prosecutor Ryan Rohlfsen brings deep experience in global investigations, compliance, and crisis management

CHICAGO, May 12, 2026 -- Latham & Watkins LLP1 is pleased to announce that Ryan Rohlfsen has joined the firm's Chicago office as a partner in the White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and member of the Litigation & Trial Department. Rohlfsen advises multinational companies, boards, and senior executives in high-stakes government-facing civil and criminal white collar matters, internal investigations, litigation, and related crisis management. He has represented clients in matters on six continents and in all major global markets.

Continue Reading

Ryan Rohlfsen, Partner, Latham & Watkins LLP

"We are thrilled to welcome Ryan to Latham," said Mary Rose Alexander, Managing Partner of the Chicago office. "Ryan is a highly regarded international white collar lawyer with extensive experience helping clients manage risk, navigate enforcement challenges, and respond to sensitive matters. He will be an important resource for clients in the United States and around the world."

Rohlfsen has particular experience advising clients on international and domestic corruption matters, securities and accounting fraud, healthcare fraud, money laundering, sanctions and export controls, data privacy, and state consumer protection matters. He routinely guides public and private companies through complex and fast moving government-facing investigations, and has secured numerous declinations. Beyond advising companies and individuals in crisis, Rohlfsen also counsels global companies on building and strengthening sophisticated compliance programs.

His experience includes serving as a federal prosecutor in the US Department of Justice's Criminal Fraud Section in Washington, D.C. and Chicago. At DOJ, Rohlfsen led complex securities fraud, healthcare fraud, and domestic corruption matters, and served in the Department's specialized unit responsible exclusively for corporate criminal enforcement of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA).

"With over two decades of experience around the world, including as a former senior US federal prosecutor and trial lawyer, Ryan brings exceptional credentials and deep investigations, compliance, and government enforcement defense expertise to Latham," said Terra Reynolds, Global Vice Chair of Latham's White Collar Defense & Investigations Practice and Healthcare & Life Sciences Industry Group.

"Ryan's judgment, litigation experience, and deep understanding of complex cross-border investigations and corporate fraud make him a strong addition to the firm," said Sean Berkowitz, a member of Latham's Executive Committee. "He has the kind of practical perspective clients value when the stakes are high, and we are excited to welcome him to the team."

"I'm delighted to join Latham and work alongside

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ LAW360 PROFILES SIMPSON THACHER AS A 2025 REAL ESTATE PRACTICE GROUP OF THE YEAR

Simpson Thacher was profiled as a Real Estate Practice Group of the Year for 2025 by Law360. The Firm was recognized for its role in several major transactions over the past year, including Blackstone's landmark $16.2 billion acquisition of Australian data center operator AirTrunk and its $4 billion acquisition of Retail Opportunity Investments Corp.

Sas Mehrara, Head of the Firm's Real Estate Practice, told Law360 that the global scope of the AirTrunk acquisition required seamless cross-border collaboration among Simpson Thacher attorneys across multiple offices and practice areas, enabling Blackstone to execute a complex transaction in a new and distinctive market. Partner Whitney Salinas added that the Firm's Real Estate Practice is adept at translating expertise across regions. She emphasized the value of "being able to use the technology and solutions that we've worked on with our clients in one geographic region and port it into another geographic region, realizing that a lot of the same markets exist in different regions."

The profile also highlighted the rapid expansion of Simpson Thacher's Real Estate Practice and noted how the recent addition of a lender-side practice provides a roadmap for future growth. "As we've grown, we've really been able to still maintain our small feel, but really grow in a way that allows for collaboration, allows the high-level respect amongst each other as we get scale," Sas said.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER ADVISES ACHIEVE TOGETHER ON £400 MILLION ALL-SENIOR DEBT PACKAGE

Simpson Thacher represented Achieve Together (backed by InfraBridge) on its new £400 million all-senior debt package (including a Social Finance Framework), raised from a syndicate of leading U.K. and international banks alongside long-term core infrastructure investors. The new financing package enables the full repayment of existing group indebtedness and enables Achieve Together to continue its next phase of strategic expansion within the specialist care sector in the U.K.

The funding under the financing closed on 4 February 2026.

The Simpson Thacher team included Paul Hibbert, Emma Serginson and Charlie Wilson (Credit); Daniel Deacon, Mark Collier and Eduardo Rodriguez (Derivatives); and Yash Rupal, Oliver Rosshandler and Daniel Griffiths (Tax).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER SECURES REVERSAL FOR NORTH AMERICAN FIRE IN DELAWARE SUPREME COURT

On February 3, 2026, Simpson Thacher secured a victory for North American Fire Ultimate Holdings LP ("North American Fire") when the Delaware Supreme Court reversed and remanded a Court of Chancery judgement that had dismissed North American Fire's complaint against a former employee for violating restrictive covenants found in an Incentive Unit Grant Agreement. In its order, the Court adopted the Firm's position that consideration is measured at the time of contract formation, not at the time of enforcement. Applying Newell Rubbermaid Inc. v. Storm, the Court held that while the value of the Incentive Units was "somewhat contingent," it was not illusory at the time of formation and therefore constituted adequate consideration to support the Agreement.

The Simpson Thacher team included Michael Garvey, Alison Sher, Ryerson Symons, Nicole Palmadesso and Sarah Heintz.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Con Edison to Invest $29 Billion in NYC Grid Enhancement Amid Electrification Trends

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-7343-RSHD-P2CB-00000-00Con Edison to Invest $29 Billion in NYC Grid Enhancement Amid Electrification Trends2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisCon Edison to Invest $29 Billion in NYC Grid Enhancement Amid Electrification Trends

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 13:01 EDT

Consolidated Edison, Inc. is set to invest over $29 billion in its electric infrastructure to enhance the grid serving New York City and its surrounding suburbs. This substantial spending plan comes as the company observes an increasing demand for electrification in its service area. The announcement was made during a first-quarter earnings presentation on the 7th of May, 2026, signaling a proactive approach to meet electrification needs that are being driven primarily by buildings and transportation rather than the data center boom seen in other regions.

The planned investment breaks down to approximately $27.2 billion for Consolidated Edison Co. of New York, commonly known as CECONY, and an additional $2.3 billion from Orange & Rockland Utilities over the next five years. This figures into a total capital spending strategy of about $38 billion that includes investments in gas and steam. Despite a slight decline in weather-adjusted electricity sales for CECONY—down 0.1% compared to the previous year—CEO Tim Cawley emphasized the company's commitment to investing in infrastructure to support future demand growth.

Cawley highlighted that the electrification of heating and transportation is accelerating, supported by state and local policies as well as customer preferences. In CECONY’s service territory, new buildings are reportedly seeking 20% to 25% more power than their predecessors. Additionally, the region witnessed an 18% increase in fast-charging capacity for electric vehicles from the previous year. These developments are indicative of a broader trend where medium- and heavy-duty transportation electrification initiatives are fostering the adoption of electric trucks and buses.

In light of expected increases in demand, CECONY has identified the need for additional reliability measures. The New York Independent System Operator has raised concerns about tightening capacity margins, especially during peak summer months. Forecasts predict that CECONY will require an additional 125 MW by 2032 and 750 MW by 2036 to adequately meet demand as old fossil fuel generators retire and new projects face uncertainties. In response, CECONY has issued a request for information to explore cost-effective options to enhance grid reliability.

Con Edison is also pushing forward with its renewable energy initiatives, proposing a large-scale renewables ownership model that would enable the utility to procure and own renewable generation in New York State. This initiative, alongside a separate procurement process for renewable projects, demonstrates Con Edison’s commitment to sustainability as it seeks to balance growth with environmental responsibility. As the company continues to adapt to the evolving energy landscape, its investments in infrastructure and renewable energy are poised to play a crucial role in ensuring a reliable and sustainable power supply for New York City.
