SECTION 1 CONSOLIDATED EDISON RECENT PRESS RELEASES

SECTION 2 CONSOLIDATED EDISON ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 CONSOLIDATED EDISON RECENT PRESS RELEASES

April 20: Consolidated Edison quarterly dividends steady

Consolidated Edison today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.75c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 88.75c. The ex-dividend date is Wednesday, May 13, 2026 and the record date is Wednesday, May 13, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 15.

February 03: Consolidated Edison quarterly dividends up 4.4% sequentially from previous quarter

Consolidated Edison today announced a quarterly dividend of 88.75c per share for Q4/2025; up 4.4% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 85.0c. The ex-dividend date is Wednesday, February 18, 2026 and the record date is Wednesday, February 18, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, March 16.

October 20, 2025: Consolidated Edison quarterly dividends steady

Consolidated Edison today announced a quarterly dividend of 85.0c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of 85.0c. The ex-dividend date is Wednesday, November 19, 2025 and the record date is Wednesday, November 19, 2025 and it is payable on Monday, December 15.

SECTION 2 CONSOLIDATED EDISON ACTIVITIES

Activities

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a leading energy utility company headquartered in New York, serving over 3.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester County. Its core business activities encompass the transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, natural gas, and steam, supporting residential, commercial, and industrial sectors with a focus on reliability and safety. The company operates through subsidiaries like Consolidated Edison Company of New York and Orange and Rockland Utilities, emphasizing infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance. Fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, as well as electric vehicle infrastructure and grid modernization initiatives, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy solutions and state-level clean energy mandates. These efforts have bolstered ConEd's revenue growth, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining operational excellence. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-RKS3-RS4G-S297-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW PACT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. Fortress will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The revelation came on 5th May 2026.The arrangement deepens a partnership that began in 2025. It underscores Upstart’s push to diversify its capital base, now drawing on more than 100 banks and credit unions that use its AI-driven models and cloud applications. Upstart’s platform supports a range of products, including personal loans, automotive loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit option.

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) AND HA SUSTAINABLE INFRASTRUCTURE CAPITAL (HASI) RAISE $508 MILLION FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1 SENIOR NOTES

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through Private Offering of Senior Unsecured Notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1) as of 11th May 2026. The proceeds will be deployed to acquire or invest in both new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States. CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, renewable natural gas, and transportation infrastructure projects.The platform began with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and in December 2025 the firms added another $500 million. The current round marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million offering completed in June 2025, and lifts the platform’s total investment capacity toward $5 billion. Cecilio Velasco, Managing Director at KKR, said the strong investor reception underscores the quality and diversity of CCH1’s asset base, and noted that with over $4 billion of investment capacity the platform is well-positioned to meet rising demand for sustainable energy infrastructure. Dan McMahon, Senior Managing Director of Syndications at HASI, welcomed the expansion and highlighted CCH1's role in accelerating sustainable infrastructure investment. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities served as Joint Lead Placement Agents for the transaction

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SELLS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Sells Danish Residential Portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, marking a notable move in the European real estate market. The sale was announced on 11th May 2026 at 21:07:57.692343 and aligns with KKR's effort to streamline its investment focus amid shifting market conditions.The Danish portfolio comprises several residential units in key urban areas, and Pears Global Real Estate aims to broaden its footprint in Northern Europe. KKR will redeploy capital toward higher-return opportunities, including private credit, as part of its portfolio optimization strategy.Pears Global Real Estate, known for strategic acquisitions in high-value markets, plans to leverage its expertise to enhance the acquired properties, supporting its European expansion during a period of rising urbanization and housing demand. Market observers will watch closely for the longer-term impact on both firms.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SELLS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) sells its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move that highlights its focus on optimizing investment holdings amid evolving market conditions. The transaction was disclosed on May 11, 2026, at 21:07:57.692343. The divestment aligns with KKR's broader strategy to sharpen its focus on core investments and capitalize on opportunities within the real estate sector by transferring the Danish assets to Pears Global Real Estate, thereby streamlining its portfolio in response to changing macro conditions. KKR reinforces its commitment to sustainability through its MSCI ESG score, which tracks environmental, social, and governance performance relative to peers, aligning with investor demand for sustainable options. Stakeholders will monitor the sale's implications for KKR's future investment strategy and overall portfolio performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI EXPAND PRIVATE MARKETS PARTNERSHIP TO BROADEN ACCESS FOR WEALTH AND RETIREMENT INVESTORS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) have expanded their partnership to develop private market investment solutions aimed at wealth and retirement investors as demand for alternative assets continues to grow across advisory and retirement channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the firms said their enhanced collaboration would focus on broadening access to institutional-style private market strategies, which include model portfolios and defined contribution retirement offerings. This builds on a multi-year relationship centered on fund administration and technology services, with Carlyle’s private markets platform integrated with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing capabilities to deliver tailored solutions for navigating private market investments. Michael Lane, head of asset management at SEI, said, "One of the most common questions we hear from clients is how to allocate to private markets. Our objective with Carlyle is to help simplify that decision by providing more streamlined access to a broader range of strategies." Jeff Nedelman, Carlyle’s co-president and global head of client business, added, "This partnership reflects the increasing role of private markets across the wealth and retirement landscape." As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported approximately $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, while Carlyle managed $477 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. The expanded collaboration is expected to enhance both firms’ capabilities in delivering sought-after private market investment solutions.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) INTEGRATES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD UNIFIED HEALTHCARE TECHNOLOGY PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating two healthcare service providers, Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The move reflects a broader shift toward automation and data-driven systems within the U.S. healthcare landscape. The combined platform is expected to support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers with tailored operational assistance.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES MAJORITY STAKE IN KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, reinforcing Carlyle's strategy to blend Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to enhance advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership targets challenging elements of the revenue cycle such as rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views the acquisition as a step to broaden its footprint in the RCM sector and to pursue additional opportunities within healthcare management

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL BOOSTS INTER MILAN WITH €5 MILLION CASH INJECTION

Oaktree Capital has injected €5 million into Inter Milan, bringing total cash infused into the club’s equity to €52 million, per a semiannual statement dated 11th May 2026.Since taking ownership nearly a year ago after acquiring Inter from Suning following a loan default, Oaktree has pursued a blend of stability and growth, keeping CEO Giuseppe Marotta in place.The injection supports plans to retain the core of last season’s Serie A title-winning squad while pursuing younger talents with resale value and advancing work on a new stadium to replace San Siro. It also follows a similar €5 million tranche in December, underscoring Oaktree’s ongoing commitment to Inter’s financial health and strategic aims.

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS SECURES $300 MILLION SENIOR LOAN FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL TO RESTRUCTURE FINANCING

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility with Oaktree Capital Management, a move that is set to reshape the company's capital structure. The financing refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives, with the deal completed on 3rd May 2026.The loan features a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure, flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, strengthening the company's funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as it pursues expansion.Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, implying growth of roughly 25-26% year over year. The loan alleviates refinancing pressure and provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without requiring an immediate equity raise, though investors should weigh ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts project about 30% annual revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a path that could support or temper profitability expectations.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) ENGAGES IN $3 BILLION SALE TALKS FOR MIDCAP FINANCIAL INVESTMENT

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is negotiating the sale of its publicly traded private credit fund MidCap Financial Investment Corp (NYSE:MFIC) in a deal valued at approximately $3 billion. The discussions, which took place on 11th May 2026, may involve a business development company as the buyer and could include shares in the transaction rather than cash.The potential sale underscores Apollo Global Management's strategic maneuvering in response to ongoing challenges in the private credit market. MidCap Financial Investment has posted a default rate of 5.3% in the first quarter of 2026, up from 3.9% at the end of 2025, and reported a quarterly loss of $61 million. As one of the largest alternative asset managers, Apollo Global Management boasts a market capitalization of about $76.79 billion and oversees $938.4 billion in assets, including $709.1 billion in fee-earning assets. The stock trades at a price-earnings ratio of 84.84x, near its five-year high. The GF Score stands at 80, with profitability rated 8 out of 10 and financial strength 6 out of 10, though the Altman Z-score remains a concern at 0.04. Insider activity has shown five insider sell transactions totaling approximately $127.57 million in the last 12 months, with only one insider buy.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) EXPANDS FRESH FOOD FOOTPRINT WITH PROSOL GROUP STAKE

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Closes Majority Stake In Prosol Group To Expand France Fresh Food FootprintThe transaction closed on May 7, 2026, and was financed through Apollo-managed funds, with Ardian as the seller. Prosol Group operates nearly 450 stores across France, primarily under the Grand Frais and fresh banners, and maintains a vertically integrated supply chain focused on quality and freshness. Jean-Paul Mochet will continue as Chief Executive Officer, guiding the company’s growth under Apollo’s ownership.Apollo, which manages about $1.03 trillion in assets, said the investment will leverage Prosol’s established supplier network and customer base to accelerate expansion in its French fresh food platform. Financial advisors UBS AG, together with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard, supported the transaction.While the investment presents growth opportunities, Apollo notes sector risks such as supply chain disruptions and shifting consumer preferences that could affect returns. The terms of the deal were not disclosed, leaving valuation details for disclosure at a later stage

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS DAILY PRICING PILOT FOR PRIVATE ASSETS

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is launching a pilot to introduce daily pricing for its private asset portfolios as it reports that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive, cited record fee-related earnings as contributing to the growth. The firm said total assets under management stood at $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent year over year, with first-quarter inflows of $115 billion and inflows of $300 billion over the past twelve months. The asset management business generated $95 billion in inflows, helped by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, along with institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. The retirement services business added $20 billion in inflows. Apollo also noted the Financial Stability Board report on vulnerabilities in private credit issued the same day and defended its approach, emphasizing prudent underwriting. The company described a pilot launched last year to produce estimated daily values for its investment-grade fixed income products, with daily pricing for corporate investment-grade fixed income assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Rowan said robust processes support transparency as public markets adjust and private markets follow. Looking ahead, Apollo targets 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE for 2026, supported by a strong origination pipeline and disciplined capital management.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT CORP. (NYSE:ARES) MAINTAINS $125 BILLION WEALTH TARGET AMID MARKET TURBULENCE

Ares Management Corp. (NYSE:ARES) On 9th May 2026 reiterated its plan to raise $125 billion from wealth clients by 2028, a target increased by $25 billion last September. The firm notes redemption activity has emerged from smaller family offices and certain institutions outside the United States, while 95% of its BDC investor base did not redeem.The recent selloff in technology stocks, particularly in software, has intensified retail concerns about exposure to private credit markets. In response, Ares Management and other alternative credit managers have taken measures to limit redemptions in non-traded BDCs. The company remains focused on long-term growth and treats current turbulence as an opportunity.Ares Management relies on diversified investment strategies and strong client relationships to pursue its wealth targets. It continues to engage with investors to reassure them about the resilience of its investments, especially in alternative assets. The broader landscape for alternative investments remains robust despite volatility in some market segments.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) POSTS $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND AMID INVESTOR DEMAND

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund in early 2026 as investor demand remains robust for Japanese logistics assets. The first close is expected to occur in spring, according to chief executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings call

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) POSTS $9.5 BILLION IN U.S. DIRECT LENDING ACROSS 70 TRANSACTIONS IN Q1 2026

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors and highlights the breadth of the firm's lending platform.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, the firm Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's plans for the North American Science Association. As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals more than $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESUMES BAGHDAD SERVICE WITH EXPANDED CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will introduce a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, complemented by twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May, thereby adding belly-hold capacity alongside dedicated freighter operations. The combined cargo capacity from Doha to Baghdad will exceed 115 tonnes from 10th May.The resumption reflects a broader network strategy that blends freighter uplift with passenger capacity to meet growing global demand. The airline's freighter network has expanded to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while its passenger network is set to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June as operations scale up. All cargo products are now bookable through standard channels, including the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, with shipment inquiries directed to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or their local representatives. Qatar Airways notes its fleet comprises 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, underpinned by a road feeder network and ongoing investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS REINSTATES ERBIL FLIGHTS AS IRAQ NETWORK REOPENS

Qatar Airways said it will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a broader reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The plan calls for restarting passenger services to three Iraqi cities - Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) - in a phased rollout designed to rebuild the regional network and reconnect key Middle Eastern destinations.In parallel, the airline will restart freighter operations to Baghdad starting 7th May, reinforcing cargo links with Iraq as part of the wider network restoration. These moves follow earlier resumptions to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as the carrier expands its global reach.By mid-June 2026, Qatar Airways expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide. Passengers traveling through Erbil International Airport (EBL) will again access the airline's global network via Hamad International Airport in Doha, described as a key connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES KEY ROUTES AS REGIONAL RECOVERY GAINS MOMENTUM

Qatar Airways is resuming its key flight routes across the Middle East, Asia, and Europe as it rebuilds operations after the West Asia crisis. As of 5th May 2026, the carrier has announced the reinstatement of services to Dubai and Sharjah, signaling progress toward normalizing regional air travel.The airline also confirms the return of daily services to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) starting 1st May 2026, with a broader plan to relaunch more than 150 destinations beginning 16th June 2026. The updated schedule runs through 15th September 2026 as part of the carrier's strategy to adapt to evolving travel demand.Qatar Airways says passengers should monitor its website or app for updates and ensure contact information is current. Confirmed travelers will receive direct notifications if any flights change, underscoring the airline's focus on customer service. The move aligns with a wider aviation sector recovery as demand climbs in the wake of the crisis.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-DVG3-S2B7-83X4-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, has announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group, under which Fortress has agreed to purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026.This forward-flow agreement marks a significant expansion of the existing partnership between Upstart and Fortress, which initially began with a transaction in 2025. The collaboration illustrates Upstart’s ongoing efforts to broaden its network of capital partners, which now includes over 100 banks and credit unions leveraging its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The platform offers a wide range of financial products, including personal loans, automotive retail and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product."We’re excited to expand our partnership with Fortress with this latest agreement. Our wide array of capital partners strengthens our resilient and stable foundation to continue driving down the cost and complexity of borrowing," said Sanjay Datta, President of Capital and Enterprise at Upstart. + QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES CARGO SERVICES TO IRAQ WITH ENHANCED CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, according to its latest published schedule, marking the return of cargo connectivity between Doha and Baghdad. This development follows the airline's phased restoration of services, with cargo sales now open for shipments on the Doha to Baghdad route.Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will introduce a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad. This will be complemented by the launch of twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route starting from 10th May, thereby adding belly-hold cargo capacity alongside dedicated freighter operations. With both freighter and passenger services in place, Qatar Airways will offer a combined cargo capacity of over 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad beginning 10th May.The new addition is expected to significantly enhance cargo flows between the two markets, leveraging a mix of scheduled freighter uplift and passenger aircraft capacity. Qatar Airways Cargo reported that its freighter network has expanded to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May. The passenger network is also set to grow further, reaching over 150 destinations from 16th June as the airline continues to scale up operations.All cargo products are now available for booking through standard sales channels, including the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams. The airline confirmed that capacity is open for customer bookings, and those with shipment-specific inquiries are encouraged to reach out to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or their local sales representatives.The resumption of services to Baghdad forms part of Qatar Airways Cargo ’s broader network strategy, which combines freighter operations with passenger aircraft capacity to support global cargo demand. The airline operates a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, supported by a road feeder network, while continuing to invest in digitalization, products, and infrastructure as part of its operational strategy.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS MARKS RETURN TO ERBIL WITH RESUMPTION OF FLIGHTS

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the return of direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a wider reinstatement of its Iraq operations, the airline announced Saturday.The Doha-based carrier stated that it will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW), and Basra (BSR) — in a phased return aimed at rebuilding its regional network and restoring connectivity across key Middle Eastern destinations.In parallel with passenger services, Qatar Airways will also resume freighter operations to Baghdad starting 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq. The airline noted that these resumptions follow recent reinstatements of flights to several regional destinations, including Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus, and Kozhikode, as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, Qatar Airways expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide. Passengers traveling through Erbil International Airport (EBL) will gain renewed access to Qatar Airways’ global network via its Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a key connector linking six continents.The carrier, which has been repeatedly recognized as the 'World’s Best Airline' by Skytrax, emphasized that its return to Iraq reflects ongoing efforts to restore stability to its route network while maintaining flexibility in response to regional conditions. The resumption of passenger flights to Erbil follows the suspension of services in late February 2026 due to the outbreak of the US–Israel–Iran war, which triggered widespread airspace closures across Iraq and the wider Middle East.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS FLIGHT NETWORK, BOOSTING AUSTRALIA'S TOURISM POTENTIAL

Australia’s tourism industry is set to receive a major boost as Qatar Airways announces significant increases in its flights between Doha and key Australian cities. As of 5th May 2026, the expanded services come at a crucial time for the Australian travel market, helping to reconnect Australia with major international tourism sources in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. With the airline resuming daily flights and adding more options for travelers, this move is expected to foster a strong recovery for Australia’s tourism sector after a period of disruption.Beginning in May 2026, Qatar Airways will reinstate daily flights from Doha to Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth. These routes, which were previously limited to around five flights a week, will now operate daily, ensuring better accessibility for international travelers looking to explore Australia’s top destinations. Additionally, the airline will launch four weekly flights to Brisbane starting 16th May, which will increase to daily flights by 16th June 2026, giving more tourists the opportunity to visit Queensland’s attractions.This flight expansion reflects Qatar Airways’ ongoing commitment to strengthening global connectivity, and the airline is capitalizing on its strategic hub in Doha. By offering these additional flights, Qatar Airways makes it easier for travelers from Europe, the Middle East, and Asia to plan direct routes to Australia, supporting the country’s tourism recovery post-pandemic. The increased connectivity will not only enhance tourism but also improve trade and business opportunities between the two nations.Australia’s travel industry stands to benefit significantly from this expansion. The extra flights are expected to provide an influx of international visitors, boosting demand for accommodations, tours, and local attractions. Major cities like Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth, which already attract millions of tourists annually, are likely to see a further rise in visitors, especially as the summer months approach. For Australia’s tourism bodies, the expanded flights mean more opportunities to market the country as an accessible, diverse, and attractive destination.However, it’s essential to acknowledge that travel advisories continue to affect certain routes. According to Australia’s Smart Traveller service, there remains an active “Do Not Travel” advisory for Qatar due to ongoing regional security concerns. This caution applies to all travelers, even those transiting through Doha’s Hamad International Airport. Despite these advisories, the increased flight frequencies demonstrate Qatar Airways’ confidence in the safety and operational efficiency of its services. Looking ahead, the partnership between Qatar Airways and Australia is expected to flourish, positioning the tourism sector for long-term growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJT-YG93-S384-J3HP-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:18ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ACE US SPACE TECH ACTIVE ETF TOPS 100 BILLION WON IN NET ASSETS AS EARLY INTEREST GROWS

As the space sector evolves, the ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capture emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth space technologies.

+ KIC ANNOUNCES 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY TO STRENGTHEN FINANCE TALENT

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, set to begin on 20th April 2026, with a mission to raise finance professionals' expertise in both public and private sectors.The program comprises eight lectures over four days, starting with a 2026 outlook on the U.S. stock market and covering alternative investments such as infrastructure and private debt, plus post-investment portfolio management from both management and operational perspectives.It will also tackle topics including the U.S. interest-rate environment and the institutionalization of stablecoins, with applications already received from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, said the organization will continue to share its overseas investment experience and global network to foster domestic growth, noting that the International Finance Academy has been expanding since its 2022 inception to strengthen local investment professionals.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION EXPANDS FOCUS ON MID-MARKET INFRA AT NEW YORK FORUM

The 28th New York Finance Forum, hosted by KIC at the Korea Society, took place on April 16, 2026, with over 40 participants focusing on North American mid-market infrastructure investments.Northleaf Capital Partners outlined that mid-market deals are typically USD 1 billion or less, with eligibility expanding to up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics; 86% of North American infra transactions occur in the mid-market, while funds remain biased toward larger vehicles.Jessica Kennedy highlighted the appeal of mid-market investments for value creation and multiple exit options, predicting solid risk-adjusted returns; Yuryoung Kim noted optimism that the forum would help domestic investors sharpen overseas investment strategies and emphasized KIC's global network to support information sharing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ARDIAN AND GOLDMAN EXPAND SECONDARY FOOTPRINT WITH CIC PURCHASE

On 15th April 2026, Goldman Sachs and Ardian acquired a $1 billion CIC portfolio at notable discounts, with Ardian obtaining a double-digit markdown and Goldman a single-digit discount.CIC is reshaping its holdings to reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. By 2024, nearly half of its assets were allocated to alternatives, and about 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally.The sale follows CIC's earlier pause on divesting stakes in Carlyle, KKR and TPG and underscores the buyers' continued expansion of the secondary market. Ardian has just raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions totaling over $80 billion. The move comes as institutions such as Harvard and various Asian managers increasingly turn to secondaries for liquidity and rebalancing in a market where concentration and illiquidity concerns persist.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES GP POSITION IN CICC–HBIS FUND, AIMING TO ACCELERATE CHINA’S ADVANCED MATERIALS, NEW ENERGY AND IT INVESTMENTS

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The announcement was made at a ceremony at HBIS Group’s R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds plans to raise RMB32 billion over a 15-year term and will deploy capital through a blend of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS’s industrial priorities, including traditional sectors and emerging fields such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.Structured around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the vehicle is designed to connect local governments with international capital and drive industrial upgrading. BlueFive will work with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to support the fund’s long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, said the team is bullish on China’s long-term growth potential and sees opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group ranks among the world’s largest steel producers and has appeared on the Fortune Global 500 list for 17 consecutive years, while CICC provides a comprehensive financial services platform in Beijing. The partnership is expected to bolster investment opportunities and regional growth.

+ INVEST ALBERTA NOTES RENEWED CHINESE INTEREST AS CANADA ATTRACTS $60 BILLION IN FDI

China and Hong Kong’s foreign direct investment into Canada surpassed $60 billion by the end of 2023, making them the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta attracted roughly half of the total, with China at $24.9 billion and Hong Kong at $32.8 billion, according to Statistics Canada, placing them behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.The Globe and Mail, in a December 30, 2025 report, highlighted sustained Alberta–China/Hong Kong engagement despite federal tensions with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse noted rising talk with federal authorities and described the period as 'harvest time' for investors who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta says the investments are centered on oil and gas, carbon capture, green-energy projects and tourism in Alberta, with China serving as a key customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline. The relationship has shifted since the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians from 2018 to 2021, with policy steps such as a 2024 visit to Beijing by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly and a potential 2026 visit linked to a broader diplomatic reset. While ties evolve, Canada remains far more exposed to the United States for FDI (about 45.7% in 2023) than to Asia-Oceania (11.8%), where Japan, Hong Kong and China play lead roles.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
